title: Inline StepRecorder/ErrorRecorder into AgentLoop
description:
## Scope

Remove `StepRecorder` and `ErrorRecorder` classes. Inline their logic as private methods on `AgentLoop`. Delete `ErrorRecordingResult` data class. AgentLoop directly builds `StepExecution`, emits events, and records steps.

This removes two indirection layers that add no value — their logic is ~30 lines each and only called from AgentLoop.

## Files to change

Delete:
- `packages/agents/src/Core/Lifecycle/StepRecorder.php` — DELETE
- `packages/agents/src/Core/Lifecycle/ErrorRecorder.php` — DELETE
- `packages/agents/src/Core/Lifecycle/ErrorRecordingResult.php` — DELETE (if exists; otherwise inline result is local vars)

Modify:
- `packages/agents/src/Core/AgentLoop.php`:
  - Remove `StepRecorder` and `ErrorRecorder` from constructor/fields
  - Add private `recordStep(AgentState): AgentState` — builds StepExecution, emits continuationEvaluated + stateUpdated, records in state
  - Add private `recordError(Throwable, AgentState): AgentState` — delegates to errorHandler, builds failure step, emits events, records in state
  - Update `handleStopException()` to use the new `recordStep()` directly
  - Update `onError()` to use the new `recordError()` directly
  - Step number derived as `$state->stepCount() + 1` (not read from transient field)
  - Step startedAt read from `$state->execution()->currentStepStartedAt()`

## Acceptance criteria

- `StepRecorder`, `ErrorRecorder`, `ErrorRecordingResult` files deleted
- AgentLoop no longer instantiates or references these classes
- Step recording logic lives as `AgentLoop::recordStep()` private method
- Error recording logic lives as `AgentLoop::recordError()` private method
- Event emission order and payloads identical to before:
  - `continuationEvaluated` emitted with stepNumber, stopSignal, stopReason
  - `stateUpdated` emitted after step recorded in state
  - `executionFailed` emitted on fatal error (error path only)
- `stepCompleted` event still emitted after recording in iterate() loop

## Validations / checks / tests

- Run full agent test suite: `vendor/bin/pest packages/agents/tests/`
- Event payload regression: capture events via wiretap in a test, verify:
  - `continuationEvaluated` contains correct `stepNumber`, `stopSignal`
  - `stateUpdated` fires after step recorded
  - `executionFailed` fires on error with correct exception
  - Step numbering is sequential (1, 2, 3...)
- Error path: verify AgentErrorHandler is still called, stop signal propagated, failure step recorded
- Stop exception path: verify `handleStopException` records step with signal from exception
- No references to `StepRecorder`, `ErrorRecorder`, `ErrorRecordingResult` remain (grep)

comments.author: ddebowczyk
comments.text: # Investigation & testing protocol\n\nObligatory: start by investigating the code causing the issue. After an initial understanding of the offending module/class, TEST FIRST: recreate the error using a deterministic mechanism (mock http client or mock inference or deterministic agent driver). Ensure the deterministic mechanism does not hide the problem; if needed, use a lower-level deterministic mechanism (mock http -> mock inference -> mock agent).

title: [laravel] Fix AgentCtrl facade so fake mode is honored by agent builder methods
description:
Problem
`AgentCtrl::fake()` does not affect `AgentCtrl::claudeCode()/codex()/openCode()` calls. The facade methods call `BaseAgentCtrl::*` statically instead of using the swapped facade root, so tests can accidentally invoke real agent executors.

Context
- packages/laravel/src/Facades/AgentCtrl.php:47-53 swaps fake instance
- packages/laravel/src/Facades/AgentCtrl.php:58-83 builder methods call `BaseAgentCtrl::...` directly
- This bypasses facade root resolution and undermines fake isolation.

Expected outcomes
- After `AgentCtrl::fake()`, all facade entrypoints use fake builders/responses.
- No accidental real CLI process execution in tests.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test proving:
  1) `AgentCtrl::fake()` + `AgentCtrl::claudeCode()->execute(...)` returns fake response
  2) fake assertions (`assertExecuted*`) observe calls from facade entrypoints
- Facade methods resolve through swapped root (or equivalent fake-aware dispatch) consistently.
close_reason: Fixed AgentCtrl facade fake mode routing: claudeCode/codex/openCode now honor swapped AgentCtrlFake root; added regression tests proving fake builder, fake execute response, and fake assertions through facade entrypoints.

title: http-client: dead code RuntimeException in GuzzlePool::handleFulfilledResponse()
description:
File: packages/http-client/src/Drivers/Guzzle/GuzzlePool.php:103-105

Problem:
  if ($this->events === null) {
      throw new \RuntimeException('Event dispatcher is required for pooled requests');
  }

The constructor always sets $this->events = $events ?? new EventDispatcher(), so $this->events is never null. This guard is unreachable dead code. Worse, the same method dispatches an event at line 100 inside 'if ($this->events !== null)' — the null check is applied inconsistently (first as a guard for dispatch, then as a supposed safety net that can never fire).

Expected outcome:
  Dead code removed. The null check on line 99 can also be removed since events is always set.

Acceptance criteria:
  - Dead RuntimeException block removed
  - Redundant null check on line 99 removed
  - $this->events used directly without null checks inside this method
notes: Removed dead/unreachable null-guard logic in GuzzlePool::handleFulfilledResponse(). GuzzlePool now maintains a guaranteed non-null EventDispatcherInterface property initialized in constructor, and fulfilled/request event dispatch paths use it directly without redundant null checks or RuntimeException dead code. Verified with pest packages/http-client/tests/Integration/GuzzlePoolTest.php --compact.
close_reason: Closed

title: Avoid duplicate start events in StructuredOutputStream
description:
Outcomes:
- StructuredOutputStream iterates the prebuilt stream and emits StructuredOutputStarted once per execution.

Context:
- Problem: streamResponses() calls getStream($this->execution) instead of using $this->stream, so StructuredOutputStarted fires multiple times for the same stream.
- Location: packages/instructor/src/StructuredOutputStream.php (streamResponses).
- Suggested fix: iterate $this->stream (created in __construct) and avoid re-creating the generator; ensure caching behavior is preserved.

Acceptance Criteria:
- StructuredOutputStarted is dispatched exactly once per StructuredOutputStream instance.
- Calling partials(), responses(), finalResponse() does not create a second stream or duplicate start events.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test with a stub attempt handler and a fake event dispatcher; call finalResponse() and assert StructuredOutputStarted dispatched once.
close_reason: Guard StructuredOutputStarted dispatch so it fires once; add unit test covering multiple reads

title: Sandbox: fix ProcRunner stdin pipe mode (EBADF)
description:
Problem: ProcRunner opens descriptor 0 with write-mode pipe definition and then writes stdin payload, producing Bad file descriptor behavior for child stdin consumption (observed with /bin/cat).\nContext: validated repro returned exit code 1 with stderr 'cat: stdin: Bad file descriptor'.

Expected outcomes:
- Child process receives stdin reliably in proc_open runner.\n- Behavior matches Symfony runner semantics for stdin payload handling.\n- No regression in stdout/stderr streaming and truncation caps.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add regression test for ProcRunner executing a command that reads stdin (e.g., cat) and echoes input; assert exit code 0 and expected stdout.
- Add test for null/empty stdin path.
- Confirm no EBADF warning occurs in test run.
- Sandbox package tests pass.
close_reason: Added ProcRunner stdin regressions (cat echo + null/empty stdin) and fixed proc_open descriptor 0 mode to read-end pipe so child stdin works without EBADF; sandbox tests pass.

title: Reflection: exclude static properties from deserialization schema

Expected outcome:

acceptance_criteria:
- Add regression test with a class containing both static and instance properties.
- Static properties are absent from generated schema properties/required arrays.
- Existing behavior for instance properties remains unchanged.
close_reason: Added regression test for static+instance properties and fixed ClassInfo reflection mapping to skip static properties, so schema properties/required include only instance-level deserializable fields.

title: Clarify maxRetries semantics in structured output
description:
Outcomes:
- maxRetries semantics are explicit and verified by tests.

Context:
- Problem: StructuredOutputExecution::maxRetriesReached uses attempts > maxRetries; it is unclear whether maxRetries means retries-after-first-attempt or total attempts.
- Location: packages/instructor/src/Data/StructuredOutputExecution.php (maxRetriesReached) and retry policy usage.
- Suggested fix: document intended meaning (e.g., retries-after-first) and add tests; consider renaming to maxAttempts if total attempts is intended.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation/tests state that total attempts = maxRetries + 1 (or renamed to maxAttempts if not).
- Retry policy behavior matches the documented meaning.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test that sets maxRetries=1 and asserts two attempts are allowed (first + one retry) before failure is final.
close_reason: Document maxRetries semantics (retries after first attempt) and add unit test for maxRetries=1

title: [stream][2.0] Validate Tee never-started branch buffering risk
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, tests, and package docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 API and runtime behavior relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
Tee can retain an unbounded buffer when a branch is created but never started, because all branches are marked active up front and deactivation only happens once a branch generator is finalized.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm whether the claim reproduces in the current implementation and whether it is reachable in realistic package usage.
- Decide whether this is a real performance / memory bug, an inherent tradeoff of the current API, or not important enough for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, implement the simplest fix that preserves clarity and avoids surprising lifecycle rules.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, record the reasoning explicitly and clarify docs if needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a clear written conclusion.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If accepted for 2.0.0, Tee no longer hides a pathological never-started-branch buffering case, or the documented contract makes the lifecycle requirement explicit with no surprise behavior.
- Regression tests are added or updated to cover the chosen behavior.
- Related tests are fixed if branch-lifecycle semantics change.
- Examples and docs are updated anywhere Tee buffering guarantees or abandonment behavior are described.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so the selected behavior remains covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage for the never-started-branch case, not just branches that are later unset after partial iteration.
- Fix or extend related Tee unit / feature tests.
- Update package docs, cheatsheet, and internals notes if buffering guarantees or usage guidance change.
- Check whether any examples should be rewritten to avoid implying stronger buffering guarantees than the code actually provides.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
close_reason: Validated with direct TeeState repro. If one branch consumes while another branch is created but never started, the never-started branch remains active and prevents buffer eviction, so buffered values accumulate until the unused branch is released. Fixing this cleanly would require changing branch activation/lifecycle semantics, which is broader than it earns for 2.0.0. No runtime code change applied. I documented the limitation in packages/stream/CHEATSHEET.md so the contract is explicit: discard unused branches promptly if another branch may run far ahead.

title: TypeDetailsFactory::fromTypeName must correctly normalize nullable/union/collection inputs
description:
Context:
`fromTypeName` passes raw strings into scalar/object/collection constructors, causing failures for valid normalized inputs like `?Foo`, `Foo|null`, `?int[]`.

Expected outcome:
`fromTypeName` consistently uses normalized parsing output and handles nullable/union/collection forms predictably.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add regression tests for `?Class`, `Class|null`, `?int[]`, and representative union cases.
- Method returns correct `TypeDetails` (or explicit documented unsupported result) without class-not-found crashes from raw composite strings.
- Parsing logic is centralized enough to avoid divergent behavior vs `fromPhpDocTypeString`.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for ?Class, Class|null, ?int[], and scalar union inputs; refactored TypeDetailsFactory to resolve from parsed TypeString in one shared path, avoiding raw composite-type crashes and aligning fromTypeName with fromPhpDocTypeString.

title: [http-client] Resolve failOnError default contract mismatch (config/docs/tests)
description:
## Problem and context
Default error-policy behavior is inconsistent across config, docs, and integration expectations.

Current runtime defaults (`packages/http-client/config/http.php`) set `failOnError: true` for built-in presets including default `curl`, so `HttpClient::default()` throws on HTTP 4xx/5xx.
However docs state default is `failOnError: false`, and existing pool integration tests expect 500 responses to be captured as `Failure` results instead of throwing.

### Repro (confirmed)
- `HttpClient::default()` + GET `/status/500` throws `ServerErrorException`.
- `vendor/bin/pest packages/http-client/tests/Integration/HttpClientPoolTest.php --filter "pool handles request failures gracefully"` fails with thrown `ServerErrorException`.
- `vendor/bin/pest packages/http-client/tests/Integration/PendingHttpPoolTest.php --filter "mixed success and failure results"` fails with thrown `ServerErrorException`.

### Code references
- `packages/http-client/config/http.php:15`
- `packages/http-client/config/http.php:25`
- `packages/http-client/config/http.php:35`
- `packages/http-client/config/http.php:45`
- `packages/http-client/docs/3-making-requests.md:64`
- `packages/http-client/tests/Integration/HttpClientPoolTest.php:97`
- `packages/http-client/tests/Integration/PendingHttpPoolTest.php:110`

## Expected outcomes
- Establish one explicit contract for default error behavior (throw vs return failure) and apply it consistently.
- Align runtime config, docs, and tests with that chosen contract.
- Keep behavior predictable for downstream packages relying on `HttpClient::default()`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Decision documented in package docs (including migration note if behavior changes).
- Runtime defaults and test expectations updated to match the chosen policy.
- Add/adjust regression tests to lock selected behavior for:
  1. single-request path (`withRequest(...)->get()` on 500),
  2. pool path (`pool()/withPool()->all()` on 500).
close_reason: Aligned default failOnError contract to false in runtime presets and docs; added regression tests for default single-request and pool behavior on 5xx and verified explicit failOnError=true paths still throw.

title: [http-client][2.0] Validate RecordingMiddleware streaming semantics vs release value
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that `RecordingMiddleware` defeats true streaming by eagerly draining streamed responses into memory before returning a response to the caller, and make an explicit judgement whether this is worth doing for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current codebase, tests, and intended usage. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. If the simplest correct change is still too invasive or low-value for 2.0.0, document that and do not force the work into the release.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: `packages/http-client/src/Extras/Middleware/RecordReplay/RecordingMiddleware.php` consumes the full upstream stream into `$chunks` before returning, which means recording mode stops being progressive and can grow memory linearly with the response size.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the claim with concrete code references and a deterministic repro.
- Decide whether the behavior is surprising enough, high-value enough, and fixable simply enough to include in 2.0.0.
- If worth doing now, implement the smallest clear fix that matches the 2.0.0 north star.
- If not worth doing now, leave the code unchanged but document the rationale and any operational limitation clearly.

## Acceptance criteria
- A deterministic test or focused repro demonstrates whether recording mode blocks progressive delivery and/or buffers the entire stream.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: streamed recording behavior is made explicit and unsurprising, with the simplest maintainable implementation.
- If go: tests are updated or added to lock the chosen behavior.
- If go: docs/examples/cheatsheet are updated anywhere recording + streaming behavior is described or implied.
- If no-go: docs/cheatsheet include a concise limitation note if needed.

## Related chores
- Review `RecordReplayStreamingTest` and related coverage for missing progressive-stream assertions.
- Fix or add regression/integration tests.
- Update relevant docs/examples/CHEATSHEET entries.
- Remove any stale wording that implies transparent streaming preservation if that is not true.
close_reason: Validated and judged no-go for a behavioral rewrite in 2.0.0 without explicit approval. RecordingMiddleware eagerly drains streamed responses before returning a replayable stream; added a focused regression test that proves the upstream stream is fully consumed during recording and documented the limitation in middleware docs/CHEATSHEET. This keeps replay deterministic and avoids a larger tee/progressive-stream redesign for 2.0.0.

title: [polyglot][2.0] Validate embeddings event payload redaction gaps
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that embeddings runtime and driver events leak sensitive configuration or request data, and explicitly judge whether fixing that behavior is worth the effort for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current codebase and emitted event payloads. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. Sensitive telemetry handling should be boring and explicit; inference already has a redaction model, so embeddings should either align or clearly document why not.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: embeddings events such as `EmbeddingsDriverBuilt` and `EmbeddingsFailed` may expose raw `apiKey`, auth headers, or query-string API keys because embeddings code does not redact payloads the way inference does.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the claim with concrete code references and deterministic event-capture tests.
- Decide whether embeddings should align with inference redaction behavior for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing now, implement the simplest consistent redaction approach.

## Acceptance criteria
- Regression tests capture emitted embeddings events and prove whether secrets are present or redacted.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: sensitive config values, request headers, query params, and request bodies are redacted consistently in embeddings telemetry.
- If go: no event payload or serialized event string contains raw secrets from the deterministic test fixture.
- If go: docs/CHEATSHEET mention the telemetry/redaction behavior only if user-visible guidance is needed.
- If no-go: document the limitation and rationale clearly.

## Related chores
- Add embeddings equivalents of existing inference redaction regression tests.
- Review runtime/driver event payload construction for embeddings.
- Update docs/CHEATSHEET if telemetry guidance exists or needs to exist.
close_reason: Validated and fixed. Embeddings runtime and driver events were emitting raw config/request data: EmbeddingsRuntime dispatched EmbeddingsDriverBuilt with config->toArray(), and BaseEmbedDriver emitted raw exception/request/headers/body on EmbeddingsFailed. I aligned embeddings telemetry with the existing inference redaction model by redacting sensitive config values, headers, request bodies, request options, query-string secrets, and exception URLs. Added regression coverage in packages/polyglot/tests/Regression/EmbeddingsSensitiveEventPayloadRedactionRegressionTest.php.

title: [stream][2.0] Validate stale TransformationStream pipeline reuse and release value
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, tests, and package docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 API and runtime behavior relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
TransformationStream can silently keep using an old execution / iterator after iteration has started, so a derived stream created with through() or using() may ignore newly added transducers and continue running the previous pipeline.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm the actual current behavior with a focused reproduction against packages/stream.
- Decide whether this is a real bug, an unsupported usage pattern, or an acceptable tradeoff for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, define and implement the simplest fix that preserves clarity and avoids hidden state reuse.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, document the reasoning clearly and close the loop with an explicit decision.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a written conclusion on whether it reproduces.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If the work is accepted for 2.0.0, derived TransformationStream instances no longer accidentally reuse stale iterator / execution state, or the API contract is intentionally narrowed and documented in a no-surprise way.
- Regression tests are added or updated to lock the chosen behavior.
- Related tests are fixed if the contract changes.
- Examples and docs are updated anywhere the supported derivation / reuse behavior is described or implied.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so the behavior stays covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage in the appropriate packages/stream regression suite.
- Fix or extend related unit tests.
- Update package docs, examples, cheatsheet, and internals notes if behavior or guarantees change.
- Remove or rewrite any examples that rely on surprising stateful behavior.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro. After iteration starts, deriving a new stream with through()/using() reused the old execution and iterator state, so newly added transducers were ignored. Fixed locally in packages/stream/src/TransformationStream.php by resetting runtime execution/iterator state when input or transformation changes, and added regression coverage in packages/stream/tests/Regression/TransformationStreamContractRegressionTest.php.

title: Sandbox: implement readablePaths support in Bubblewrap driver
description:
Problem: BubblewrapSandbox applies writablePaths but ignores readablePaths policy, causing driver inconsistency and surprising API behavior.\nContext: command construction comparison showed policy->withReadablePaths() does not affect Bubblewrap command.

Expected outcomes:

acceptance_criteria:

close_reason: Added regression coverage for Bubblewrap readablePaths mounts plus parity check across non-host drivers; implemented readablePaths handling in Bubblewrap buildCommand using --ro-bind with existing path validation semantics; sandbox tests pass.

title: Fix InferenceRequest::hasMessages empty check
description:
Outcomes:
- InferenceRequest::hasMessages accurately reflects empty Messages and cached context.

Context:
- Problem: hasMessages uses empty($this->messages) on a Messages object, which always evaluates as non-empty, so empty message sets still return true.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceRequest.php (hasMessages).
- Suggested fix: use $this->messages->isEmpty() and check cachedContext->messages()->isEmpty().

Acceptance Criteria:
- hasMessages returns false when both request and cached context have no messages.
- hasMessages returns true when either contains at least one message.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test covering empty Messages and cached context combinations for hasMessages().
close_reason: Use Messages::isEmpty for hasMessages and add Pest coverage for empty/request/cached cases

close_reason: Remove redundant null coalescing in InferenceResponse accessors

title: Schema: validate nested object-shape additionalProperties roundtrip for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests nested anonymous object shapes may lose additionalProperties:false on parse/render roundtrip, which could reopen object shapes that were previously closed.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. First validate the claim against the current codebase and existing intended semantics, then judge whether fixing it is valuable enough for 2.0.0. Avoid speculative complexity if the behavior is not materially important.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the roundtrip claim with a minimal nested-object reproduction.
- Clarify whether ArrayShapeSchema/ObjectSchema rendering is intentionally different or accidental drift.
- Make a release judgement: implement now, document as intentional, or defer based on value vs complexity for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing, deliver the smallest clear change that keeps object-shape behavior unsurprising.

Related chores:
- Add or adjust regression tests for nested anonymous object shapes and roundtrip rendering.
- Update fixtures/examples that cover parsed JSON Schema object shapes.
- Update docs only if public rendering semantics change.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and records whether the roundtrip behavior is actually incorrect.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, nested anonymous object shapes preserve the intended additionalProperties semantics across parse/render flow.
- Relevant tests/fixtures are added or updated.
- Any necessary docs/examples are updated, or the task records why no docs/examples change is needed.
notes: Confirmed with deterministic roundtrip repro. Nested anonymous object shapes parse as ArrayShapeSchema, but JsonSchemaRenderer::renderArrayShape() omits additionalProperties:false, reopening the shape on render. Applying a narrow renderer fix plus regression coverage.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed. Nested anonymous object shapes were preserved as ArrayShapeSchema on parse, but JsonSchemaRenderer::renderArrayShape() omitted additionalProperties:false on render, reopening those shapes on roundtrip. Added a narrow renderer fix and extended roundtrip regression coverage. No broader design change or docs update was needed.

Context

title: [laravel] Fix HTTP client wiring so Http::fake() actually intercepts Instructor calls
description:
Problem
Laravel HTTP fakes/middleware are bypassed because the service provider creates a brand-new HTTP Factory instance instead of using Laravel's container-bound HTTP client. This breaks the documented `Http::fake()` integration.

Context
- packages/laravel/src/InstructorServiceProvider.php:120 uses `new \Illuminate\Http\Client\Factory()`
- Laravel `Http::fake()` swaps the facade root (`Illuminate\Http\Client\Factory`) in the container/facade, not arbitrary new instances.
- README and docs promise Http::fake support.

Expected outcomes
- Instructor Laravel integration uses Laravel's shared HTTP factory from the container so `Http::fake()` and related behaviors apply.
- No behavior regression for runtime HTTP requests.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test proving `Http::fake()` intercepts Instructor calls through Laravel package wiring.
- Service provider no longer instantiates a standalone `new Factory()` for runtime client wiring.
- Existing inference/structured-output flows still function with real HTTP when not faked.
close_reason: Fixed: Laravel service provider now resolves Illuminate HTTP Factory from container; added regression test proving container-bound factory is used by laravel driver wiring.

title: Make Usage accumulation API consistent
description:
Outcomes:
- Usage API is consistent about mutability.

Context:
- Problem: Usage has both a mutable accumulate() and an immutable withAccumulated(), which makes the API inconsistent and can lead to unintended mutations.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/Usage.php (accumulate/withAccumulated).
- Suggested fix: choose one style (prefer immutable) and adjust call sites accordingly; make the other method private or remove it.

Acceptance Criteria:

- No accidental mutations of shared Usage instances.

How to Test:

close_reason: Remove mutable accumulate() and make withAccumulated purely immutable

title: ConfigResolver: cache key ignores default argument and corrupts get() semantics
description:
Problem and context:
`ConfigResolver::get()` caches through `CachedMap` keyed only by path. The first lookup for a missing key with a default value is cached and then reused for all subsequent lookups of that path, regardless of the new default or strict/no-default mode.

Repro:
- `get('missing.key', 'a')` returns `'a'`
- `get('missing.key', 'b')` incorrectly returns `'a'`
- `get('missing.key')` incorrectly returns `'a'` instead of throwing `ConfigurationException`

Expected outcomes:
`get()` semantics are call-local and deterministic: strict lookups remain strict, and per-call defaults are respected.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression tests for:
  - same missing path with two different defaults returns the per-call default each time;
  - missing path looked up with default first, then without default, throws `ConfigurationException`.
- Fix caching strategy (e.g. key includes lookup mode/default, or cache only provider resolution and compute fallback per call) so behavior matches contract.
- Keep provider precedence behavior unchanged for existing keys.
close_reason: Confirmed cache-key bug in ConfigResolver::get() where path-only caching leaked prior defaults into subsequent calls and strict mode. Added regression tests and changed caching strategy to store provider resolution per path (found/value), applying default-vs-strict behavior per call via argument presence; provider precedence for existing keys remains unchanged.

title: agents: ReadFileTool static withPolicy -> fromPolicy (2.0 clean cut)
description:
Goal: remove static with* constructor naming ambiguity in ReadFileTool for 2.0.0.

Changes:
- Rename ReadFileTool::withPolicy(ExecutionPolicy) to ReadFileTool::fromPolicy(ExecutionPolicy).
- Keep/retain ReadFileTool::inDirectory(...) as-is.
- Update all internal call sites, examples, docs, and tests to new API.
- Do NOT add deprecations or aliases. Hard BC break is intentional for 2.0.0.

Acceptance:

- Static analysis/tests for touched packages pass.
- Method-conventions semgrep has no new regressions.
close_reason: Renamed ReadFileTool::withPolicy to fromPolicy, verified no remaining usages, and ran packages/agents tests.

title: [laravel] Make instructor:test return failure exit code when checks fail
description:
Problem
`instructor:test` command always exits with success (`0`) even when connection checks fail. This makes CI and operational automation treat broken configuration as healthy.

Context
- packages/laravel/src/Console/InstructorTestCommand.php:61-66 chooses test path
- Both `testInference()` and `testStructuredOutput()` return `self::SUCCESS` unconditionally (lines 129 and 186), regardless of task result.

Expected outcomes
- Command exits non-zero on failed test checks/exceptions.
- Success/failure status is machine-reliable for automation.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests for command exit codes:
  1) passing scenario returns success code
  2) failing scenario returns failure code
- Keep existing human-readable command output.
close_reason: Fixed instructor:test exit codes: command now returns FAILURE when task callback reports false and SUCCESS on pass; added regression tests covering success/failure for inference and structured-output paths.

title: Make Polyglot request value objects typed-only and move compatibility to factories
description:
Context
The fluent request APIs above Polyglot are now largely typed, but the core request value objects still accept compatibility-first unions in public constructors and mutators. In particular, InferenceRequest and CachedInferenceContext still normalize strings and arrays late, which weakens the core contract and keeps hydration concerns mixed into normal construction.

Expected outcome
Polyglot request value objects should have one strict internal shape: Messages, ToolDefinitions, ToolChoice, and ResponseFormat. Compatibility input should be isolated to explicit hydration/factory entry points such as fromArray() or dedicated fromAny()/fromInput() helpers where they are intentionally kept.

Scope
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceRequest.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/CachedInferenceContext.php
- adjacent tests and callers affected by constructor/mutator typing
acceptance_criteria:
- InferenceRequest public constructors/mutators no longer expose string|array compatibility unions for messages, tools, toolChoice, or responseFormat
- CachedInferenceContext public constructors/mutators no longer expose compatibility unions for those same fields
- Array/string compatibility, if retained, is available only through explicit hydration/factory methods
- Existing serialization round-trip coverage still passes
- New regression tests lock the typed-only constructor/mutator contract
close_reason: Completed by the typed-only request value object refactor: constructors/mutators are strict and compatibility is isolated to hydration factories.

title: Schema: validate nullable constructor requiredness inference for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests constructor-backed nullable properties may be treated as optional even when the constructor parameter has no default, which would under-specify required input in generated schemas.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. Do not assume this finding should be fixed just because it was reported. First validate the claim against the current codebase and actual package behavior, then judge whether the work is valuable enough to include in 2.0.0.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the review claim with a minimal reproducer in the current codebase.
- If confirmed, document the exact semantic mismatch between constructor requirements and generated schema required fields.
- Make a release judgement: either justify doing the work in 2.0.0 or explicitly defer/reject it because it adds complexity or low value.
- If the work is worth doing, implement the smallest clear fix that preserves package simplicity and avoids surprising behavior.

Related chores:
- Add or adjust regression tests for nullable constructor parameters with and without defaults.
- Update package examples if constructor-required nullable fields are user-visible behavior.
- Update package docs/README/CHEATSHEET only if public semantics change.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and records whether it is true, false, or partially true.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, generated schema required fields match constructor invocation requirements for nullable parameters without defaults.
- Relevant tests are added or updated.
- Any necessary docs/examples are updated, or the task records why no docs/examples change is needed.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed for 2.0.0. Constructor-backed nullable properties without defaults were incorrectly treated as optional because PropertyInfo::isRequired() returned false when the constructor parameter allowed null. Simplified requiredness to follow constructor invocation semantics: constructor parameters without defaults are required regardless of nullability. Added regression coverage for PropertyInfo and generated schema required fields; focused schema tests passed.

title: ResponseIterators: dedup hash can collapse distinct objects (silent missed partial emissions)
description:
## Problem and context
`DeserializeAndDeduplicateReducer::hashObject()` hashes `Json::encode($object)`. For many DTO/value-object styles (e.g. private/promoted properties, custom serialization), `json_encode` can collapse different object states to the same payload such as `{}`. This causes dedup to treat distinct objects as unchanged.

Location:
- `packages/instructor/src/ResponseIterators/ModularPipeline/Pipeline/DeserializeAndDeduplicateReducer.php`
- `packages/utils/src/Json/Json.php`

Impact:
- `PartialResponseGenerated` may be skipped for real updates.
- Streaming UIs and downstream observers can miss state transitions.

## Expected outcomes
- Dedup key generation is robust for object variants used by Instructor.
- Distinct semantic object states are not suppressed as duplicates.
- Equal semantic states still deduplicate.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test that fails on current implementation with two distinct objects collapsing to same hash / no second emission.
- Replace hash strategy with deterministic, semantically stable representation for transformed value types used in pipeline.
- Ensure no behavioral regression in existing modular pipeline tests.
- Confirm new regression test passes after fix.

## Process spec (bug)
1. Add regression test that confirms current faulty behavior.
2. Run tests to verify failure before code changes.
3. Implement fix in reducer/hash path.
4. Re-run regression and related suite to confirm fix.
acceptance_criteria: Regression test first: reproduce collision-driven suppression of partial emissions for distinct objects; fix dedup hash strategy; confirm emissions are produced only for truly equal semantic objects.
close_reason: Added fail-first regression proving collision-driven suppression for distinct private-state objects, replaced JSON-encode hash with deterministic semantic snapshot hashing (scalars/arrays/enums/datetime/json-serializable/objects with reflected properties), and validated modular pipeline suites.

title: [logging][2.0] Validate Symfony request-id fallback stability and release value
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, tests, and docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 package contract relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
SymfonyLoggingFactory generates a fresh fallback request_id for each logged event when X-Request-ID is absent, so multiple events from the same request cannot be correlated reliably.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm the current fallback request_id behavior for multiple events emitted during one request.
- Decide whether this is a real observability bug, an acceptable fallback tradeoff, or not important enough for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, implement the simplest fix that preserves clarity and stable per-request correlation.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, document the reasoning explicitly and clarify the contract if needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a clear written conclusion.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If accepted for 2.0.0, fallback request correlation is stable within a request, or the documented contract clearly states the behavior with no hidden magic.
- Regression tests are added or updated to cover repeated events within one request context.
- Related tests are fixed if enrichment semantics change.
- Symfony integration docs, examples, and cheatsheet references are updated anywhere request correlation behavior is described or implied.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so the chosen behavior remains covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage for repeated pipeline invocations against one RequestStack request without X-Request-ID.
- Fix or extend SymfonyLoggingFactory tests.
- Update docs/examples to reflect the actual request correlation behavior.
- Check whether parallel Laravel behavior should be aligned or intentionally different.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro. SymfonyLoggingFactory generated a fresh fallback request_id for each event when X-Request-ID was absent. Fixed locally by caching one fallback id per current Request object and regenerating only when the request instance changes. Added regression coverage in packages/logging/tests/Regression/SymfonyLoggingFactoryRegressionTest.php.

title: [logging][2.0] Validate LoggingPipeline build immutability and release value
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, tests, and package docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 package contract relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
LoggingPipeline::build() returns a callable that still follows later mutations to the builder, so an already-built pipeline can silently change when more filters, enrichers, formatters, or writers are added.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm the current runtime behavior of built pipelines after subsequent builder mutations.
- Decide whether this is a real bug, an intentional mutable-builder contract, or an acceptable tradeoff.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, choose the simplest no-surprise contract and implement it consistently.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, document the builder semantics clearly and close the loop with an explicit decision.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a clear written conclusion.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If accepted for 2.0.0, a built pipeline no longer changes unexpectedly after build(), or the mutable contract is narrowed and documented in a way that is explicit and unsurprising.
- Regression tests are added or updated to lock the chosen behavior.
- Related tests are fixed if builder semantics change.
- Examples, README, and cheatsheet are updated anywhere the build contract is described or implied.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so the selected contract remains covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage for building a pipeline and then mutating the builder afterward.
- Fix or extend LoggingPipeline unit tests.
- Update docs/examples to make builder lifecycle and supported usage explicit.
- Check adjacent factories and integrations for assumptions about builder mutability.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro. LoggingPipeline::build() returned a callable that still followed later builder mutations because the closure read the builder arrays directly. Fixed build() to snapshot filters, enrichers, formatters, and writers at build time. Added regression coverage in packages/logging/tests/Regression/LoggingPipelineContractRegressionTest.php and documented the snapshot contract in packages/logging/CHEATSHEET.md.

title: Refactor OpenResponsesMessageFormat to use typed Message objects
description:
Refactor OpenResponsesMessageFormat to iterate Message objects instead of converting to arrays. Has unique multi-item return pattern for tool calls.

## Current state
- map() calls $messages->toArray() then loops over raw arrays
- map() has special handling: mapMessage may return multiple items (checked via isset($nativeMessage[0]))
- mapMessage(array $message) branches on string role + nested _metadata
- toMessageItem reads $message['role'], $message['content']
- toContentItems(string|array $content, string $role) branches is_string vs array, maps content types
- toFunctionCallItems reads $message['_metadata']['tool_calls'], $message['content']
- toFunctionCallOutputItem reads $message['_metadata']['tool_call_id'], handles content as string|array via Json::encode

## Expected outcomes
- map() iterates $messages->messageList()->all() yielding Message objects
- mapMessage(Message $message) uses $message->isAssistant(), $message->isTool()
- toMessageItem(Message $message) uses $message->role()->value, $message->content()
- toContentItems(Content $content, string $role) uses $content->isComposite(), iterates partsList()->all()
- ContentPart type mapping uses $part->type(), $part->toString(), $part->get('image_url')
- toFunctionCallItems uses $message->metadata()->get('tool_calls', []), $message->content()
- toFunctionCallOutputItem uses $message->metadata()->get('tool_call_id', ''), $message->content()->toString()
- No array $message or string|array $content parameters remain (except final output arrays)

## Acceptance criteria
1. All method signatures use Message, Content, ContentPart instead of array for input
2. No call to $messages->toArray() in the class
3. Multi-item return pattern in map() preserved (functional behavior unchanged)
4. Content dispatch uses Content::isComposite() instead of is_string()
5. ContentPart iteration uses partsList()->all() with typed accessors
6. Tool output content uses $message->content()->toString() — no Json::encode fallback for array content needed
7. Existing tests in OpenResponsesMessageFormatTest pass without modification
8. No changes to CanMapMessages interface or OpenResponsesBodyFormat
close_reason: Implemented: OpenResponsesMessageFormat now maps typed Message/Content/ContentPart objects with flatMap item expansion preserved.

title: Fix PHPStan return type specification errors
description: Fix ~10 PHPStan errors for methods with unspecified callable return types

title: [pipeline] Make PipelineBuilder::create() produce immutable snapshots
description:
Problem
PipelineBuilder::create() does not snapshot builder state. Pipelines created earlier can be mutated by later builder modifications because OperatorStack instances are shared by reference.

Context
- packages/pipeline/src/PipelineBuilder.php:228-235 passes $this->steps/$middleware/$hooks/$finalizers directly.
- packages/pipeline/src/Pipeline.php stores those references directly in constructor.
- OperatorStack is mutable (add/prepend/clear), so builder changes after first create() leak into previously created pipelines.
- Repro:
  1) $b = Pipeline::builder()->through(fn($x)=>$x+1)
  2) $p1 = $b->create()
  3) $b->through(fn($x)=>$x*10)
  4) $p1 on input 1 returns 20 (expected 2)

Expected outcomes
- create() produces an immutable snapshot of the builder configuration at creation time.
- Subsequent builder mutations do not affect already created Pipeline instances.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests that:
  1) verify p1 behavior is unchanged after builder adds new steps and creates p2
  2) cover middleware/hooks/finalizers snapshot behavior too (not just steps)
- Existing pipeline tests remain green.
close_reason: Fixed: added builder snapshot regressions and implemented immutable stack snapshots via OperatorStack::snapshot()/with() in PipelineBuilder::create(), preventing post-create builder mutations from leaking into existing pipelines.

title: Add contract tests for typed request and message boundaries
description:
Context:
The refactor changed the intended contract of the SDK in a meaningful way: messages are first-class domain objects, request parts are typed, empty messages are preserved when serialized, and package boundaries now pass typed request parts into Polyglot.

Most of this is currently covered by focused unit tests, but the design itself is not yet protected by a small set of cross-package contract tests. That leaves room for silent regression back to array-heavy behavior during future refactors.

Expected outcomes:
- The new typed boundary rules are captured by high-signal tests rather than tribal knowledge.
- Regressions around Messages serialization, typed request assembly, and package-to-package handoff fail fast.
- The tests describe the architectural contract, not just isolated implementation details.

Scope:
- packages/messages/tests
- packages/polyglot/tests
- packages/instructor/tests
- packages/agents/tests

What good looks like:
- A contributor can read the tests and immediately understand the intended request/message boundary.
- Future changes that reintroduce raw-array request flow break in a focused, obvious way.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Add focused contract-style tests that assert Messages remains the domain type through request assembly and package handoff into InferenceRequest.
2. Add tests that lock down the current Messages::toArray() semantics, including preservation of empty messages.
3. Add tests that assert instructor and agents pass typed request parts (Messages, ToolDefinitions, ToolChoice, ResponseFormat) into polyglot rather than relying on downstream normalization.
4. Add tests or coverage points that protect provider message mapping semantics where the typed Message API is now the source of truth.
5. The resulting test set is small and high-signal: it should validate the architecture without duplicating broad implementation coverage already present elsewhere.
6. Relevant package test suites pass after the additions.
close_reason: Implemented: typed boundary contract tests exist across messages/polyglot/instructor/agents/laravel, and the reflection helper collision was fixed so they run together.

title: Fix PHPStan null safety issues in packages/auxiliary/src/Beads/
description:
Multiple null safety and type checking issues in auxiliary package Beads code:

Files affected:
- UpdateAction.php:102 - Expression on left side of ?? is not nullable
- TbdClock.php:24 - Strict comparison using === between DateTimeImmutable and false 
- DependencyService.php:66 - Only booleans allowed in &&, bool|null given

These are strict PHPStan level 8 violations that need proper null handling and type guards.

After an initial understanding of the offending module/class, TEST FIRST: recreate the error using a deterministic mechanism (mock http client or mock inference or deterministic agent driver). Ensure the deterministic mechanism does not hide the problem; if needed, use a lower-level deterministic mechanism (mock http -> mock inference -> mock agent).

title: Schema tests: harden assertions and add integration coverage for reference/cycle edge-cases
description:
Context:
Multiple high-impact defects slipped through despite a green suite. Existing tests over-focus on happy paths and include permissive assertions in compatibility-adapter tests, with limited end-to-end coverage of renderer + refs behavior.

Expected outcome:
Test suite becomes capable of reliably catching schema correctness regressions before release.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add integration tests for `StructuredOutputSchemaRenderer`/tool-call schema generation with `useObjectReferences=true` covering nested refs and same-basename namespace collisions.
- Add self-referential model test proving generation terminates in default mode.
- Tighten permissive adapter assertions to explicit expected behaviors (or clearly partitioned, version-specific expectations) so real regressions cannot pass silently.
- CI docs/gates indicate these tests are required for 2.0.0 release readiness.
close_reason: Hardened schema regression coverage by adding StructuredOutputSchemaRenderer integration tests for same-basename refs collisions and default-mode self-reference termination, tightened PropertyInfo adapter assertions to explicit outputs, and documented required 2.0 schema regression gates.

title: Guard ResponseModel::toArray for non-object instances
description:
Outcomes:
- ResponseModel::toArray safely handles non-object instances.

Context:
- Problem: toArray calls get_object_vars($this->instance) without checking if instance is an object; mixed instances can cause a TypeError.
- Location: packages/instructor/src/Data/ResponseModel.php (toArray).
- Suggested fix: guard with is_object($this->instance) and return an empty array or serialize safely when instance is not an object.

Acceptance Criteria:
- toArray does not throw when instance is not an object.
- Existing object instances serialize as before.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test that constructs a ResponseModel with a non-object instance and asserts toArray() does not throw.
close_reason: Guard ResponseModel::toArray for non-object instances and add unit test

title: ConfigResolver API: withSuppressedProviderErrors() mutates shared instance state
description:
Problem and context:
`ConfigResolver::then()` is immutable (returns a new instance), but `withSuppressedProviderErrors()` mutates and returns `$this`. This mixed fluent semantics causes hidden cross-consumer side effects when a resolver instance is reused/shared.

Repro:
- `$a = ConfigResolver::using($provider); $b = $a;`
- `$a->withSuppressedProviderErrors(false);`
- `$b` now also throws provider errors, even though caller only changed `$a`.

Expected outcomes:
Configuration of error suppression has explicit, predictable semantics consistent with the resolver's fluent API style.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide one contract and apply consistently:
  - immutable fluent (`with...` returns new instance), or
  - explicit mutable setter naming (`set...`) with docs.
- Add regression tests that pin the chosen behavior.
- Update docs/examples so consumers cannot misread API behavior.
close_reason: Made withSuppressedProviderErrors immutable (returns new resolver), added regression coverage for shared-instance side effects, and documented immutable behavior in config cheatsheet.

Acceptance criteria: (1) keep a short Quick Start path focused on UseSubagents and AgentDefinition; (2) move fan-out/specialized/hierarchical narratives to an advanced section or separate page; (3) ensure every example matches current API (AgentBuilder capabilities, tool names, driver usage); (4) keep code snippets short and task-oriented; (5) add a clear troubleshooting section for depth limits and missing subagent definitions.

title: [stream][2.0] Validate TransformationStream output buffering semantics and release value
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, tests, and package docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 API and runtime behavior relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
TransformationStream stores all emitted output in memory via materializedOutput, so it is not actually memory-bounded streaming. This may contradict package docs that describe it as lazy and memory-efficient for large datasets.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm the actual current runtime and memory behavior of TransformationStream.
- Compare that behavior to the documented contract and examples.
- Decide whether this is a real bug, an intentional tradeoff, or just a docs mismatch.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, choose the simplest path: change implementation, narrow the contract, or correct the documentation.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, record the reasoning explicitly.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a clear written conclusion.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If accepted for 2.0.0, the resulting behavior and docs agree: either TransformationStream no longer retains full output unexpectedly, or the API/docs clearly state the materialization contract with no hidden magic.
- Regression tests are added or updated to lock the chosen behavior.
- Related tests are fixed if semantics change.
- Examples, README, CHEATSHEET, and INTERNALS are updated anywhere memory or laziness claims are made.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so the selected contract remains covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage around partial iteration, getCompleted(), and repeated iteration semantics.
- Fix or extend related unit tests.
- Update docs and examples that currently describe large-dataset or memory-constrained use cases.
- Remove contradictory claims if implementation is intentionally buffered.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
notes: Validated against code and docs. TransformationStream is lazy in evaluation start, but it retains all emitted items in materializedOutput once consumed, so it is not memory-bounded by queue size alone. This looks like a docs/contract mismatch rather than a 2.0-worthy runtime redesign. Narrowing docs and adding explicit contract coverage.
close_reason: Validated against code, tests, and docs. Claim confirmed in part: TransformationStream starts lazily, but it retains all emitted items in materializedOutput so completed output is replayable and not memory-bounded by queue size alone. This is not worth a 2.0 runtime redesign under the simplicity/no-magic bar. Resolved by tightening the documented contract and adding explicit regression coverage for replay from the materialized snapshot after completion.

title: Json::decode collapses valid falsy JSON values to default
description:
Problem and context:
`Json::decode()` uses `empty($decoded)` to decide fallback behavior. This treats valid decoded JSON values (`0`, `false`, `[]`, `""`) as decode failure and returns the provided default.

Code:
- `packages/utils/src/Json/Json.php:65`
- `packages/utils/src/Json/Json.php:77`

Repro:
- `Json::decode('0', 'DEF')` returns `'DEF'`
- `Json::decode('false', 'DEF')` returns `'DEF'`
- `Json::decode('[]', 'DEF')` returns `'DEF'`
- `Json::decode('""', 'DEF')` returns `'DEF'`

Expected outcomes:
Fallback should be used only for empty input or parse failure, not for valid falsy JSON payloads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression tests for valid falsy JSON values (`0`, `false`, `[]`, `""`, `null`) with and without defaults.
- Replace `empty($decoded)` check with parse-success-based logic.
- Maintain current behavior for invalid JSON: throw when default is null, else return default.
close_reason: Fixed: added regression tests for falsy JSON values and changed Json::decode to use parse-success semantics instead of empty() fallback.

title: Fix HttpClientConfig::fromDsn typed value coercion
description:
## Problem and context
`HttpClientBuilder::withDsn()` delegates to `HttpClientConfig::fromDsn()`, but DSN parsing currently returns string values. `HttpClientConfig` constructor requires typed ints/bools, so DSNs with keys like `connectTimeout=1` or `failOnError=true` can throw `TypeError`. Docs had to be reduced to string-only DSN examples.

## Expected outcomes
Make DSN configuration robust for typed HTTP config fields and safe for 2.0 release documentation.

## Scope notes
Keep behavior explicit and backward compatible where possible.
acceptance_criteria:
- `HttpClientConfig::fromDsn()` accepts DSN values for all constructor fields and coerces them to expected scalar types (`int`, `bool`, `string`) before constructing config.
- Invalid typed values produce clear configuration exceptions (not raw `TypeError`).
- Add regression tests covering valid numeric/bool DSN values and invalid inputs.
- Re-enable full typed DSN example in docs after fix.
close_reason: Added strict typed DSN coercion in HttpClientConfig::fromDsn() for int/bool/string fields with explicit ConfigurationException on invalid values; added regression tests for valid and invalid DSN values; restored typed DSN docs and cheatsheet examples; pest/phpstan/docs-qa passed.

title: [polyglot] Fix inference hydration fatal errors for in-progress attempts
description:
Problem and context:
Inference serialization/hydration currently crashes or mutates state when an attempt has no finalized response yet.

Evidence:
- InferenceAttempt::fromArray() always calls InferenceResponse::fromArray($data['response'] ?? []).
- InferenceResponse::fromArray() always calls HttpResponse::fromArray($data['responseData'] ?? []).
- HttpResponse::fromArray([]) fatals because required keys are missing.
- InferenceExecution::fromArray() always creates currentAttempt from empty array when key is absent.

Repro:
$attempt = InferenceAttempt::started();
InferenceAttempt::fromArray($attempt->toArray());
Observed: fatal TypeError from HttpResponse::__construct() via HttpResponse::fromArray([]).

Code references:
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceAttempt.php:186-189
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceResponse.php:233-246
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceExecution.php:311-320

Expected outcomes:
- Hydration handles missing/null response, responseData, and currentAttempt safely.
- fromArray() preserves null semantics (no synthetic empty response/attempt objects).
- Round-trip serialization works for in-progress attempts/executions without fatal errors.
acceptance_criteria: 1) InferenceAttempt started-state payload round-trips via fromArray without exception. 2) InferenceExecution with currentAttempt=null round-trips without creating synthetic attempts. 3) Regression tests cover null/missing response, responseData, and currentAttempt payloads. 4) Existing polyglot inference tests remain green.
close_reason: Fixed in-progress inference hydration null semantics: fromArray now preserves null for missing response/currentAttempt/responseData instead of constructing synthetic empty objects that crash HttpResponse hydration. Added regression tests for started attempt roundtrip, null currentAttempt roundtrip, and missing responseData payload.

title: StepResult not recorded on failure yields stale continuation outcome
description: When a step throws after at least one successful StepResult, onFailure records a failure step without a StepResult. canContinue then reads lastStepResult from a prior step, so hasNextStep may remain true and the loop can continue after failure; Agent onNoNextStep can also overwrite Failed with Completed. Fix by recording a StepResult for failure steps (evaluating criteria on failed state) or by making canContinue fall back when stepResults count < stepCount/currentStep mismatch. Affected: packages/addons/src/Agent/Agent.php:82-159, packages/addons/src/Agent/Core/Data/AgentState.php:159-182, packages/addons/src/ToolUse/ToolUse.php:73-142, packages/addons/src/Chat/Chat.php:74-145, packages/addons/src/Collaboration/Collaboration.php:74-145.
close_reason: Recorded StepResult for failure steps and added regression test; hasNextStep now stops on failure.
