title: Doctools Quality Refactor: package-owned YAML rules + ast-grep engine
description:
Goal:
Refactor Doctools docs quality checks into a scalable, package-owned system for 2.0.0.

Problem / Context:
Current docs QA rules are code-hardcoded and regex-centric, which blocks fast package-level iteration and creates maintenance bottlenecks. We need package-specific rules editable without PHP changes and stronger snippet-level semantic checks.

Expected outcomes:
- Rules can be defined per package in YAML and maintained by docs owners.
- Core Doctools quality runner remains shared and reusable.
- ast-grep becomes first-class for fenced snippet structural checks.
- Existing UX remains stable during migration (compatible command flow and output).
- Hardcoded package-specific anti-pattern rules are removed from services.
acceptance_criteria:
- YAML rule schema exists and is validated at load time.
- Rule discovery supports profile baseline + package-local rules + explicit CLI rule file(s).
- Quality runner executes regex and ast-grep rules and reports markdown file + line.
- Existing broken-link and snippet-lint checks continue to work.
- At least instructor, polyglot, and http-client docs have package-owned rules adopted.
- No package-specific hardcoded anti-pattern literals remain in quality service code.
close_reason: All epic child tasks completed and validated with doctools test suite.

title: Quality rules domain model + YAML schema loader
description:
Goal:
Introduce a typed, extensible quality rule model and YAML-backed loading/validation.

Problem / Context:
Rules are currently represented in code and tightly coupled to implementation details.

Expected outcomes:
- Add generic rule model supporting regex and ast-grep engines.
- Add YAML schema/version handling and strict validation errors.
- Replace direct hardcoded rule arrays with loaded rule sets.
acceptance_criteria:
- Introduce typed rule DTOs/entities for shared fields and engine-specific fields.
- Implement YAML loader with clear validation errors (file path + rule id + reason).
- Invalid configs fail fast with actionable error messages.
- Unit tests cover valid/invalid YAML parsing and model mapping.
close_reason: Implemented typed quality rule model and YAML rules loader with validation; added loader unit tests.

title: Rule discovery + precedence (profile, package-local, CLI)
description:
Goal:
Implement deterministic rule discovery so packages can own local quality policy while inheriting baseline rules.

Problem / Context:
No standardized mechanism exists to combine baseline and package-specific rules.

Expected outcomes:
- Discover rules from baseline profile, package docs-local files, and explicit CLI files.
- Deterministic merge order and override semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Discovery supports profile baseline + package-local rules + explicit --rules input.
- Merge behavior is deterministic and documented.
- Duplicate rule ids resolve predictably (documented policy).
- Integration tests verify precedence and effective rule set composition.
close_reason: Implemented rules discovery and precedence (profile, local .qa, explicit --rules); added discovery/provider precedence tests.

title: Ast-grep engine for fenced snippets with markdown line remapping
description:
Goal:
Add structural snippet checks using ast-grep as first-class quality engine.

Problem / Context:
Regex cannot robustly detect API misuse in code snippets; semantic checks are required for 2.0 docs quality.

Expected outcomes:
- Execute ast-grep rules against extracted snippet code.
- Report violations mapped back to markdown source path + line.
acceptance_criteria:
- Implement ast-grep runner via Symfony Process with JSON parsing.
- Snippet temp-file mapping back to markdown line numbers is accurate and tested.
- Missing ast-grep binary behavior is explicit and configurable (default strict behavior).
- Regression/integration tests cover positive matches, no-match cases, and missing binary handling.
close_reason: Implemented ast-grep snippet engine with markdown line mapping, strict/non-strict behavior, and regression tests.

title: Migrate baseline profile rules from code to YAML; remove hardcoded anti-patterns
description:
Goal:
Eliminate hardcoded package-specific anti-pattern rules from service code.

Problem / Context:
Current hardcoded provider slows rule updates and requires code deploys for docs policy changes.

Expected outcomes:
- Existing baseline profile behavior is represented in YAML presets.
- Runtime uses loaded presets, not hardcoded literals.
acceptance_criteria:
- Instructor baseline profile is fully represented in YAML and functionally equivalent.
- Existing docs quality command behavior remains compatible during migration.
- Service layer no longer contains package-specific anti-pattern regex literals.
- Regression tests assert compatibility with pre-migration expected findings.
close_reason: Migrated baseline instructor/none profiles to YAML and removed hardcoded ProfileRuleProvider/AntiPatternRule.

title: Adopt package-local quality rules for instructor, polyglot, http-client
description:
Goal:
Move quality policy ownership to package docs teams for highest-impact packages.

Problem / Context:
Without local rule files, policy remains centralized and doesn't scale across packages.

Expected outcomes:
- Add package-local rules files and wire them into quality runs.
- Verify package-specific high-signal checks run from local config.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add package-local rules files for instructor, polyglot, and http-client docs.
- Each package defines at least one package-specific rule beyond baseline.
- Quality runs load local rules without code modifications.
- Feature/integration tests validate local rule discovery and enforcement.
close_reason: Added package-local rules files for instructor, polyglot, and http-client; added feature coverage for local rules loading.

title: CLI + output contract for Quality command (rules, ast-grep-bin, format, strict)
description:
Goal:
Evolve docs quality CLI for configurable rule sources and CI-friendly output while preserving compatibility.

Problem / Context:
Current options do not fully support rule-file workflows or structured CI consumption.

Expected outcomes:
- Add CLI options for explicit rules path(s), ast-grep binary override, strict mode, and json output.
- Preserve compatibility with existing usage patterns.
acceptance_criteria:
- CLI supports --rules, --ast-grep-bin, --format=text|json, and strict-mode behavior.
- Existing command invocation path remains backward compatible.
- JSON output is stable and documented for CI ingestion.
- Feature tests cover new flags and backward-compatible invocation.
close_reason: Extended Quality CLI with --rules, --ast-grep-bin, --format, and --strict/--no-strict; added json output coverage.

title: Documentation + rollout playbook for package-owned quality rules
description:
Goal:
Provide clear maintainer guidance for defining and evolving package quality rules.

Problem / Context:
Without concise docs and examples, adoption will fragment and quality policy will drift.

Expected outcomes:
- Add practical docs for schema, discovery order, rule examples, and troubleshooting.
- Define rollout checks for CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add maintainer docs with minimal examples for regex and ast-grep rules.
- Document rule discovery/precedence and failure modes.
- Provide CI integration guidance and recommended scripts.
- Include a migration checklist used by at least one package as proof.
close_reason: Added quality rules and rollout documentation with schema, precedence, CLI usage, and checklist.

title: Sandbox: resolve package-level PHPStan errors and tighten type annotations
description:
Problem: package PHPStan check currently fails (39 errors), mostly missing iterable value types and one non-exhaustive match path.
Context: static analysis gate should be clean for 2.0 release readiness.

Expected outcomes:
- package/sandbox PHPStan run is green.
- Iterable/value types are explicit and stable across public/internal APIs.
- Type-driven docs and signatures reduce ambiguity for contributors.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add required phpdoc/native type hints for arrays/lists/maps across package.
- Fix non-exhaustive match issue in Sandbox API.
- Run and pass `vendor/bin/phpstan analyse -c packages/sandbox/phpstan.neon`.
- Include any necessary baseline reduction/removal as part of acceptance.
close_reason: Reproduced sandbox PHPStan failures, added explicit iterable/value-type annotations across package APIs/internal helpers, and verified gates: phpstan clean + sandbox tests passing.

title: ResponseIterators: ExtractDelta snapshot parsing is O(n²) on long streams
description:
## Problem and context
`ExtractDeltaReducer` rebuilds buffer from full cumulative snapshot each frame (`response->content()` / `toolArgsSnapshot`) and re-parses repeatedly. This yields O(n²) behavior with large chunk counts.

Location:
- `packages/instructor/src/ResponseIterators/ModularPipeline/Pipeline/ExtractDeltaReducer.php`

Impact:
- Latency and CPU spikes on large streamed payloads.
- Production cost and tail-latency regressions.

## Expected outcomes
- Streaming extraction cost is near-linear with chunk count.
- Existing snapshot semantics are preserved (no behavioral regressions).
- Clear performance guardrail exists in tests/benchmarks.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add performance-oriented regression/characterization test (or benchmark assertion) showing current superlinear behavior risk.
- Refactor extraction/buffer path to avoid repeated full-snapshot reparsing.
- Validate functional parity with existing modular extraction tests.
- Demonstrate measurable improvement (document before/after metric in issue comments or test notes).

## Process spec (bug)
1. Add characterization test/benchmark that exposes current scaling behavior.
2. Run to capture baseline.
3. Implement optimization without changing external semantics.
4. Re-run characterization + functional tests to confirm improvement and correctness.
acceptance_criteria: Regression/benchmark test first: demonstrate superlinear growth with long cumulative snapshots; optimize extraction/buffering strategy; confirm improved scaling and preserved semantics.
notes: Added characterization regression in ExtractDeltaReducerTest: cumulative snapshots previously assembled as lengths [1,2,3,4], now [1,1,1,1] via stream-local delta assembly. Refactored ExtractDeltaReducer to maintain active buffer + last snapshot and append only incremental suffix when snapshot grows monotonically, with fallback rebuild for non-monotonic snapshots. Verified with pest packages/instructor/tests/Unit/ModularPipeline/ExtractDeltaReducerTest.php and pest packages/instructor/tests/Unit/ModularPipeline --compact.
close_reason: Closed

title: Logging Symfony factory crashes when security token user is non-object (e.g. string principal)
description:
Problem and context:
Symfony user-context enricher assumes token user always supports `getUserIdentifier()`. In real setups token user can be a string (anonymous/system principal) or another unsupported type. Current code calls method on string and throws fatal `Error`.

Code:
- `packages/logging/src/Factories/SymfonyLoggingFactory.php:85`
- `packages/logging/src/Factories/SymfonyLoggingFactory.php:89`
- `packages/logging/src/Factories/SymfonyLoggingFactory.php:93`

Repro:
- `security.token_storage` returns token where `getUser()` is `'anon'`
- Pipeline invocation throws: `Call to a member function getUserIdentifier() on string`

Expected outcomes:
Logging pipeline should never crash due to user principal shape; unsupported principals should degrade gracefully to empty/minimal user context.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression tests for token users as:
  - object implementing identifier method,
  - plain string principal,
  - null/unsupported values.
- Guard user extraction with robust type checks and fallbacks.
- Ensure logging still writes entries when user context cannot be enriched.
- Keep existing enriched fields for supported user objects.
close_reason: Fixed: added SymfonyLoggingFactory user-context regression tests and hardened token/user extraction for string, object, null, and unsupported principal shapes without crashing.

title: Codify ergonomic facades with strictly typed constructors and internals
description:
Context:
The codebase now has a clear architectural decision: facade-level APIs should stay ergonomic, but constructors and internal APIs should be strictly typed. That rule is not applied consistently yet across top-level convenience surfaces, Laravel fakes, and internal request/data objects.

Scope examples:
- packages/laravel/src/Testing/InferenceFake.php
- packages/laravel/src/Testing/StructuredOutputFake.php
- packages/laravel/src/Facades/StructuredOutput.php
- top-level convenience surfaces that should remain ergonomic while normalizing immediately into typed internals

Expected outcome:
- User-facing facades, fakes, and convenience APIs continue to accept ergonomic inputs where that is intentional, but they normalize immediately into typed values before crossing into internal builders/data objects.
- Internal constructors and core request/value objects no longer carry convenience unions just to support facade ergonomics.

Acceptance criteria:
- Facade-level and fake/testing APIs that are intentionally ergonomic are explicitly documented and tested as such.
- Ergonomic entry points normalize immediately into typed `Messages`, `ToolDefinitions`, `ToolChoice`, `ResponseFormat`, and related value objects before crossing internal boundaries.
- Constructors and internal/core APIs do not reintroduce convenience unions just to support facade ergonomics.
- Laravel facade phpdocs and fakes align with the chosen ergonomic-vs-typed boundary.
- Add or update tests that enforce the distinction between ergonomic public entry points and strictly typed internal constructors.
notes: Executing now as task 3 of 3. Scope: Laravel facades/fakes and other ergonomic entry points normalize immediately into typed internals without reintroducing unions in constructors/core request objects.
close_reason: Completed. Ergonomic entry points and Laravel/testing surfaces now normalize immediately into typed internals, with regression coverage for the fake boundary.

title: Align option handling between StructuredOutput and request builder
description:
Outcomes:
- Options API accepts non-boolean values without TypeError.
- StructuredOutput and StructuredOutputRequestBuilder option methods align in types and behavior.
- Tests cover non-boolean options.

Acceptance criteria:
- `StructuredOutput::withOption` and `StructuredOutputRequestBuilder::withOption` accept the same value types and allow float/string options without errors.
- `withStreaming` continues to set the stream option correctly.
- Relevant tests updated or added.
close_reason: Align withOption value types and add options test

title: [enhancement] AgentFinished event should reflect termination reason in status
description:
## Problem

When an agent is forcibly stopped by a guard criterion (e.g., TokenUsageLimit), the AgentFinished event reports `status: 'in_progress'` which is confusing since the agent has actually finished.

## Evidence

Log output:
```
AgentFinished • status => 'in_progress' • steps => 1
```

This occurs because:
1. TokenUsageLimit stopped continuation after step 1
2. AgentState.status was never transitioned from 'in_progress'
3. AgentFinished event just reflects the state's status field

## Expected Behavior

When an agent terminates due to a guard criterion, the status should indicate the termination was abnormal, e.g.:
- `status: 'interrupted'` or `status: 'stopped'`
- Or add a separate `stopReason` field alongside status

## Current Behavior

Status remains 'in_progress' even after forced termination, making it hard to distinguish between:
- Agent that completed naturally
- Agent interrupted by token limit
- Agent interrupted by time limit
- Agent interrupted by step limit

## Suggested Fix

Option A: Add `terminationReason` field to AgentFinished event (non-breaking)
Option B: Update AgentState.status to 'interrupted' when guards force stop
Option C: Add `wasInterrupted` boolean to AgentFinished event

## Impact

Low - debugging/observability improvement only
close_reason: Fixed: AgentFinished now sets status to Completed/Failed based on StopReason

title: Add ToolCall/ToolResult accessor methods to Message
description:
Add convenience accessors on Message that formalize the tool metadata contract using typed objects.

## File to modify
packages/messages/src/Message.php

## New methods
```php
// Query methods
public function isToolCall(): bool;    // assistant message with tool calls
public function isToolResult(): bool;  // tool role message
public function hasToolCalls(): bool;  // has non-empty tool_calls metadata

// Tool call accessors (on assistant messages)
public function toolCalls(): ToolCalls;  // hydrated from metadata

// Tool result accessor (on tool messages)
public function toolResult(): ?ToolResult;  // hydrated from metadata
```

## Hydration behavior
- toolCalls() reads metadata('tool_calls') and hydrates:
  - If already ToolCall objects → wrap in ToolCalls
  - If raw arrays → ToolCall::fromArray() each, wrap in ToolCalls
  - If absent → ToolCalls::empty()
- toolResult() reads metadata fields and constructs ToolResult:
  - content from $this->content()->toString()
  - callId from metadata('tool_call_id')
  - toolName from metadata('tool_name')
  - isError from metadata('is_error', false)
  - Returns null if !isTool()

## Why accessors not properties
- Message is final readonly with existing constructor signature
- Metadata storage is the established pattern
- Lazy hydration means zero cost when not accessed
- Backwards compatible — existing code setting metadata arrays still works

## Acceptance criteria
1. isToolCall() returns true only for assistant messages with non-empty tool_calls metadata
2. isToolResult() returns true only for tool-role messages
3. toolCalls() returns ToolCalls collection — handles both typed and legacy array metadata
4. toolResult() returns ToolResult with all fields populated from metadata + content
5. toolResult() returns null for non-tool messages
6. Existing Message tests pass unmodified
7. New unit tests cover all accessor methods
close_reason: Superseded by the implemented MessageType-based design: Message now carries typed ToolCalls/ToolResult state directly, exposes hasToolCalls()/toolCalls()/hasToolResult()/toolResult(), and classifies semantic tool message state via type() instead of adding separate isToolCall()/isToolResult() helpers. Hydration is handled in MessageInput rather than lazy metadata parsing on Message itself.

title: [bug] Duplicate ContinuationEvaluated events emitted per iteration in StepByStep
close_reason: Fixed: Removed redundant hasNextStep() check from nextStep() in StepByStep.php

title: Utils: validate silent JSON encoding failure behavior for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests Cognesy\Utils\Json\Json may silently collapse JSON encoding failures to an empty string in public helpers such as Json::encode(), Json::fromArray(), and Json::format(). Because these helpers are used broadly across the codebase for tool arguments, metadata, exceptions, and event payloads, silent failure may erase data instead of surfacing an error.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. Do not assume this finding must be fixed just because it was reported. First validate the claim against the current codebase and real call sites, then judge whether the work is valuable enough to include in 2.0.0.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the silent-failure claim with focused reproductions, including invalid UTF-8 and other realistic encoding failures.
- Identify which public helpers and downstream packages are actually affected, and whether current callers intentionally rely on empty-string fallback semantics.
- Make a release judgement: justify fixing it in 2.0.0, document it as intentional, or explicitly defer/reject it if the value is low relative to compatibility risk.
- If the work is worth doing, implement the smallest clear change that makes JSON encoding behavior explicit and unsurprising.

Related chores:
- Create and confirm a regression test for the validated failure mode.
- Fix or update related tests in packages/utils and downstream packages if public behavior changes.
- Update examples and docs/CHEATSHEET/README only if public encoding semantics change.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and records whether it is true, false, or partially true.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, public JSON helpers no longer fail silently in the validated problematic cases, or the task clearly documents why existing behavior remains.
- A regression test is added and confirmed for the chosen scenario.
- Related tests, examples, and docs are updated where needed, or the task records why no changes are necessary.
notes: Validated with focused runtime repro. Json::encode(), Json::fromArray(), and Json::format() collapse encoding failure to an empty string because they use the json_encode result in a falsey-or-empty-string fallback. Invalid UTF-8 reproduces this deterministically. The impact surface is broad: 54 non-test call sites across agents, messages, polyglot, instructor, addons, events, and utils. Changing these helpers to throw or return a Result would be a cross-package public semantic change, so I did not make code changes under the current instruction to defer major changes to human approval.

title: Add regression test for failure after prior step stopping loop
description: Add tests (Agent and ToolUse; optionally Chat/Collaboration when forceThrowOnFailure=false) that exercise a successful step followed by a driver exception. Assert hasNextStep becomes false/finalStep stops and state remains Failed (no extra steps), preventing stale lastStepResult from keeping the loop running. Covers issue instructor-6yc.
close_reason: Added Agent regression test and already-added ToolUse test to ensure hasNextStep stops after failure; both pass.

title: Add tests ensuring internal options never reach provider request bodies
description: Add unit coverage to assert internal execution options (retryPolicy, etc.) are stripped from request payloads for all inference drivers (OpenAI, Gemini, Mistral, Deepseek, Perplexity) and embeddings requests. Anthropic has a specific test; others are missing.
close_reason: Added retryPolicy omission tests for Gemini, Mistral, Deepseek, Perplexity, and embeddings body formats

title: Trim AgentState/ExecutionState (remove continuation plumbing)
description: Trimmed AgentState/ExecutionState continuation plumbing, removed continuation outcome accessors, simplified error handling to StopSignal, dropped unused continuation request clear; tests updated.
acceptance_criteria:
- ExecutionState no longer stores pendingEvaluations/ContinuationOutcome
- ContinuationEvaluation/EvaluationProcessor removed if unused
- AgentState reduced to identity + context + execution + stop signal accessors
- Tests green; coverage for stop reason and event payloads updated

title: Refactor AnthropicMessageFormat to use typed Message objects
description:
Refactor AnthropicMessageFormat to iterate Message objects instead of converting to arrays. Most complex of the four formatters.

## Current state
- map() calls $messages->toArray() then loops over raw arrays
- mapMessage(array $message) branches on string role + nested _metadata array
- toNativeContent(string|array $content) branches is_string vs array iteration
- contentPartToNative(array $contentPart) reads $contentPart['type'], $contentPart['cache_control']
- toNativeImage parses $contentPart['image_url']['url'] with Str helpers
- toNativeToolCall reads $message['_metadata']['tool_calls'][0]['function']

## Expected outcomes
- map() iterates $messages->messageList()->all() yielding Message objects
- mapMessage(Message $message) uses $message->isAssistant(), $message->isTool()
- toNativeContent(Content $content) uses $content->isComposite() instead of is_string branch
- contentPartToNative(ContentPart $part) uses $part->type(), $part->toString(), $part->has('cache_control'), $part->get('image_url')
- Tool methods use $message->metadata()->get('tool_calls', []) and $message->metadata()->get('tool_call_id', '')
- No array $message or array $contentPart parameters remain

## Acceptance criteria
1. All method signatures use Message, Content, ContentPart instead of array
2. No call to $messages->toArray() in the class
3. Content dispatch uses Content::isComposite() instead of is_string()
4. ContentPart iteration uses partsList()->all() instead of raw array foreach
5. cache_control check uses $part->has('cache_control') instead of array key access
6. Image handling uses $part->get('image_url') instead of $contentPart['image_url']
7. Existing tests pass without modification
8. No changes to CanMapMessages interface or AnthropicBodyFormat
close_reason: Implemented: AnthropicMessageFormat now maps typed Message/Content/ContentPart objects and uses MessageType-driven dispatch.

title: Update RetryLimit criterion
description: Return ForbidContinuation when max retries exceeded, AllowContinuation otherwise.
close_reason: Updated to use ContinuationDecision enum

title: Accumulate usage for failure steps in onFailure
description: onFailure() builds a transitionState with withAccumulatedUsage() for evaluation, but the returned failed state does not accumulate the failure step's usage. Because processors are bypassed on failure, usage totals (and AgentFailed/ToolUseFailed events) under-report token usage. Update failure path to carry accumulated usage (or run AccumulateTokenUsage) so state->usage reflects failed step usage.
close_reason: Accumulate failure-step usage in onFailure across Agent/ToolUse/Chat/Collaboration and added regression test

title: Refactor StructuredOutput into a thin, single-use facade
description:
Outcomes:
- StructuredOutput is a thin builder/facade with minimal mutable state.
- Pipeline creation and request/config state live in dedicated builders/factories.
- Chainable API preserved with a clear single-use lifecycle.

Acceptance criteria:
- StructuredOutput does not retain execution state between calls; one request per instance is enforced by design or documented.
- Builder/factory responsibilities are explicit and isolated from the facade.
- Tests updated to reflect single-use behavior.

title: http-client: json_encode failure silently produces empty request body
description:
File: packages/http-client/src/Data/HttpRequestBody.php:19

Problem:
  is_array($body) => json_encode($body) ?: '',

If json_encode() returns false (non-serializable value: resource, Closure, circular ref), the request body becomes an empty string with no exception raised. The HTTP request is sent silently with an empty body, making the issue invisible to the caller.

Expected outcome:
  An exception is thrown when json_encode fails, preventing a malformed request from being sent.

Acceptance criteria:
  - json_encode failure throws an explicit exception (e.g. InvalidArgumentException or a domain HttpRequestException) with a clear message
  - No silent empty-string fallback on encoding failure
  - Unit test covering the error path
close_reason: Added fail-first regression for json_encode failure in HttpRequestBody, replaced silent empty-string fallback with explicit InvalidArgumentException using JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR, and updated HTTP client docs/cheatsheet.

title: Unify sync and streaming response pipelines (shared extraction, no Result)
description:
Outcomes:
- Sync and streaming final responses use the same extract/deserialize/validate/transform path.
- Streaming partial updates still skip validation.
- Extraction contracts are unified and Result is removed from extraction surfaces.

Acceptance criteria:
- Sync and streaming final value generation share the same pipeline stages or shared classes.
- Partial streaming objects are not validated.
- `CanExtractContent` and `CanExtractResponse` are consolidated into a single contract.
- `ResponseExtractor` no longer returns Result; failures use exceptions or nulls as agreed.
- Existing extraction/streaming tests updated and pass.

Design notes (approved):
- Use a single buffer abstraction for both sync and streaming extraction; sync builds buffer from full InferenceResponse.
- ResponseExtractor returns array directly; internal extractor chain returns array or throws (or null for “not me”).
- Parsing and format detection remain inside extractors (format-aware), not artificially separated.
- Extraction surface removes Result; ResponseExtractor aggregates failures and throws a single extraction exception on total failure.
- Final response pipeline is shared for sync + streaming completion; streaming partials remain unvalidated.
close_reason: Refactored extraction pipeline; extraction tests pass. composer test fails due to missing AgentBlueprint interface in addons tests.

title: Resolve LLMProvider immutability mismatch
description:
Outcomes:
- LLMProvider immutability expectations are clear and accurate.

Context:
- Problem: Class comment says configuration is immutable after construction, but methods like withLLMPreset/withDsn mutate $this.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/LLMProvider.php (immutability comment and with* methods).
- Suggested fix: either update the comment to reflect mutability, or make with* methods return cloned instances to be truly immutable.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation matches behavior, or behavior matches documentation.
- No misleading immutability claims remain.

How to Test:
- No new tests required; ensure any behavior change is covered by existing tests.
close_reason: Update LLMProvider comment to reflect mutable with* configuration

title: Fix ContextCacheLLM example assertion error
description: [95] ContextCacheLLM example fails: PHP Fatal error: Uncaught AssertionError: assert($response->usage()->... (6.23 sec). Investigate example, reproduce, and fix.
close_reason: Removed brittle cache token asserts and added cache usage mock test

comments.author: ddebowczyk
comments.text: # Investigation & testing protocol\n\nObligatory: start by investigating the code causing the issue. After an initial understanding of the offending module/class, TEST FIRST: recreate the error using a deterministic mechanism (mock http client or mock inference or deterministic agent driver). Ensure the deterministic mechanism does not hide the problem; if needed, use a lower-level deterministic mechanism (mock http -> mock inference -> mock agent).

title: Align withToolChoice signature with request builder
description:
Outcomes:
- HandlesRequestBuilder::withToolChoice accepts the same types as InferenceRequest (string|array).

Context:
- Problem: HandlesRequestBuilder::withToolChoice currently accepts string only, but InferenceRequestBuilder and InferenceRequest support string|array toolChoice.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Traits/HandlesRequestBuilder.php (withToolChoice).
- Suggested fix: update signature to string|array and pass through unchanged.

Acceptance Criteria:
- withToolChoice signature is string|array and static analysis passes.
- Existing string usage remains backward compatible.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test or static-analysis-only check that calling withToolChoice(['type' => 'auto']) works and builds a request with the array preserved.
close_reason: Allow array toolChoice in HandlesRequestBuilder and add Pest coverage

title: Remove unused $tmp in StructuredOutputStream::finalResponse
description:
Outcomes:
- Remove unused temporary variable in StructuredOutputStream::finalResponse.

Context:
- Problem: $tmp = $partialResponse is unused and adds noise.
- Location: packages/instructor/src/StructuredOutputStream.php (finalResponse).
- Suggested fix: remove the unused assignment.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No unused variables in finalResponse.

How to Test:
- No tests required.
close_reason: Remove unused variable from StructuredOutputStream::finalResponse

title: Epic: Simplify pending operation lifecycle
description:
Goal: keep pending objects in 2.0.0, but narrow them to clear lazy-operation handles and remove duplicated lifecycle orchestration where possible.

Context:
- PendingInference and PendingStructuredOutput are both justified public objects because they expose different completion contracts.
- The problem is duplicated outer lifecycle/session orchestration, not the existence of pending objects themselves.
- The north star is low memory for long streams, simple explicit code, and no generic abstraction tricks.

Expected outcome:
- PendingInference remains the raw pending operation handle for Polyglot.
- PendingStructuredOutput remains the structured pending operation handle for Instructor.
- Instructor lifecycle orchestration is collapsed behind one internal execution session/runner instead of being spread across PendingStructuredOutput.
- No per-chunk memory regression is introduced.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pending objects keep a clear public role: lazy execution trigger, cached result/stream coordination, and convenience accessors.
- Instructor no longer duplicates raw pending lifecycle logic in multiple places.
- Tests, docs, and examples reflect the simplified lifecycle boundaries.
close_reason: Completed the pending lifecycle simplification: both packages keep clear public pending handles while mutable orchestration lives in explicit internal session objects, with docs/tests updated accordingly.

title: Define pending object boundaries and invariants
description:
Expected outcome:
- The public responsibilities of PendingInference and PendingStructuredOutput are documented and made explicit in code comments/docs.
- Pending objects are defined as lazy operation handles, not generic lifecycle engines.
- The intended ownership boundary between Polyglot raw lifecycle and Instructor structured lifecycle is frozen before refactoring internals.

Acceptance criteria:
- PendingInference and PendingStructuredOutput each have a short, explicit contract describing what they own and what they do not own.
- No new shared base class or generic pending abstraction is introduced.
- Tests are added or updated to lock in the expected pending semantics: lazy execution, single execution/cache coordination, and stream/result interoperability.
- Relevant docs are updated where pending semantics are part of the public story: package OVERVIEW/INTERNALS/usage docs as appropriate.
- Examples are only updated if they currently misrepresent pending behavior.
notes: Added explicit pending-object contracts in code/docs and test coverage for lazy execution plus single-execution coordination.

title: Collapse Instructor lifecycle orchestration behind one internal execution session
description:
Expected outcome:
- Instructor has one internal owner for structured pending lifecycle orchestration (for example a session/runner object), instead of spreading it across PendingStructuredOutput.
- PendingStructuredOutput becomes a thin public wrapper over that session.
- No additional per-chunk allocations are introduced in streaming paths.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cached raw response, cached stream, lazy execution driver creation, and finalized execution state are coordinated in one internal Instructor object.
- PendingStructuredOutput delegates instead of implementing its own branching lifecycle state machine.
- Streaming continues to rely on mutable in-place state for long streams; no per-chunk response snapshots are reintroduced.
- Instructor tests covering get()/response()/rawResponse()/stream() interplay are updated or expanded.
- Instructor INTERNALS/CHEATSHEET/usage docs are updated if public behavior or mental model changes.
- Examples are updated only if construction or access patterns materially change.
notes: Extracted StructuredOutputExecutionSession and reduced PendingStructuredOutput to a thin delegator without changing public behavior or streaming memory characteristics.

title: Reduce duplicated raw lifecycle logic at the Polyglot/Instructor boundary
description:
Expected outcome:
- Instructor stops duplicating raw pending lifecycle logic that already belongs to Polyglot.
- The handoff between raw inference completion and structured output completion becomes explicit and minimal.

Acceptance criteria:
- Instructor no longer mirrors raw pending-state decisions that can be delegated to Polyglot or to the new Instructor execution session.
- The boundary between raw InferenceResponse access and structured StructuredOutputResponse/value access is simple and explicit.
- Regression tests cover mixed access patterns: get() after response(), rawResponse() after stream(), stream() after response(), and cache-policy-sensitive paths.
- Docs explain the separation between raw inference lifecycle and structured output lifecycle where needed.
- Examples are updated if any public access patterns are clarified or renamed.
notes: Reducing remaining raw-lifecycle duplication between Instructor session logic and Polyglot pending inference behavior.
close_reason: Removed the redundant session-level raw-response cache, delegated raw response resolution to finalized execution or memoized stream state, and added regression coverage for rawResponse() after streaming.

title: Trim PendingInference to raw lifecycle concerns only
description:
Expected outcome:
- PendingInference remains the raw pending operation handle, but its responsibilities are constrained to raw execution lifecycle concerns.
- Any logic that is no longer appropriate for the pending wrapper is pushed into clearer helpers within Polyglot.

Acceptance criteria:
- PendingInference owns lazy execution, retry/result coordination for raw inference, cached stream/final response handles, and raw convenience accessors only.
- No structured-output-specific concerns leak back into PendingInference.
- Polyglot tests are updated or added for stream/result/retry one-shot semantics and caching behavior.
- Polyglot docs/internal docs are updated if the pending mental model becomes simpler or more explicit.
- Examples are updated only if raw inference usage changes.
notes: Trimming PendingInference to raw lazy execution, memoized response/stream access, and raw convenience accessors only; checking for logic that can move behind small explicit helpers without affecting streaming memory.
close_reason: Moved raw retry/stream/cache lifecycle orchestration behind InferenceExecutionSession, leaving PendingInference as the public lazy handle with raw convenience accessors.

title: Final pending-lifecycle cleanup across docs, tests, and examples
description:
Expected outcome:
- The repo tells one coherent story about pending operations in 2.0.0.
- Tests, docs, and examples no longer reflect the old duplicated lifecycle model.

Acceptance criteria:
- Targeted instructor and polyglot test suites pass after the refactor.
- Pending-related docs are consistent across README/CHEATSHEET/usage/internals/upgrade notes where relevant.
- Any stale examples or comments referring to the old pending orchestration are removed or updated.
- No public docs imply that pending objects are large mutable lifecycle engines; they are described as lazy handles over explicit execution/session machinery.
notes: Final cleanup pass for pending-lifecycle story across docs/tests/examples after session extraction on both Polyglot and Instructor sides; checking for stale descriptions of pending wrappers as inline lifecycle engines.
close_reason: Aligned pending-lifecycle docs across Polyglot and Instructor on the public-handle/internal-session split, corrected stale StructuredOutput response docs, and revalidated the package suites.

title: [schema2][v2-api] Radical review and split of public API surface
description:
Problem and context:
Schema2 has undergone heavy internal simplification, but the public API still includes classes with mixed maturity and unclear long-term value. Before further invasive refactors and downstream callsite rewrites, we need a hard split of the current public API into (1) solid 2.0 foundation and (2) questionable/must-change elements.

Expected outcomes:
- Complete inventory of public classes under Cognesy\Schema exposed to other packages/docs/examples.
- Every public class assigned to one bucket:
  1) solid foundation for 2.0 API
  2) questionable, to be refactored, must-change for cleaner/simpler API
- For questionable classes: explicit redesign direction and target replacement shape.
- Downstream impact map listing external callsites that will need adaptation.
- Output serves as input for a new implementation/migration plan centered on foundation API + redesigned parts.
acceptance_criteria:
- Public API inventory is complete and reproducible (based on code + usage scan, not assumptions).
- Bucketing decision is documented for each public class with concise rationale (keep as-is vs redesign).
- Must-change items include concrete redesign intent (what changes, what becomes obsolete, compatibility stance).
- Callsite impact is mapped across monorepo packages (at minimum: instructor, dynamic, addons, experimental, agents docs/examples).
- A follow-up execution plan can be created directly from this task output without additional discovery pass.
close_reason: Completed V2 public API split analysis and downstream migration guidance

comments.author: Dariusz Debowczyk
comments.text:
Completed deep API-surface review and split with downstream impact map.

Deliverable:
- packages/schema2/research/v2-api-surface-review.md

What is included:
- Reproducible inventory method over all public classes under packages/schema2/src
- Split into 2 groups: foundation vs must-change (with rationale)
- Contract-first target API proposal to narrow public surface
- Explicit leakage map for runtime packages using schema internals
- Rewrite recommendations per package: instructor, dynamic, addons, experimental, agents docs/examples
- Dynamic alignment section tied to research/better-dynamic-structure-design.md
- Decision set for moving schema away from utility-bucket role and toward focused schema capabilities.

title: 2.0: move stream replay/caching to low-level transport and make stream semantics explicit
description:
## Context
Current default response stream caching is memory-heavy because full streamed state snapshots are retained by default. We want a 2.0 simplification that keeps memory bounded while preserving explicit replay behavior when needed.

## Goal
Move stream replay/caching concern to a low-level transport boundary (HTTP stream layer) so downstream layers (polyglot/instructor iterators) do not need to manage cache mechanics.

## Scope
- Introduce a low-level stream cache manager contract (e.g. `CanManageStreamCache`) at runtime/HTTP client boundary.
- Keep downstream stream APIs one-shot by default unless replay is explicitly enabled by injected cache manager/policy.
- Ensure repeated final access methods remain idempotent via memoized final response.

## Expected Outcomes
1. Default behavior is memory-safe for long streams (no unbounded in-memory snapshot accumulation in downstream stream wrappers).
2. Replay is explicit and configurable via low-level cache backend (`none`, `memory`, optionally `temp-file`).
3. Second-pass iteration semantics are deterministic:
   - with replay cache: replays captured bytes/events;
   - without replay cache: fails fast with clear exception.
4. No behavioral regression for single-pass streaming UX (`partials()/responses()/sequence()`) and final result APIs.
5. Clear docs communicate that replay != re-calling provider; replay only reuses already captured stream data.

## Implementation Notes
- Keep policy propagation coherent between Instructor and Polyglot request/execution models.
- Remove/avoid downstream full `StructuredOutputExecution` snapshot caching for replay.
- Preserve existing event semantics (`...Updated` during streaming, `...Generated` on finalization).
- Ensure retry/attempt logic remains unchanged for real provider calls.

## Test Plan
Add/adjust tests to cover:
1. Memory profile guard:
   - long streaming payload does not grow via downstream snapshot cache when default replay is disabled.
2. One-shot semantics:
   - second iteration over stream without replay cache throws expected exception message/type.
3. Replay semantics:
   - with replay cache enabled, second iteration yields same sequence without new provider call.
4. Final memoization:
   - `finalResponse()/finalValue()` remain idempotent and do not re-dispatch terminal events.
5. Partial/final invariants:
   - progressive updates still emitted immediately;
   - generated/final events emitted exactly once.
6. Backend coverage (if implemented):
   - `memory` backend correctness;
   - `temp-file` backend correctness and cleanup.

## Docs & Examples Changes Required
Update docs in `packages/*/docs` and `./examples` to include:
1. New low-level cache/replay architecture and defaults.
2. Explicit one-shot stream contract and second-pass behavior.
3. How to enable replay, with tradeoffs (`none` vs `memory` vs `temp-file`).
4. Caveat section: replayed stream is captured data, not a fresh LLM execution.
5. Migration notes for 2.0 users relying on implicit replay.
6. Example snippets demonstrating:
   - default one-shot usage;
   - replay-enabled usage;
   - safe collection pattern for consumers needing re-iteration.

## Non-Goals
- Making LLM calls themselves deterministic or re-callable.
- Changing provider-side caching semantics.
notes:
Implemented first execution slice: low-level HTTP stream cache manager and policy mapping, one-shot stream semantics by default, default cache policy switched to none, targeted tests added/passing, and docs/examples updated for replay contract.
Added optional stream cache manager injection path via Inference::withStreamCacheManager(), InferenceRuntime static factories, and InferenceDriverFactory with BaseInferenceRequestDriver passthrough.
Completed replay path for ResponseCachePolicy::Memory in polyglot/instructor streams (second-pass replay without new provider call), added replay tests and default-none config tests, and added upgrade migration note for one-shot default + memory opt-in replay.
Added cache-footprint guard tests to ensure default None does not retain replay buffers, while Memory policy does.

title: [polyglot][2.0] Validate embeddings retry-on-status behavior and release value
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that embeddings `retryOnStatus` does not actually work for normal provider HTTP error responses, and explicitly judge whether fixing that behavior is worth the effort for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current codebase, retry policy, and a deterministic repro. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. Retry behavior should either work clearly or be documented clearly; hidden dead config is not acceptable if this is in scope for 2.0.0.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: `packages/polyglot/src/Embeddings/Drivers/BaseEmbedDriver.php` converts HTTP status failures into generic `RuntimeException`, while `EmbeddingsRetryPolicy` only retries `HttpRequestException` statuses and network/timeout exceptions. That may make configured status-based retries (`429`, `500`, etc.) ineffective.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the claim with concrete code references and a deterministic retry test.
- Decide whether embeddings should support status-based retries for 2.0.0, or whether the policy surface should be simplified/documented differently.
- If worth doing now, implement the smallest clear fix.

## Acceptance criteria
- A deterministic regression test demonstrates whether `retryOnStatus` triggers for provider responses like `429` or `500`.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: embeddings retry behavior is explicit and matches the public retry policy surface.
- If go: retry tests cover at least status-based retry and non-retriable failure behavior.
- If go: docs/examples/CHEATSHEET reflect the chosen embeddings retry semantics.
- If no-go: docs/CHEATSHEET explicitly describe the limitation or policy simplification.

## Related chores
- Add/update regression tests for embeddings retries on provider error statuses.
- Review embeddings docs/examples for retry claims.
- Update docs/CHEATSHEET and remove stale wording if needed.
notes: Confirmed likely bug from code inspection: BaseEmbedDriver throws RuntimeException on HTTP status failures, bypassing EmbeddingsRetryPolicy status-based retry path. Implementing minimal fix with focused regression coverage.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed. BaseEmbedDriver was converting provider HTTP status failures into RuntimeException, which bypassed EmbeddingsRetryPolicy status-based retries. It now throws HttpRequestException with request/response context so retryOnStatus works for normal 429/5xx responses. Added focused regression coverage for retriable 429 and non-retriable 400 paths. Existing docs already matched the intended retry surface, so no docs change was required.

title: Migrate polyglot ToolCall/ToolCalls imports to packages/messages
description:
Replace Cognesy\Polyglot\Inference\Data\ToolCall with Cognesy\Messages\ToolCall across the codebase, and Cognesy\Polyglot\Inference\Collections\ToolCalls with Cognesy\Messages\ToolCalls.

## Scope

### polyglot package (~15 files)
- Response adapters (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenResponses, CohereV2): use new ToolCall, keep ToolCallDelta/ToolCallId/ToolCallIdByStreamIndex in polyglot
- InferenceResponse, PartialInferenceResponse: use new ToolCalls
- InferenceStreamState: use new ToolCalls

### agents package (~25 files)
- ToolExecutor, ToolExecution, ToolExecutionFormatter
- AgentLoop, ReActDriver, MakeToolCalls
- Hook/Data/HookContext, Exceptions
- All test files referencing ToolCall

### addons package
- ToolUse/*, ToolExecutionFormatter, tests

## Polyglot-specific classes that STAY in polyglot
- ToolCallId (extends OpaqueExternalId — stricter validation for polyglot layer)
- ToolCallDelta (streaming concern)
- ToolCallIdByStreamIndex (streaming concern)

## Strategy for polyglot ToolCalls streaming methods
The current polyglot ToolCalls has withAddedToolCall() and withLastToolCallUpdated() used by InferenceStreamState. Options:
- Add these to the messages-layer ToolCalls (they're generic enough)
- Or keep a polyglot StreamingToolCalls that wraps messages ToolCalls
Decision: add to messages ToolCalls — they're useful generally and don't introduce streaming dependencies

## Handling toToolCallArray()
Current polyglot ToolCall::toToolCallArray() produces OpenAI Chat format. This logic moves to:
- OpenAIMessageFormat::toNativeToolCall() — for Chat Completions output
- XAiMessageFormat::toNativeToolCall() — XAI override
- ToolExecutionFormatter needs updating if it currently uses toToolCallArray() for metadata storage

## Acceptance criteria
1. No imports of Cognesy\Polyglot\Inference\Data\ToolCall remain (except polyglot-internal streaming code if needed)
2. No imports of Cognesy\Polyglot\Inference\Collections\ToolCalls remain
3. Old polyglot ToolCall class deleted or converted to alias/deprecation shim
4. Old polyglot ToolCalls class deleted or converted to alias
5. All existing tests pass across polyglot, agents, and addons packages
6. toToolCallArray() logic relocated to formatters
close_reason: Completed: Polyglot/Agents/Addons now import ToolCall and ToolCalls from packages/messages, the old Polyglot ToolCall/ToolCalls classes are gone, and provider-specific array shaping was moved out of the shared value object layer. Focused messages/polyglot/agents verification passed; one addons ToolUse flow failure appears unrelated to the import migration itself and should be handled separately if still reproducible.

title: [instructor] High: withInput(mixed) contract mismatch causes scalar TypeError
description:
## Problem and context
`StructuredOutput::withInput()` is declared as `withInput(mixed $input)`, suggesting any input type is accepted.

Implementation delegates directly to `Messages::fromInput($input)`, which requires `string|array|object`. Scalars like `int|float|bool|null` trigger immediate `TypeError`.

### Repro
`(new StructuredOutput())->withInput(123)` throws:
- `TypeError: Messages::fromInput(): Argument #1 ($input) must be of type object|array|string, int given`

### Why this matters
`StructuredOutput` is a top-level facade heavily used in docs and integrations. Signature/behavior mismatch causes avoidable runtime failures and ambiguous API contract.

### Code context
- `packages/instructor/src/StructuredOutput.php` (`withInput`)

## Expected outcomes
- API contract and runtime behavior are aligned:
  - either narrow method signature to `string|array|object`, or
  - support scalar coercion/normalization consistently.
- Error messaging is explicit if unsupported types remain unsupported.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test(s) for scalar input handling consistent with chosen contract.
- Update method signature and/or implementation + docs to remove ambiguity.
- No raw `TypeError` from `Messages::fromInput` should leak for documented-supported inputs.
close_reason: Aligned withInput contract with supported message input types by narrowing signature to string|array|object, added regression tests for scalar rejection at facade boundary, and removed nested Messages::fromInput TypeError leakage.

title: Make BashTool output configurable with BashPolicy + head/tail truncation
description: Introduce BashPolicy (max output, head/tail sizes), wire into BashTool/AgentBuilder/AGENT.md, update tests as needed.
notes: Added BashPolicy (max/head/tail); BashTool truncation now head+tail; AgentBuilder/AGENT.md updated.

title: Result transformation methods fail to capture Throwable (TypeError escapes monadic flow)
description:
Problem and context:
`Result` methods (`map`, `then`, `ensure`, `tap`, `recover`, `mapError`) catch `Exception` only, while `TypeError`/other `Error` types are `Throwable` but not `Exception`. In practice, callback type errors escape and break Result pipelines unexpectedly.

Code:
- `packages/utils/src/Result/Result.php:186`
- `packages/utils/src/Result/Result.php:206`
- `packages/utils/src/Result/Result.php:239`
- `packages/utils/src/Result/Result.php:256`
- `packages/utils/src/Result/Result.php:274`
- `packages/utils/src/Result/Result.php:297`

Repro:
- `Result::success(1)->map(fn() => strlen([]))` throws `TypeError` instead of returning `Failure`.
- Same behavior observed for `then`, `tap`, `recover`, `mapError`, and `ensure` failure branch.

Expected outcomes:
Result transformations should preserve monadic error handling guarantees by converting thrown `Throwable`s into `Failure` values.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression tests asserting `TypeError` is captured into `Failure` for each transformation path.
- Update catch blocks from `Exception` to `Throwable` where transformations are expected to trap callback failures.
- Ensure existing behavior for normal `Exception` handling remains unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed: added TypeError regression tests across Result transformation methods and updated catches from Exception to Throwable to preserve monadic failure flow.

title: TypeDetailsFactory::fromValue should not crash on null/resources (unsupported types)
description:
Context:
`fromValue` can call `get_class()` on non-objects (e.g., null/resource) due object-detection path, causing runtime type errors.

Expected outcome:
`fromValue` gracefully handles unsupported/non-object values and returns safe `TypeDetails` (typically mixed/unsupported pathway) without fatal errors.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add regression tests for `null`, open resource, and closed resource values.
- No `get_class()` type error is thrown for non-object inputs.
- Behavior is documented and consistent with package type model.
close_reason: Added regression tests for null/open-resource/closed-resource inputs; hardened TypeDetailsFactory::fromValue to branch by runtime value kind and only call get_class on objects, returning mixed for unsupported values without TypeError.
