comments.author: ddebowczyk
comments.text: # Investigation & testing protocol\n\nObligatory: start by investigating the code causing the issue. After an initial understanding of the offending module/class, TEST FIRST: recreate the error using a deterministic mechanism (mock http client or mock inference or deterministic agent driver). Ensure the deterministic mechanism does not hide the problem; if needed, use a lower-level deterministic mechanism (mock http -> mock inference -> mock agent).

title: ConfigPresets: malformed preset config causes TypeError instead of domain exception
description:
Problem and context:
`ConfigPresets` declares strict return types (`array` / `string`) but forwards provider results without validating shape in key paths (`getOrDefault()` and `defaultPresetName()`). Invalid config shapes currently trigger runtime `TypeError` instead of actionable domain exceptions.

Repro examples:
- `defaultPreset` as int -> `defaultPresetName(): Return value must be of type string`
- preset value as scalar -> `getOrDefault(): Return value must be of type array`

Expected outcomes:
Config shape violations are handled predictably with `ConfigPresetNotFoundException` (or another explicit config-domain exception), not raw PHP type fatals.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression tests covering:
  - non-string `defaultPreset` value;
  - non-array preset payload returned by `getOrDefault()`.
- Validate provider output at boundaries before returning typed values.
- Throw clear domain exception messages with failing group/path context.
- Ensure phpstan max-level return-contract errors in `ConfigPresets` are resolved.
close_reason: Confirmed malformed ConfigPresets shapes leaked raw TypeError (non-string defaultPreset and non-array getOrDefault payload). Added regression tests and validated provider outputs at boundaries: defaultPresetName now enforces non-empty string and get/getOrDefault now route through array-shape validation, throwing ConfigPresetNotFoundException with path context.

title: agents: EditFileTool static withPolicy -> fromPolicy (2.0 clean cut)
description:
Goal: align static constructor naming in EditFileTool for 2.0.0.

Changes:
- Rename EditFileTool::withPolicy(ExecutionPolicy) to EditFileTool::fromPolicy(ExecutionPolicy).
- Keep/retain EditFileTool::inDirectory(...) as-is.
- Update all call sites/examples/docs/tests.
- No deprecations/aliases; clean break.

Acceptance:
- No usages of EditFileTool::withPolicy remain.
- QA/static checks for touched scope pass.
close_reason: Renamed EditFileTool::withPolicy to fromPolicy, verified no remaining usages, and ran packages/agents tests.

title: Update CanDecideToContinue interface
description: Change canContinue() return type from bool to ContinuationDecision. Rename method to decide().
close_reason: Implemented ContinuationDecision enum, updated interface, refactored ContinuationCriteria

title: Normalize ValidationResult::invalid error types
description:
Outcomes:
- ValidationResult::invalid consistently produces ValidationError[].

Context:
- Problem: ValidationResult::invalid accepts string|array and may store strings in $errors even though the property is intended to be ValidationError[].
- Location: packages/instructor/src/Validation/ValidationResult.php (invalid).
- Suggested fix: restrict invalid() to ValidationError[] or normalize strings into ValidationError objects (e.g., field "" with message).

Acceptance Criteria:
- ValidationResult::$errors always contains ValidationError instances.
- Call sites using invalid('message') continue to work via normalization or are updated.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test that calls ValidationResult::invalid('message') and asserts getErrors()[0] is a ValidationError with the message.
close_reason: Require ValidationError inputs for invalid(), normalize errors to ValidationError[] and add unit test; update call sites

title: Refactor GeminiMessageFormat to use typed Message objects
description:
Refactor GeminiMessageFormat to iterate Message objects instead of converting to arrays.

## Current state
- map() calls $messages->toArray() then loops over raw arrays
- mapMessage(array $message) branches on string role + nested _metadata
- toNativeToolCall reads $message['_metadata']['tool_calls'] array
- toNativeToolResult reads $message['_metadata']['result'], $message['_metadata']['tool_name'], $message['content']
- toNativeContentParts(string|array $contentParts) branches is_string vs array
- contentPartToNative(array $contentPart) reads $contentPart['type'], $contentPart['text'], $contentPart['image_url']['url']

## Expected outcomes
- map() iterates $messages->messageList()->all() yielding Message objects
- mapMessage(Message $message) uses $message->isAssistant(), $message->isTool()
- toNativeContentParts(Content $content) uses isComposite() instead of is_string branch
- contentPartToNative(ContentPart $part) uses $part->type(), $part->toString(), $part->get('image_url')
- toNativeToolCall uses $message->metadata()->get('tool_calls', [])
- toNativeToolResult uses $message->metadata()->get('result'), $message->metadata()->get('tool_name'), $message->content()
- No array $message or array $contentPart parameters remain

## Acceptance criteria
1. All method signatures use Message, Content, ContentPart instead of array
2. No call to $messages->toArray() in the class
3. Content dispatch uses Content::isComposite() instead of is_string()
4. ContentPart iteration uses partsList()->all()
5. Image URL extraction uses $part->get('image_url') instead of array access
6. Tool result reads metadata via typed $message->metadata()->get() calls
7. Existing tests pass without modification
8. No changes to CanMapMessages interface or GeminiBodyFormat
close_reason: Implemented: GeminiMessageFormat now maps typed Message/Content/ContentPart objects and uses MessageType-driven dispatch.

title: Documentation: Update STEPBYSTEP.md with continuation observability
description: Added ContinuationDecision enum, evaluate() method, ContinuationOutcome, StopReason, and CanExplainContinuation interface documentation
close_reason: Closed

title: Final cleanup + event payload regression test
description:
## Scope

Final cleanup pass: delete all dead code, verify no stale references, add event payload regression test, verify green suite.

## Files to check

- Grep for: `StepRecorder`, `ErrorRecorder`, `ErrorRecordingResult`, `CanReportObserverState`, `observerState()`, `applyObserverState`, `withNewStepExecution`, `withStepInProgressCleared`, `currentStepNumber` — all should return zero hits
- `AgentErrorContextResolver` — verify no references to deleted continuation types
- `AgentEventBroadcaster` — verify compatible with StopSignal-based events (it already uses `$event->stopSignal` — should be fine)
- `ContinuationEvaluated` event — verify payload fields match what AgentLoop now emits

## Acceptance criteria

- Zero grep hits for all deleted type names
- Full test suite green: `vendor/bin/pest packages/agents/tests/`
- New test: `AgentEventPayloadRegressionTest` — runs a multi-step agent scenario via DeterministicAgentDriver, captures events via wiretap, asserts:
  - `continuationEvaluated` events contain `stepNumber`, `stopSignal` (with `reason`, `source`, `message`)
  - `executionFinished` contains correct `status` and `stopReason`
  - Step numbering is sequential
  - Error path emits `executionFailed` with correct exception data

## Validations / checks / tests

- `vendor/bin/pest packages/agents/tests/` — all green
- New regression test covers happy path + error path + stop exception path
- `grep -r 'StepRecorder\|ErrorRecorder\|CanReportObserverState\|applyObserverState\|withNewStepExecution\|withStepInProgressCleared' packages/agents/src/` returns empty
close_reason: Acceptance already satisfied in current tree: zero stale-symbol hits in packages/agents/src, AgentEventPayloadRegressionTest present and covering continuation/stop/failure payloads, and full agents suite passes (415 tests).

title: Events package: make package-level quality scripts executable in monorepo workflow
description:
Problem and context:
`packages/events` composer scripts reference package-local `vendor/bin/*` and psalm config path assumptions that fail in current monorepo usage, weakening release quality gates for this package.

Expected outcomes:
Package-local test/static-analysis commands are runnable and documented for repository contributors and CI.
acceptance_criteria:
- Update scripts/config so `tests`, `phpstan`, and `psalm` commands are executable in the current repo layout.
- Document canonical package-level quality commands for maintainers.
- Verify commands run successfully (or fail on real code issues, not path/config errors).
close_reason: Made package-level quality scripts runnable in monorepo (tests/phpstan/psalm), fixed package config path assumptions, and documented canonical commands in packages/events/README.md.

title: Update ContinuationEvaluator
description: Update to work with ContinuationDecision enum if needed.
close_reason: Removed - ContinuationEvaluator was dead code, not used anywhere. The continuation system now uses ContinuationCriteria with evaluate() and CanDecideToContinue interface.

title: Add AgentState high-level transitions
description: Add AgentState::recordStep() and AgentState::failWith() (and related helpers) to reduce repeated withX chains while keeping immutability. Update usage in packages/addons/src/Agent/Core/Data/AgentState.php and packages/addons/src/Agent/Agent.php.
close_reason: Added AgentState recordStep/failWith helpers and updated agent logic with tests.

title: HTTP Client 2.0: simplify architecture, streaming, docs/examples overhaul
description: Prepare packages/http-client for v2.0 by critically reviewing architecture, abstractions, and real usage. Keep the package for controlled streaming in LLM environments (including stream caching), but remove overdesign and cruft from 1.0. Scope includes: (1) simplify core API and internal layering, (2) align implementation with actual monorepo usage and examples, (3) remove impractical/nonsensical examples and replace with concise practical ones, (4) rebuild docs into dense, Laravel-style guidance focused on core workflows, (5) identify high-value breaking/non-breaking migration tasks with clear acceptance criteria.
close_reason: Completed v2 http-client review epic scope: immutable client/request/middleware transitions, corrected pending sync/stream semantics, fixed pool driver/config resolution, hardened streamed record/replay correctness, consolidated SSE stack around EventSource pipeline, pruned/deprecated low-value API surface, rewrote docs/examples to concise core workflows, and added custom pool-handler extensibility for deterministic custom/mock pooling.

title: Make HttpClient state transitions truly immutable (client/request/middleware)
description: Current API advertises immutable fluent behavior, but mutations leak: HttpRequest::withHeader()/withStreaming() mutate in place, MiddlewareStack mutates in append/prepend/remove, and HttpClient::withMiddleware() can mutate the original instance. This creates hidden side effects across requests and makes chaining semantics unreliable.
acceptance_criteria: 1) HttpRequest uses immutable with* semantics (no in-place mutations). 2) MiddlewareStack and HttpClient with* methods do not mutate prior instances. 3) Add regression tests proving original client/request remain unchanged after fluent operations. 4) Update docs to clearly state immutable behavior.
close_reason: Implemented immutable with* semantics for HttpRequest and MiddlewareStack, ensured HttpClient middleware transitions no longer mutate prior instances, added dedicated regression tests for request/stack/client immutability, and updated http-client CHEATSHEET immutability guidance.

title: Redesign PendingHttpResponse sync vs stream execution semantics
description: PendingHttpResponse caches one response instance regardless of call path. Calling get() then stream() or stream() then content()/body() yields inconsistent behavior because stream() mutates request mode and cache is reused. This is a correctness bug and an unclear API contract.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Define explicit contract for pending execution modes (sync/stream) and enforce it. 2) Remove hidden request mutation from PendingHttpResponse::stream(). 3) Add tests for get->stream, stream->get/content, and repeated calls. 4) Ensure error messages guide users to correct usage.
close_reason: Redesigned PendingHttpResponse with explicit independent sync/stream caches, removed hidden in-place request mutation by cloning mode-specific request snapshots, and added feature tests for get->stream, stream->content, and repeated access behavior.

title: Fix pool execution to use actual client driver/config instead of implicit default
description: HttpClient::pool()/withPool() currently build pool handlers with getConfigFromDriver() returning null, which falls back to default config/driver. Pool execution can silently use the wrong backend and ignore custom driver/preset configuration.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Pool execution uses the same resolved driver family/config as the client instance. 2) Unsupported pools (e.g. mock/custom without pool support) fail explicitly with actionable error. 3) Add tests proving preset-specific pool selection and no silent fallback to default driver. 4) Remove placeholder getConfigFromDriver() behavior.
close_reason: Implemented pool handler resolution from active driver, propagated HttpClientConfig via builder, and added regression tests for driver-specific pool selection + explicit failure for non-pooling external drivers.

title: Consolidate duplicate SSE middleware stacks into one coherent streaming API
description: There are two overlapping SSE pipelines (Middleware/EventSource/* and Middleware/ServerSideEvents/*), both parsing SSE blocks with different extension points. Debug stack wires EventSourceMiddleware, while HttpClient::withSSEStream() wires StreamSSEsMiddleware. This duplication increases maintenance cost and confuses users.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Choose one SSE middleware path and deprecate/remove the duplicate. 2) Preserve required capabilities (parser hook, listener hooks, debug events). 3) Update HttpClientBuilder debug wiring and HttpClient::withSSEStream() to the unified implementation. 4) Add migration notes and coverage for SSE parsing behavior.
close_reason: Consolidated SSE handling around EventSource pipeline: EventSourceStream now supports optional payload parser mode, StreamSSEsMiddleware and ServerSideEventResponseDecorator delegate to EventSourceResponseDecorator, and HttpClient::withSSEStream now composes EventSourceMiddleware with parser. Added feature tests covering parity, raw mode preservation, and withSSEStream composition.

title: Make RecordReplay streaming cache path production-safe (or remove from core)
description: Record/replay is positioned as a key feature but currently has correctness issues in streamed records (e.g. StreamedRequestRecord::getResponseHeaders() drops headers and contains placeholder 'simplified implementation' paths). This makes stream replay unreliable for real LLM integrations.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Fix streamed record serialization/deserialization to preserve status, headers, body, and chunk boundaries. 2) Remove placeholder/simplified code paths in StreamedRequestRecord. 3) Add end-to-end tests covering record+replay for streamed responses with headers. 4) If feature is out of scope for core v2, explicitly move it to an extension package and document migration.
close_reason: Fixed streamed record correctness by removing placeholder StreamedRequestRecord code paths, preserving request/response metadata and chunk data in serialization, and ensuring RecordingMiddleware returns replayable streamed responses after recording (no stream exhaustion for callers). Added end-to-end streaming record+replay feature tests asserting status, headers, body, and chunk boundaries.

title: Rewrite http-client docs/examples around core workflows and remove stale API surface
description: Current http-client docs are oversized and inconsistent with runtime code (nonexistent middleware/classes, outdated constructors, stale driver names, mismatched debug config shape). HTTP examples are duplicated (root + hub), include impractical network-dependent cases, and overemphasize niche flows over daily usage.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Replace docs with concise Laravel-style guides focused on core workflows: build client, request/response, streaming, retries/debug, custom driver for tests. 2) Remove or rewrite stale references to nonexistent APIs/classes. 3) Replace C01_Http examples with clean, deterministic examples (mock-first; no external network). 4) Establish single source of truth for examples consumed by hub docs to prevent drift.
close_reason: Replaced http-client docs with concise core-workflow guides (overview, getting started, requests, responses, streaming, pooling, middleware), archived overblown advanced pages to remove stale API references, converted C01_Http examples to deterministic mock/in-memory patterns, and added sync script + policy docs to make examples/C01_Http the source of truth for hub copies.

title: Prune dead API surface and package-scope cruft in http-client
description: The package contains unused or low-value artifacts that increase cognitive load (e.g. unused factories/classes, near-empty README, legacy aliases/methods, and broad config payload not required by http-client runtime). v2 should intentionally keep only high-value public surface.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Inventory public symbols and classify: keep, deprecate, remove. 2) Remove or deprecate confirmed-unused classes/methods with migration notes. 3) Keep package config limited to groups actually consumed by http-client runtime. 4) Add CI/static checks (or tests) to prevent reintroducing unused public cruft.
close_reason: Completed v2 pruning inventory and migration guidance in V2_API_SURFACE.md, deprecated confirmed low-value compatibility aliases/wrappers (builder alias methods, withSSEStream alias, ServerSideEvents stack wrappers, StreamedRequestRecord alias), and added ApiSurfacePruningTest guard checks to keep deprecated surface explicit and prevent new core references to deprecated namespace.

title: Define v2 minimal core and move niche drivers/middleware to optional modules
description: Monorepo usage is concentrated on HttpClientBuilder + basic request/stream + mock driver, while several advanced integrations are effectively unused (e.g. EventSource stack, RecordReplay, Idempotency, CircuitBreaker, ext-http). Keeping everything in core increases maintenance and docs complexity.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Define v2 core feature set based on actual usage. 2) Mark niche features as optional modules/extensions or deprecate/remove. 3) Keep first-class support for critical paths: default driver, streaming, mock/testing. 4) Publish migration matrix for moved/removed components.
close_reason: Defined v2 minimal core and migration matrix in V2_CORE_SCOPE.md, explicitly separated compatibility layer vs core path, and marked niche ext-http driver/pool as optionalization candidates via deprecation annotations. Core first-class paths (default drivers, streaming, mock/testing, pooling) remain intact and covered by feature/unit tests.

title: Simplify Extras and deprecate inference mixins
description:
Outcomes:
- Scalar/Sequence/Maybe are cohesive classes with fewer traits and explicit dependencies.
- Sequence uses a collection class instead of raw arrays.
- HandlesInference and HandlesSelfInference are deprecated with migration guidance.

Acceptance criteria:
- Traits are reduced or folded; internal instantiation of SchemaFactory/SymfonyDeserializer is removed where DI is expected.
- Sequence list storage is a collection class, not a raw array.
- HandlesInference/HandlesSelfInference are marked deprecated and replacement usage is documented.
- Extras-related tests updated.
close_reason: Completed Extras simplification: Scalar/Sequence trait logic folded into classes, Sequence now stores items in ArrayList collection, Maybe/Sequence dependency points made explicit in constructors, mixin traits/interfaces marked deprecated with migration guidance in upgrade docs/examples, and instructor test suite passes (364 tests).

title: Add unit tests for AfterToolMiddleware
description:
Test cases:
- Callback can modify AgentExecution result
- Callback returning AgentExecution replaces result
- Callback returning non-AgentExecution keeps original
- Matcher returning false skips callback
- Original execution is called before callback
- Callback receives correct arguments (execution, call, state)
close_reason: Created AfterToolMiddlewareTest with 7 test cases - all passing

title: Investigate OpenCodeBasic example - runs too fast (0.11s), likely silent failure
description: Example [10] OpenCodeBasic completes in 0.11 seconds which is abnormally fast for an LLM API call. Likely a silent failure or muted error messages. Check examples/B05_LLMExtras/OpenCodeBasic/run.php
close_reason: Examples working correctly. Recent commits added proper CLI detection (ProcUtils::findOnPath). Fast execution times occur when CLI not installed, now properly showing ERROR status.

title: Events 2.0 refactor: explicit event wiring and single convention
description:
Goals
- Deliver a 2.0 event-system refactor that removes implicit/magic event injection and standardizes event wiring across the monorepo.
- Establish one explicit convention for event bus type and listener registration APIs.
- Improve maintainability by eliminating hidden resolver behavior and trait-based auto-wiring side effects.

Context
- Current implementation relies on optional event dependencies and resolver indirection (`EventBusResolver::using/default`) across many constructors and builders.
- Runtime behavior can silently diverge when classes instantiate their own local event bus.
- Framework logging integrations currently auto-attach via trait discovery, which is non-transparent and hard to reason about.

Expected Outcomes
- Event wiring is explicit and deterministic: non-root classes receive an already-constructed event bus instance.
- One convention is used codebase-wide for event bus dependencies and listener registration.
- Resolver-based magic is removed from runtime paths and framework logging auto-wiring is explicit.
- Tests/docs/CI checks enforce the new model and prevent regressions.

Scope
- In scope: packages/events, addons, agents, agent-ctrl, polyglot, instructor, http-client, logging integrations, plus supporting tests/docs/CI checks.
- In scope: codebase cleanup checks, including resolver removal when approved by the final design decision.
- Out of scope: redesign of event payload domain models.
acceptance_criteria:
- Single event-bus convention is documented and implemented across the codebase (final decision recorded in research).
- Non-root constructors no longer use nullable/union event bus signatures (e.g. `?CanHandleEvents`, `null|CanHandleEvents|EventDispatcherInterface`).
- Resolver-based wiring is removed from runtime paths; if resolver removal is approved, `packages/events/src/EventBusResolver.php` is deleted and all usages are removed.
- Framework logging integrations no longer auto-wire by trait discovery; wiring is explicit to a known event bus service.
- Completeness checks are added and passing (grep/linters/tests/static analysis), including explicit checks for removed resolver usage and stale APIs.
close_reason: All Events 2.0 child tasks completed: wiring migration, integrations, checks, and docs

title: Events 2.0: lock API decision and invariants
description:
Goals
- Confirm and lock the single event-bus convention for 2.0 and publish a canonical contract for event registration/listener semantics.

Context
- Refactor touches multiple packages and public APIs; work must be guided by one approved contract.

Expected Outcomes
- Canonical design decision is approved and referenced by implementation tasks.
- Invariants for listener APIs (`addListener`, `wiretap`, facade `onEvent`) are documented and testable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Approved design states one convention for event bus type and listener registration APIs.
- Listener semantics for addListener/wiretap/onEvent are documented with explicit guarantees.
- Decision artifact is linked from epic and implementation tasks.
close_reason: Decision and invariants documented in research/better-events/* with explicit convention and listener semantics

title: Events package core: remove resolver indirection and simplify wiring
description:
Goals
- Simplify packages/events core by removing resolver indirection and making event bus wiring explicit.

Context
- Resolver and helper trait currently hide behavior and permit inconsistent wiring.

Expected Outcomes
- Core events package has explicit constructor/wiring semantics.
- Tests and package docs reflect final 2.0 behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- Runtime wiring no longer depends on EventBusResolver in packages/events.
- If resolver removal is approved, `packages/events/src/EventBusResolver.php` is deleted and tests/docs adjusted accordingly.
- `packages/events` tests pass with updated semantics and no regression in listener ordering/wildcard behavior.
close_reason: Removed EventBusResolver from packages/events, updated HandlesEvents to explicit CanHandleEvents injection, cleaned resolver docs/tests, and verified packages/events tests

title: Composition roots/runtimes: harden event bus constructor contracts
description:
Goals
- Enforce explicit event bus injection in runtime/composition-root APIs where graph construction happens.

Context
- Current runtimes/builders accept nullable and union event signatures and resolve internally.

Expected Outcomes
- Runtime/factory signatures are hardened.
- Object graph consistently shares one bus instance from composition root.
acceptance_criteria:
- Target runtime/builder APIs use explicit `CanHandleEvents` in non-root paths.
- Nullable/union event signatures are removed from scoped runtime/factory internals.
- Tests confirm shared bus identity through constructed runtime graphs.
close_reason: Hardened composition/runtime contracts: removed resolver/union internals in InferenceRuntime, EmbeddingsRuntime, StructuredOutputRuntime, HttpClientBuilder, HttpClient, InferenceDriverFactory; added shared-bus identity regression tests across polyglot/instructor/http-client; verified with targeted Pest suite.

title: Addons migration: explicit event injection and no resolver fallbacks
description:
Goals
- Remove resolver/optionality patterns from addons package event wiring.

Context
- Addons contains many `$events ?? EventBusResolver::using($events)` patterns and mutable paths that can fork buses.

Expected Outcomes
- Addons constructors/mutators use explicit event bus semantics.
- Behavior remains functionally equivalent with clearer dependencies.
acceptance_criteria:
- Addons source no longer uses resolver fallback patterns for event wiring in non-root classes.
- Mutator/with-method paths preserve existing bus by default and never create implicit replacement buses.
- Addons tests pass with event behavior parity.
close_reason: Removed EventBusResolver/fallback wiring across addons source (chat, collaboration, tool-use, processors, participants/collaborators), preserved existing bus on mutator paths, updated resolver-based tests to explicit EventDispatcher, and verified with addons suite: 163 passed, 1 skipped.

title: Agents + AgentCtrl migration: explicit event bus injection
description:
Goals
- Migrate agents and agent-ctrl event wiring to explicit injection and remove hidden fallback patterns.

Context
- Agents stack includes drivers/hooks/tools with optional event dependencies and helper indirection.

Expected Outcomes
- Agents and agent-ctrl rely on explicit event bus dependencies in non-root classes.
- Event-related tests updated for new contracts.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agents and agent-ctrl non-root classes use explicit event bus dependencies.
- Hidden/optional resolver patterns are removed from scoped classes.
- Agents/agent-ctrl tests pass and event emission behavior remains covered.
close_reason: Migrated agents + agent-ctrl off resolver patterns: removed EventBusResolver from drivers/hooks/subagent tools and bridge builder, switched to explicit/preserved CanHandleEvents wiring, and updated unit tests to explicit EventDispatcher assumptions. Verification: agents + agent-ctrl suites passed (502 tests).

title: Polyglot + Instructor + HttpClient migration: explicit event wiring
description:
Goals
- Migrate polyglot, instructor, and http-client packages to explicit event bus injection.

Context
- These packages contain central runtime/builder flows and broad call-site impact.

Expected Outcomes
- Non-root constructors no longer resolve optional event buses internally.
- Runtime trees are wired from explicit composition roots.
acceptance_criteria:
- Polyglot, instructor, and http-client scoped classes remove resolver-based implicit event construction in non-root paths.
- Constructor signatures and factory wiring align with the approved event convention.
- Package tests pass and core event-driven regressions are covered.
close_reason: Migrated polyglot/instructor/http-client scoped wiring to explicit CanHandleEvents flow: removed remaining resolver usage in scope (incl. instructor HandlesInference), aligned runtime/factory wiring and shared-bus propagation, and added runtime graph identity regressions. Verification: scoped pest sweep passed 850 tests; 2 http-client integration-server startup failures are environment-related in sandbox (IntegrationTestServer port bind).

title: Logging integrations: remove trait-discovery auto-wiring magic
description:
Goals
- Remove trait-discovery-based automatic logging wiring in Laravel/Symfony integrations.

Context
- Current integrations attach wiretaps by scanning for trait usage, creating hidden side effects.

Expected Outcomes
- Logging pipeline hookup is explicit and deterministic.
- Integration behavior is covered by focused tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- Symfony/Laravel logging integrations no longer discover event-capable services by trait scanning for auto-wiretap attachment.
- Logging pipeline attachment is explicit to a known bus service/path.
- Integration tests verify expected logging hookup behavior.
close_reason: Completed explicit event-bus logging integration wiring and tests

title: Completeness checks + cleanup sweep for Events 2.0 refactor
description:
Goals
- Add enforcement checks and perform final cleanup/completeness sweep for the event refactor.

Context
- Refactor spans many packages and can regress without hard automated checks.

Expected Outcomes
- CI/static checks fail if old patterns reappear.
- Final cleanup confirms no stale resolver/optionality/trait-magic remains in scoped areas.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add explicit grep/static checks for prohibited legacy patterns (resolver usage, nullable/union event signatures in scoped non-root APIs, trait-discovery magic in logging integrations).
- Final sweep confirms all scoped cleanup tasks complete, including resolver class removal if in-scope decision is approved.
- Quality gates pass (tests + static analysis + check scripts).
close_reason: Added Events 2.0 completeness checks, removed remaining resolver usages, and passed scoped quality gates

title: Docs/examples migration guide for Events 2.0
description:
Goals
- Publish user-facing and contributor-facing migration guidance for Events 2.0.

Context
- Constructor signature changes and wiring model updates require clear migration steps.

Expected Outcomes
- Docs/examples/migration notes are aligned with final implementation.
- No stale guidance references resolver-based magic.
acceptance_criteria:
- Migration docs cover constructor/API changes and explicit wiring model.
- Examples use the approved event convention and no stale resolver guidance remains.
- Package docs reference final listener APIs and integration patterns.
close_reason: Published Events 2.0 migration docs and updated package docs/examples to explicit event wiring conventions

title: [stream] Honor Iterator rewind contract to prevent silent data loss
description:
Problem
The stream engine assumes iterators are already positioned. For iterators that only become valid after rewind(), transformations and Tee can silently produce empty output instead of consuming data.

Context
- packages/stream/src/TransformationExecution.php:27 initializes exhaustion from iterator->valid() without rewind
- packages/stream/src/TeeState.php:68-73 checks source->valid() without rewind
- IteratorUtils::toIterator() returns Iterator instances as-is (packages/stream/src/Iterator/IteratorUtils.php:15-16)
- Repro: custom Iterator where valid() is false until rewind() -> Transformation::define()->withInput()->execute() returns []; Tee::split() yields empty branches

Expected outcomes
- Input iterators are initialized in a way consistent with PHP iteration semantics before first valid/current checks.
- No silent empty-output behavior for valid custom Iterator implementations.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests with a custom Iterator that requires rewind() before valid() becomes true, covering both:
  1) Transformation execution path
  2) Tee::split path
- Both paths produce the expected values instead of empty results.
- No behavioral regression for generators/ArrayIterator existing tests.
close_reason: Fixed: added regression coverage for iterators requiring rewind and updated stream initialization (TransformationExecution + TeeState) to safely prepare iterator position without breaking generator on-demand consumption semantics.

title: Refactor body formatters to use ToolDefinitions and ToolChoice
description:
Update all body formatters to consume typed ToolDefinitions and ToolChoice from InferenceRequest instead of raw arrays.

## Files to modify
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/OpenAI/OpenAIBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/Anthropic/AnthropicBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/Gemini/GeminiBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/OpenResponses/OpenResponsesBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/Mistral/MistralBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/Deepseek/DeepseekBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/Perplexity/PerplexityBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/GeminiOAI/GeminiOAIBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/Minimaxi/MinimaxiBodyFormat.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/CohereV2/CohereV2BodyFormat.php

## Key simplifications per formatter

### OpenAI
- toTools(): $request->tools()->all() then $tool->name(), $tool->parameters()
- toToolChoice(): switch on $request->toolChoice()->isAuto(), isSpecific(), etc.

### Anthropic
- toAnthropicTool(ToolDefinition $tool): $tool->name(), $tool->description(), $tool->parameters()
- toToolChoice(): $toolChoice->isSpecific() ? $toolChoice->functionName() : $toolChoice->mode()
- Cache marker injection iterates ToolDefinition objects

### Gemini
- toTools(): $tool->toArray()['function'] replaced by direct ['name'=>$tool->name(), 'description'=>$tool->description(), 'parameters'=>$tool->parameters()]
- toToolChoice(): $toolChoice->isSpecific() ? $toolChoice->functionName() : mapToolChoice($toolChoice->mode())

### Others
- Similar patterns — replace $tool['function']['name'] with $tool->name() etc.
- Perplexity/CohereV2 that strip tools: no functional change but types are cleaner

## Expected outcomes
- No $tool['function']['name'] or $toolChoice['function']['name'] destructuring in any formatter
- Tool choice logic uses named boolean checks instead of string/array matching
- Each formatter's toTools()/toToolChoice() is shorter and more readable

## Acceptance criteria
1. No raw array access on tool definitions in any body formatter
2. No string|array type checks on tool choice in any body formatter  
3. All formatters use ToolDefinition accessors (name(), description(), parameters())
4. All formatters use ToolChoice query methods (isAuto(), isSpecific(), functionName(), mode())
5. Existing body format tests pass (AnthropicBodyFormatDeterministicTest, OpenResponsesBodyFormatTest, etc.)
6. Golden tests pass (InferenceOpenAI_GoldenTest, InferenceAnthropic_GoldenTest, InferenceGemini_GoldenTest)
close_reason: Completed: body formatters now consume ToolDefinitions and ToolChoice via typed accessors, no raw tool definition parsing remains in body formatters, and OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini golden tests pass.

title: http-client: dump() used in PrintToConsole::printBody() is not production-safe
description:
File: packages/http-client/src/Middleware/EventSource/Listeners/PrintToConsole.php:22

Problem:
  protected function printBody(string $body): void {
      dump(json_decode($body));
  }

dump() is a Symfony VarDumper debug helper. It is used here despite a @noinspection suppression acknowledging this. Two concerns:
  1. dump() requires symfony/var-dumper to be installed; if this package is used outside a Symfony context it will fail with 'Call to undefined function dump()'
  2. The rest of the class uses Console::print/println for output — printBody() is inconsistent and bypasses any output formatting/capture

The class is activated only via DebugConfig flags, so this is not hit in production by default, but it is fragile.

Expected outcome:
  printBody() uses Console::println() or a proper JSON pretty-printer consistent with the rest of the class, with no dependency on symfony/var-dumper.

Acceptance criteria:
  - dump() call removed
  - Body printed via Console::println() with formatted/indented JSON
  - No symfony/var-dumper dependency introduced or required
close_reason: Added fail-first regression around PrintToConsole body output, removed dump() usage, implemented Console::println-based JSON pretty printing with raw-body fallback, and validated SSE/streaming middleware tests.

title: Implement StepResult pattern across all StepByStep orchestrators
description:
## Overview
Introduce a StepResult wrapper for clean step+outcome bundling and apply the pattern consistently to Agent, ToolUse, Chat, and Collaboration orchestrators.

## Goals
1. Create StepResult abstraction to cleanly bundle step + continuation outcome
2. Apply pattern to Agent (refactor existing implementation)
3. Apply pattern to ToolUse
4. Apply pattern to Chat
5. Apply pattern to Collaboration
6. Remove continuation outcome from Step classes (outcome lives in StepResult)

## Success Criteria
- All four orchestrators use the same StepResult pattern
- Continuation outcomes are accessible via StepResult on each state
- All existing tests pass
- Serialization includes StepResults with outcomes
- API is consistent across all orchestrators
notes: Plan file: research/2026-01-16-agents/step-result-pattern-implementation-plan.md
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 1: Create StepResult wrapper class
description:
## Objective
Create the StepResult abstraction that bundles a step with its ContinuationOutcome.

## Files to Create
- `packages/addons/src/StepByStep/Step/StepResult.php`

## Implementation Details
- Generic readonly class that wraps any step object + ContinuationOutcome
- Provide `shouldContinue()` convenience method
- Provide `stopReason()` accessor
- Must be serialization-friendly

## Success Criteria
- [ ] StepResult class exists at specified path
- [ ] Class is readonly and immutable
- [ ] Constructor accepts step object and ContinuationOutcome
- [ ] `shouldContinue()` returns outcome's shouldContinue value
- [ ] `stopReason()` returns the stop reason from outcome
acceptance_criteria: StepResult class created and unit tested

title: Phase 2: Refactor Agent to use StepResult
description:
## Objective
Refactor Agent's existing continuation outcome implementation to use StepResult wrapper instead of storing outcome directly on AgentStep.

- `packages/addons/src/Agent/Core/Data/AgentState.php` - Store StepResults, add accessors

- `packages/addons/src/Agent/Serialization/SlimAgentStateSerializer.php` - Handle StepResult serialization

## Implementation Details
1. Remove `continuationOutcome` property from AgentStep
2. Remove `withContinuationOutcome()` method from AgentStep
3. AgentState stores StepResults (step + outcome bundled)
4. Update `performStep()` to create StepResult and pass to state
5. Update `canContinue()` to read from StepResult
6. Update serializer to handle StepResults

## Success Criteria
- [ ] AgentStep no longer has continuationOutcome property
- [ ] AgentState stores and exposes StepResults
- [ ] `performStep()` creates StepResult after evaluation
- [ ] `canContinue()` reads from last StepResult
- [ ] Serialization preserves StepResults with outcomes
- [ ] All Agent tests pass

title: Simplify ExecutionState step lifecycle (6 transitions → 3)
description:
## Scope

Simplify ExecutionState step lifecycle from 6 transitions to 3. Remove stored `$currentStepNumber` (derive it). Replace `withNewStepExecution()` with `withStepStarted()`. Replace separate record + clear with `withStepRecorded()`. Delete `withStepInProgressCleared()`. Update AgentState delegate methods.

## Files to change

State:
- `packages/agents/src/Core/Data/ExecutionState.php`:
  - Remove `$currentStepNumber` field
  - Remove `withNewStepExecution()` — replace with `withStepStarted(DateTimeImmutable $at): self` (sets currentStepStartedAt, clears pendingStopSignal + continuationRequested, initializes hookContext)
  - Remove `withStepInProgressCleared()`
  - Add `withStepRecorded(StepExecution $execution): self` — appends to stepExecutions, clears currentStepStartedAt + hookContext, preserves currentStep
  - Derive step number: `stepExecutions->count() + 1` where needed
  - Keep `withReplacedStepExecution()` only if error path requires it; remove otherwise

- `packages/agents/src/Core/Data/AgentState.php`:
  - Remove `withNewStepExecution()` → add `withStepStarted()`
  - Remove `withStepInProgressCleared()`
  - Add `withStepRecorded(StepExecution)` delegate
  - Remove `currentStepNumber()` accessor or make it derive from `stepCount() + 1`

Loop:
- `packages/agents/src/Core/AgentLoop.php`:
  - `iterate()`: replace `withNewStepExecution()` with `withStepStarted(new DateTimeImmutable())`
  - `iterate()`: remove `withStepInProgressCleared()` call (handled by `withStepRecorded()`)
  - `recordStep()`: derive stepNumber as `$state->stepCount() + 1`, use `withStepRecorded()`
  - `recordError()`: same pattern

Serialization:
- `packages/agents/src/Serialization/ContinuationAgentStateSerializer.php` — verify no references to removed fields (currently doesn't touch ExecutionState — should be fine)
- `packages/agents/src/Serialization/SlimAgentStateSerializer.php` — verify same
- `ExecutionState::toArray()` / `fromArray()` — update to exclude `currentStepNumber`, mark `currentStepStartedAt` + `hookContext` + `currentStep` as transient

## Acceptance criteria

- ExecutionState step lifecycle is 3 transitions: `withStepStarted()` → [driver] → `withStepRecorded()`
- `$currentStepNumber` field removed from ExecutionState
- `withNewStepExecution()`, `withStepInProgressCleared()` deleted
- `withStepStarted()` and `withStepRecorded()` are the only step lifecycle methods
- Step number derived correctly as `stepExecutions->count() + 1`
- Transient fields (`currentStepStartedAt`, `hookContext`, `currentStep`) excluded from serialization
- Serialization round-trip works: serialize with transient fields populated → deserialize → transient fields are null

## Validations / checks / tests

- Run full agent test suite: `vendor/bin/pest packages/agents/tests/`
- `AgentLoopStepNumberTest` — update for derived step numbers
- `ContinuationAgentStateSerializerTest` — add round-trip test with transient fields
- `SlimAgentStateSerializerTest` — add round-trip test with transient fields
- `AgentStateContinuationTest` — update for new lifecycle methods
- `AgentStateExecutionBufferTest` — update for new lifecycle methods
- Verify step numbers in events are sequential (1, 2, 3...) via wiretap test
- No references to `withNewStepExecution`, `withStepInProgressCleared`, `$currentStepNumber` remain (grep)

title: Epic: Replace Instructor partial snapshots with StructuredOutputStreamState
description:
Goal: remove Instructor's dependency on Polyglot PartialInferenceResponse by introducing an Instructor-owned mutable StructuredOutputStreamState that accumulates PartialInferenceDelta in place during streaming.

Expected outcome:
- Instructor streaming internals mutate StructuredOutputStreamState in place instead of allocating Polyglot snapshot objects
- Polyglot remains delta-only on the streaming boundary
- Instructor partial parsing, sequence updates, dedupe, and streaming events operate on Instructor-owned state
- docs, examples, CHEATSHEET content, and upgrade notes are aligned before 2.0.0

Acceptance criteria:
- No production source under packages/instructor/ depends on Cognesy\Polyglot\Inference\Data\PartialInferenceResponse
- Instructor streaming still supports partial object updates, sequence updates, tool streaming, retry handling, and final response generation
- Targeted Polyglot and Instructor tests pass after the migration
- Instructor docs/examples/CHEATSHEET.md document the new streaming state model clearly
- Upgrade notes describe the contract break from PartialInferenceResponse-based partials to StructuredOutputStreamState-based streaming state
close_reason: Epic complete. Instructor production code no longer depends on PartialInferenceResponse, streaming is delta/state-based with StructuredOutputStreamState and StructuredOutputResponse, raw inference access is explicit, event payloads are unified, tests/support/benchmarks are aligned, and upgrade/docs coverage is in place for the 2.0 contract.

- StructuredOutputStream exposes explicit final raw inference access (for example finalInferenceResponse()).
- No Instructor API uses raw InferenceResponse as the default primary response object.
- Sync and streaming tests cover both structured response access and explicit raw response access.
- Instructor docs, examples, and upgrade notes show the split clearly.
- packages/instructor/CHEATSHEET.md reflects the new access pattern.
close_reason: Completed in code. Instructor now exposes explicit raw inference access via inferenceResponse() and finalInferenceResponse() while keeping StructuredOutputResponse as the primary response surface. Public API, docs, cheatsheet, examples, and tests were updated to the renamed accessors.

title: Stage 7: Update helpers, fixtures, and benchmarks for state-based streaming
description:

Expected outcome:

- Remaining PartialInferenceResponse usage is eliminated from production code and minimized/removed from test infrastructure where appropriate.

FakeInferenceDriver and related probe helpers are delta-only, the streaming object-profile fixture was refreshed, and the full Instructor test suite passes.

description:
Stage 8 from research/better-delta/plan-part2.md.

Upgrade notes now describe the 2.0 contract clearly for Instructor and Polyglot, docs and CHEATSHEET use StructuredOutputResponse plus explicit raw inference access, and the stale partial-response example comment was removed/updated. title: Implement CallableProcessor for step hooks
description:
Create packages/addons/src/StepByStep/StateProcessing/Processors/CallableProcessor.php

Wraps a callable as a StateProcessor for step-level hooks. Features:
- Constructor takes Closure, priority, and position ('before'/'after')
- Integrates with existing StateProcessors mechanism

This provides syntactic sugar over the existing processor mechanism.

title: Investigate CodexBasic example - runs too fast (0.10s), likely silent failure
description: Example [6] CodexBasic completes in 0.10 seconds which is abnormally fast for an LLM API call. Likely a silent failure or muted error messages. Check examples/B05_LLMExtras/CodexBasic/run.php

title: Avoid duplicate completion events for streamed inferences
description:
Outcomes:
- Streaming inferences dispatch completion/usage/attempt-success events exactly once.

Context:
- Problem: InferenceStream::finalizeStream dispatches InferenceAttemptSucceeded/InferenceUsageReported/InferenceCompleted, and PendingInference::response dispatches the same events after stream()->final(), causing duplicates for streamed requests.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Streaming/InferenceStream.php (dispatchStreamCompletionEvents) and packages/polyglot/src/Inference/PendingInference.php (handleAttemptSuccess/dispatchInferenceCompleted).
- Suggested fix: make PendingInference the single owner of completion events for both streaming and non-streaming; gate or remove stream completion dispatches (keep InferenceResponseCreated and partial events in InferenceStream).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Streamed requests emit InferenceAttemptSucceeded, InferenceUsageReported, and InferenceCompleted exactly once.
- Non-streamed requests remain unchanged.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test with a stub streamed driver and a fake event dispatcher that asserts each completion event fires once when calling PendingInference::response() on a streamed request.
close_reason: Remove stream-level completion events to avoid duplicates and add Pest coverage for single completion events

title: Fix PHPStan Laravel/Illuminate integration errors
description: Fix ~80 PHPStan errors related to missing Laravel/Illuminate classes and functions in Beads migrations and service providers

title: Refactor makeInferenceDriver to remove duplication
description:
Outcomes:

Context:
- Problem: makeInferenceDriver repeats factory->makeDriver inside and outside the explicit-driver branch.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Inference.php (makeInferenceDriver).
- Suggested fix: compute explicit driver once and fall back to factory call in a single return path.

Acceptance Criteria:

How to Test:

close_reason: Simplify makeInferenceDriver to compute explicit driver once and fall back to factory

title: [polyglot] Fix embeddings DSN preset and config override contract mismatches
description:
Problem and context:
Embeddings configuration paths have two contract breaks that make DSN/preset and override flows unreliable.

Repro A (DSN preset path):
EmbeddingsProvider::dsn('preset=openai')->resolveConfig() throws ConfigurationException (unknown named parameter $preset).
Cause: provider merges DSN params directly into EmbeddingsConfig input instead of stripping control keys.

Repro B (override path):
(new EmbeddingsConfig(dimensions: 1536))->withOverrides(['model' => 'm']) throws ConfigurationException (unknown named parameter $defaultDimensions).
Cause: EmbeddingsConfig::toArray() emits key defaultDimensions, but constructor expects dimensions.

Code references:
- packages/polyglot/src/Embeddings/EmbeddingsProvider.php:159-164
- packages/polyglot/src/Embeddings/Config/EmbeddingsConfig.php:40-43
- packages/polyglot/src/Embeddings/Config/EmbeddingsConfig.php:45-55

Expected outcomes:
- DSN-based provider flow supports preset parameter without leaking it into EmbeddingsConfig constructor payload.
- EmbeddingsConfig toArray/withOverrides round-trip is self-consistent.
- Embeddings configuration APIs behave predictably for user-provided DSN and override flows.
acceptance_criteria: 1) EmbeddingsProvider::dsn("preset=openai")->resolveConfig() succeeds. 2) EmbeddingsConfig withOverrides works when dimensions is already set. 3) Regression tests cover both failure reproductions. 4) Existing embeddings unit/feature tests stay green.
close_reason: Fixed embeddings config contract mismatches: DSN overrides now strip control key preset before EmbeddingsConfig hydration; EmbeddingsConfig toArray/withOverrides now use dimensions consistently while fromArray supports legacy defaultDimensions. Added regression tests for both DSN preset and withOverrides paths.

title: Fix InvalidCast error in ExtHttpResponseAdapter
description: Cannot cast http\Header to int for Content-Length header

title: Handle JsonException in ResponseExtractor tool-call encoding
description:
Outcomes:
- ResponseExtractor converts tool-call JSON encoding errors into Result::failure instead of throwing.

Context:
- Problem: getToolCallContent uses json_encode(JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR), so JsonException bypasses Result handling and retry logic.
- Location: packages/instructor/src/Extraction/ResponseExtractor.php (getToolCallContent).
- Suggested fix: wrap json_encode in try/catch (or use Result::try around getContentString) and return Result::failure on JsonException.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Extraction for tool-call content never throws JsonException; failures are reported via Result::failure.
- Retry logic can proceed on encoding failures.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test that builds an InferenceResponse with tool-call args containing invalid UTF-8 and asserts extract() returns Result::failure (no exception).
close_reason: Return Result::failure on tool-call JSON encode errors; add UTF-8 failure test

title: Add Pi coding agent bridge to AgentCtrl
description: Implement full Pi (pi-mono) CLI agent support in agent-ctrl package. Pi is a minimal terminal coding harness (npm: @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent, binary: pi) that outputs JSONL events via --mode json. Integration follows the existing bridge pattern (OpenCode/Codex/ClaudeCode). Pi-specific features: thinking levels (off..xhigh), provider/model:thinking shorthand, TypeScript extensions, @file arguments, skills, granular JSONL events (agent_start/end, turn_start/end, message_start/update/end, tool_execution_start/update/end). Reference: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/tree/main/packages/coding-agent
close_reason: Closed

title: Pi domain layer: enums, value objects, stream event DTOs
description:
## Context
First foundational layer for Pi integration. All other Pi components depend on these types. Follow the pattern established by OpenCode/Domain/ and ClaudeCode/Domain/.

## Scope
Create `src/Pi/Domain/` with:

### Enums
- `OutputMode` — values: `json`, `rpc` (maps to `--mode json` / `--mode rpc`)
- `ThinkingLevel` — string-backed enum: `off`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh` (maps to `--thinking`)

### Value Objects
- `PiSessionId` — wraps session UUID from Pi JSONL header event. Must implement `__toString()` and `fromString()`. Follow `OpenCodeSessionId` pattern.

### Stream Event DTOs (`src/Pi/Domain/Dto/StreamEvent/`)
Map Pi's JSONL event types to typed readonly classes:
- `StreamEvent` (abstract base) — `fromArray()` factory dispatching on `type` field
- `SessionEvent` — header: `{type:"session", version, id, cwd, timestamp}`
- `AgentStartEvent` — `{type:"agent_start"}`
- `AgentEndEvent` — `{type:"agent_end", messages:[...]}`
- `TurnStartEvent` — `{type:"turn_start"}`
- `TurnEndEvent` — `{type:"turn_end", message, toolResults}`
- `MessageStartEvent` — `{type:"message_start", message:{role, content}}`
- `MessageUpdateEvent` — `{type:"message_update", message, assistantMessageEvent:{type:"text_delta", delta}}`
- `MessageEndEvent` — `{type:"message_end", message}`
- `ToolExecutionStartEvent` — `{type:"tool_execution_start", toolCallId, toolName, args}`
- `ToolExecutionEndEvent` — `{type:"tool_execution_end", toolCallId, toolName, result, isError}`
- `ErrorEvent` — error data
- `UnknownEvent` — fallback for unrecognized types

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All enums are string-backed with values matching Pi CLI flags exactly
- [ ] PiSessionId follows OpenCodeSessionId pattern (fromString, toString, equals)
- [ ] StreamEvent::fromArray() correctly dispatches all Pi event types
- [ ] MessageUpdateEvent exposes `textDelta()` accessor for the nested delta string
- [ ] ToolExecutionEndEvent exposes `toolCallId`, `toolName`, `result`, `isError`
- [ ] All DTOs are `readonly` classes
- [ ] Unit tests cover fromArray() for each event type with sample Pi JSONL payloads

title: Pi request DTO and command builder
description:
## Context
Translates configuration into CLI argv for the `pi` binary. Follow the pattern of `OpenCodeCommandBuilder` and `OpenCodeRequest`.

## Scope

### PiRequest (`src/Pi/Application/Dto/PiRequest.php`)
Readonly DTO holding all configuration for a single Pi execution:
- `prompt` (string, required)
- `outputMode` (OutputMode, default: json)
- `model` (?string) — supports `provider/model` and `provider/model:thinking` shorthand
- `provider` (?string) — explicit provider override
- `thinkingLevel` (?ThinkingLevel)
- `systemPrompt` (?string)
- `appendSystemPrompt` (?string)
- `tools` (?array) — list of tool names to enable
- `noTools` (bool)
- `files` (?array) — file paths to attach as @-prefixed args
- `extensions` (?array) — extension paths/sources
- `noExtensions` (bool)
- `skills` (?array) — skill paths
- `noSkills` (bool)
- `apiKey` (?string)
- `continueSession` (bool)
- `sessionId` (?PiSessionId|string)
- `noSession` (bool)
- `sessionDir` (?string)
- `verbose` (bool)

### PiCommandBuilder (`src/Pi/Application/Builder/PiCommandBuilder.php`)
Builds `CommandSpec` from `PiRequest`:
- Base argv: `['pi']` (no subcommand needed for headless json mode)
- `--mode json` always appended
- Maps each PiRequest field to corresponding CLI flag
- @-prefixed files go before the prompt positional argument
- Prompt is the final positional argument
- Uses `BuildsCliArgv` trait
- Validates: non-empty prompt, no conflicting session flags, valid file paths

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `PiCommandBuilder::build(PiRequest)` returns correct `CommandSpec`
- [ ] `--mode json` is always present in argv
- [ ] Model string with embedded thinking (e.g. `sonnet:high`) is passed as-is to `--model`
- [ ] `--provider` only added when explicitly set (not extracted from model string)
- [ ] `--thinking` only added when ThinkingLevel is explicitly set
- [ ] Files are prefixed with `@` in argv, positioned before the prompt
- [ ] `--tools` joins array with commas: `--tools read,bash,edit`
- [ ] Mutually exclusive flags validated: `continueSession` vs `sessionId`, `noSession` vs session flags
- [ ] `CliArgumentValidator` used for model, files, sessionId
- [ ] Unit tests cover: basic prompt, all flags, combined flags, validation errors, @file ordering

title: Pi response parser
description:
## Context
Parses Pi JSONL output into a structured response. Follow the `OpenCode/Application/Parser/ResponseParser` pattern.

## Scope

### PiResponse (`src/Pi/Application/Dto/PiResponse.php`)
Wraps parsed Pi output:
- `result` (ExecResult) — raw process result
- `decoded` (DecodedObjectCollection) — all parsed JSON objects
- `sessionId` (?string) — extracted from `session` header event
- `messageText` (string) — accumulated text from message_update deltas
- `usage` (?TokenUsage) — from agent_end message usage data
- `cost` (?float) — if available in events
- `exitCode()` — delegates to ExecResult
- `parseFailures` / `parseFailureSamples` — malformed line tracking

### ResponseParser (`src/Pi/Application/Parser/ResponseParser.php`)
- `parse(ExecResult, OutputMode): PiResponse`
- JSON mode: splits stdout into lines, decodes each as JSON
  - Extracts session ID from first `session` event
  - Accumulates text from `message_update` events where `assistantMessageEvent.type == "text_delta"` using the `delta` field
  - Extracts tool calls from `tool_execution_end` events
  - Extracts usage from `agent_end` event messages (if present)
- Text mode (fallback): wraps raw stdout as messageText
- Tracks parse failures with samples (max 3), respects `failFast` flag

### TokenUsage mapping
- Add `TokenUsage::fromPi(?array)` factory in main `Dto/TokenUsage.php`
- Pi usage data structure TBD from actual output; start with input/output/cache fields

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parser correctly extracts session ID from `session` header event
- [ ] Text accumulation uses `assistantMessageEvent.delta` from `message_update` events only
- [ ] Tool calls extracted from `tool_execution_end` with correct `toolCallId`, `toolName`, `result`, `isError`
- [ ] Parse failures tracked gracefully (non-failFast mode skips bad lines)
- [ ] failFast mode throws `JsonParsingException` on first bad line
- [ ] Text-mode fallback returns raw stdout
- [ ] Unit tests with realistic Pi JSONL fixtures covering: clean run, tool calls, errors, malformed lines

title: Pi bridge and builder
description:
## Context
Core integration layer: the Bridge executes Pi CLI and returns normalized AgentResponse; the Builder provides the fluent API. Follow OpenCodeBridge/OpenCodeBridgeBuilder patterns exactly.

## Scope

### PiBridge (`src/Bridge/PiBridge.php`)
Implements `AgentBridge` interface:
- Constructor accepts all Pi-specific config fields (model, provider, thinkingLevel, systemPrompt, appendSystemPrompt, tools, noTools, files, extensions, noExtensions, skills, noSkills, apiKey, continueSession, sessionId, noSession, sessionDir, verbose, workingDirectory, sandboxDriver, timeout, events, failFast)
- `execute(prompt)` delegates to `executeStreaming(prompt, null)`
- `executeStreaming(prompt, handler)`:
  1. Switch to working directory
  2. Guard: `CliBinaryGuard::assertAvailableForDriver('pi', AgentType::Pi, sandboxDriver)`
  3. Build PiRequest → PiCommandBuilder::build() → CommandSpec
  4. Execute via `SandboxCommandExecutor::forPi()`
  5. Stream: process JSONL via JsonLinesBuffer, dispatch to StreamHandler callbacks
  6. Parse: ResponseParser extracts text, tool calls, session, usage
  7. Return normalized AgentResponse with AgentType::Pi

Streaming callback mapping:
- `message_update` with `text_delta` → `handler->onText(delta)`
- `tool_execution_end` → `handler->onToolUse(ToolCall)`
- Error events → `handler->onError(StreamError)`

Event dispatching: emit all infrastructure events (RequestBuilt, CommandSpecCreated, ProcessExecutionStarted, StreamChunkProcessed, etc.) matching OpenCodeBridge pattern.

### PiBridgeBuilder (`src/Builder/PiBridgeBuilder.php`)
Extends `AbstractBridgeBuilder`:

Common (inherited): withModel, withTimeout, inDirectory, withSandboxDriver, withConfig, onText, onToolUse, onComplete, onError, execute, executeStreaming, build

Pi-specific methods:
- `withProvider(string): static`
- `withThinking(ThinkingLevel): static`
- `withSystemPrompt(string): static`
- `appendSystemPrompt(string): static`
- `withTools(array): static` — e.g. `['read', 'bash', 'edit']`
- `noTools(): static`
- `withFiles(array): static` — file paths for @-args
- `withExtensions(array): static`
- `noExtensions(): static`
- `withSkills(array): static`
- `noSkills(): static`
- `withApiKey(string): static`
- `ephemeral(): static` — sets noSession=true
- `withSessionDir(string): static`
- `verbose(): static`
- `continueSession(): static`
- `resumeSession(string): static`
- `agentType(): AgentType` → returns `AgentType::Pi`
- `build(): AgentBridge` → constructs PiBridge with all fields

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PiBridge implements AgentBridge interface
- [ ] execute() returns AgentResponse with agentType=Pi
- [ ] Streaming callbacks fire for text deltas and tool completions
- [ ] Session ID extracted and returned via AgentResponse::sessionId()
- [ ] Token usage and cost populated when available
- [ ] Tool calls normalized to ToolCall DTOs with correct fields
- [ ] Working directory switch/restore in try/finally
- [ ] All infrastructure events dispatched
- [ ] PiBridgeBuilder exposes all Pi-specific options as fluent methods
- [ ] build() passes all config to PiBridge constructor

title: Register Pi agent in AgentCtrl facade and shared infrastructure
description:
## Context

### TokenUsage

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `AgentCtrl::pi()` returns `PiBridgeBuilder`
- [ ] `AgentCtrl::make(AgentType::Pi)` returns `PiBridgeBuilder`
- [ ] `AgentType::from('pi')` resolves correctly
- [ ] `SandboxCommandExecutor::forPi()` creates executor with 'pi' binary
- [ ] Full round-trip works: `AgentCtrl::pi()->withModel('sonnet')->execute('hello')` builds correct command
- [ ] No regressions on existing agent types (run full test suite)

title: Pi bridge unit tests
description:
## Context
Each agent has a test suite under tests/Unit/{Agent}/. Pi needs equivalent coverage.

## Scope

### Test directory: `tests/Unit/Pi/`

#### CommandBuilder tests
- Basic prompt generates correct argv: `['pi', '--mode', 'json', 'prompt text']`
- Model flag: `--model sonnet:high`
- Provider flag: `--provider anthropic`
- Thinking flag: `--thinking high`
- System prompt: `--system-prompt "..."`
- Append system prompt: `--append-system-prompt "..."`
- Tools: `--tools read,bash,edit`

- Files: `@file1.ts @file2.ts` before prompt
- Extensions: `-e ./ext1.ts -e ./ext2.ts`
- No extensions: `--no-extensions`
- Skills: `--skill /path/to/skill`

#### ResponseParser tests
- Parse clean JSONL with session header, message events, agent_end
- Extract session ID from session event
- Accumulate text from message_update deltas
- Extract tool calls from tool_execution_end events
- Handle parse failures gracefully (non-failFast)
- Throw on parse failures (failFast)
- Text-mode fallback

#### StreamEvent tests

- Each event type correctly extracts its fields
- Unknown types produce UnknownEvent

#### Builder tests

- `build()` passes all config to PiBridge constructor

### Test fixtures

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Pi bridge integration smoke test
description:
## Context
Verify the full round-trip works against a real Pi CLI installation. This is a manual/CI-optional test that requires `pi` to be installed and an API key configured.

## Scope

### Test file: `tests/Integration/PiBridgeTest.php`

Smoke tests (skipped if `pi` binary not available):
- Basic execute: send simple prompt, verify AgentResponse has text and exitCode=0
- Streaming: verify onText callback fires with text content
- Tool use: prompt that triggers bash tool, verify tool call appears in response
- Session: execute, get sessionId, resume with sessionId, verify continuation works
- Model override: specify explicit model, verify command builds correctly
- Read-only mode: `withTools(['read', 'grep', 'find', 'ls'])`, verify no write tools available

### Skip conditions
- Skip all if `pi` binary not found in PATH
- Skip all if no API key configured (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or similar)
- Tag with `@group integration` for selective CI runs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully when pi not installed
- [ ] At least one test verifies successful round-trip with real Pi CLI
- [ ] Streaming test confirms callbacks fire
- [ ] Tests tagged for selective CI execution

title: Implement buffer/summary behavior to match intended design
description: Change processors/store handling so buffer overflow prepends to summary then summarizes; keep buffer/messages raw; ensure summary updated rarely based on buffer size and used in inference order system->summary->buffer->messages. Make new tests pass.
close_reason: Adjusted SummarizeBuffer to prepend buffer content to summary before summarizing; updated test expectation and verified full test suite.

title: agents: SchemaDefinition static withDescription -> fromClass(...)->withDescription(...) (2.0 clean cut)
description:
Goal: reserve with* for instance chaining in SchemaDefinition for 2.0.0.

Changes:
- Remove static SchemaDefinition::withDescription(class, description).
- Add/keep instance method withDescription(string): self.
- Use SchemaDefinition::fromClass(class)->withDescription(description) pattern.

Acceptance:

title: [polyglot-docs] Simplify high-traffic docs to Laravel-style concise format
description:
## Problem and context
High-traffic Polyglot docs still contain long narrative sections, duplicated examples, and oversized snippets that hurt scanability and maintenance.

This is most visible in:
- `packages/polyglot/docs/essentials/overview.md`
- `packages/polyglot/docs/essentials/inference-class.md`
- `packages/polyglot/docs/advanced/custom-config.md`

Current style is inconsistent with the intended Laravel-like docs standard (simple language, short examples, minimal prose).

## Expected outcomes
- Hot-path docs become concise, task-oriented, and easy to scan.
- Each section uses short runnable examples (single purpose per snippet).
- Repetition is removed in favor of focused cross-links.

## Acceptance criteria
- Refactor the three documents above to:
  1. keep examples short (prefer <= 15 lines each),
  2. remove duplicated patterns/examples,
  3. move deep detail to advanced/internals pages with links,
  4. preserve existing doc structure where possible.
- Add a short “See also” cross-link block at the end of each rewritten page.
- Manual review confirms docs can be skimmed quickly without losing core workflows.
close_reason: Rewrote high-traffic Polyglot docs to concise Laravel-style format and re-validated API alignment. Updated: essentials/overview.md, essentials/inference-class.md, advanced/custom-config.md. Removed duplicate/long narrative, ensured short runnable snippets, corrected runtime/driver APIs, and added See also cross-links.

title: Sandbox API: replace stringly driver selection with enum-safe API
description:
Problem: Sandbox::using(string) uses raw string match and throws UnhandledMatchError for invalid values; SandboxDriver enum exists but is not used.\nContext: validated repro with invalid driver value produced runtime UnhandledMatchError.

Expected outcomes:
- Public API is explicit and type-safe (prefer SandboxDriver enum).\n- Invalid driver input yields controlled domain exception with clear message.\n- API remains ergonomic for existing call sites (migration path for 2.0).
acceptance_criteria:
- Introduce/adjust API to accept SandboxDriver (or add typed overload/factory) and eliminate unhandled match path.
- Add regression test for invalid input behavior with stable, explicit exception type/message.

title: Fix Anthropic API response parsing - No JSON found errors
description:
Multiple tests are failing with 'Structured output recovery attempts limit reached after 1 attempt(s) due to: No JSON found in the response' errors. This indicates that the Anthropic API response format differs from OpenAI's format, causing JSON extraction to fail.

Location: packages/instructor/src/RetryPolicy/DefaultRetryPolicy.php:92
Error: StructuredOutputRecoveryException - No JSON found in the response

Tests affected include:
- BasicDataExtractionModesTest
- EventsTest  
- ExtractionTest
- MaybeTest, MixinTest, ScalarsTest
- FeaturesTest, InstructorTest
- StructuredOutputSmokeTest

Root cause: Anthropic API responses have different structure than OpenAI responses, and the JSON extraction logic is not compatible.

Need to:
1. Analyze Anthropic vs OpenAI response formats
2. Update JSON extraction logic to handle both formats  
3. Ensure response parsing works for both API providers
4. Update instructor extraction logic for Anthropic compatibility

title: Make Polyglot request value objects typed-only and move compatibility to factories
description:
Context:
The public fluent/request APIs above Polyglot are now typed-first, but the core raw request value objects still normalize strings and arrays late. `InferenceRequest` and `CachedInferenceContext` accept compatibility shapes in constructors and mutators, which keeps multiple contracts alive inside the core request model.

Files / scope:
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceRequest.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/CachedInferenceContext.php

Expected outcome:
- Core Polyglot request value objects are strictly typed in constructors and `with*()` methods.
- Compatibility input is isolated to explicit hydration/coercion paths such as `fromArray()` or dedicated named factories.
- Internals stop doing late normalization for ordinary request construction.

Acceptance criteria:
- `InferenceRequest` constructor and mutators accept only typed `Messages`, `ToolDefinitions`, `ToolChoice`, `ResponseFormat`, and typed auxiliaries.
- `CachedInferenceContext` constructor and mutators accept only typed `Messages`, `ToolDefinitions`, `ToolChoice`, and `ResponseFormat`.
- Array/string compatibility remains available only through explicit hydration/coercion APIs, not through ordinary constructors/mutators.
- Existing serialization round-trip behavior remains covered by tests.
- Add or update contract tests proving typed-only constructor/mutator signatures and explicit compatibility boundaries.
close_reason: Merged into instructor-l98r for execution as part of the broader typed-boundary cleanup.

title: Refactor OpaqueExternalId to support empty/null state
description:
OpaqueExternalId currently throws on empty string. This prevents typed ID value objects from representing 'absent' state, forcing callers to use nullable primitives instead.

## File to modify
packages/utils/src/Identifier/OpaqueExternalId.php

## Current behavior
```php
public function __construct(public string $value) {
    if (trim($value) === '') {
        throw new \InvalidArgumentException('External ID cannot be empty');
    }
}
```

## Proposed changes
- Remove the throw on empty string from constructor
- Add static factory: `static::empty(): static` — constructs with empty string
- Add query: `isEmpty(): bool` — true when value is ''
- Add query: `isPresent(): bool` — inverse of isEmpty
- toString() returns '' for empty IDs (safe for serialization)
- equals() treats two empty IDs of the same class as equal

## Why
ToolCallId (extends OpaqueExternalId) needs to represent 'no ID assigned' for:
- Gemini non-streaming responses (no call IDs in API response)
- ToolCall construction before ID assignment
- Serialization round-trips where ID may be absent

Currently this forces ToolCall to use ?string instead of typed ToolCallId, losing type safety. The fix is simple: let the value object represent the absent state.

## Acceptance criteria
1. OpaqueExternalId accepts empty string without throwing
2. empty() factory creates an empty instance
3. isEmpty()/isPresent() query methods work correctly
4. toString() returns '' for empty IDs
5. equals() handles empty-to-empty comparison
6. Existing OpaqueExternalId subclasses (MessageId, ToolCallId, etc.) still work
7. No behavioral change for non-empty IDs
notes: Started implementation: reviewing OpaqueExternalId subclasses, current constructor contract, and affected tests before applying empty-state support.
close_reason: Completed: OpaqueExternalId now has explicit empty() and isPresent() APIs, null() delegates to empty(), and focused identifier tests cover empty-state semantics.
