title: Epic: Remove OutputMode from Polyglot and make response shaping explicit for 2.0.0
description:
Goal: remove OutputMode from the Polyglot inference API and make response shaping explicit through responseFormat, tools, and toolChoice only.

Context: Polyglot currently mixes transport-level inference parameters with upper-layer structured-output strategy. That makes the API surprising, duplicates intent across responseFormat and mode, and leaks Instructor concepts like MdJson into the generic inference layer.

Expected outcome: Polyglot models only provider request parameters. Instructor owns structured-output strategy selection and translates that strategy into plain inference requests. Driver capabilities describe real inference features rather than mode labels.
acceptance_criteria: InferenceRequest no longer stores mode. DriverCapabilities no longer expose outputModes. Pure Inference APIs use responseFormat, tools, and toolChoice only. Instructor keeps any remaining structured-output strategy concepts above Polyglot. Docs and examples describe the 2.0.0 break clearly.
notes: All child stages completed for the 2.0 OutputMode cleanup epic.
close_reason: Completed the 2.0 OutputMode cleanup: DriverCapabilities simplified, ResponseFormat promoted, raw Inference moved to explicit responseFormat/tools/toolChoice, structured-output strategy kept above Polyglot, and docs/examples/migration notes updated.

title: Simplify DriverCapabilities to real inference features
description: Refactor DriverCapabilities so it describes concrete inference features instead of upper-layer strategy labels.\n\nExpected outcome: capabilities become smaller, clearer, and directly useful to driver and client code. Replace outputModes-based capability reporting with explicit support for response formats, tool calling, tool choice, streaming, and simple limits such as max context/output tokens.
acceptance_criteria: DriverCapabilities no longer contains outputModes or supportsOutputMode(). It exposes only concrete inference features such as streaming, toolCalling, toolChoice, responseFormatJsonObject, responseFormatJsonSchema, responseFormatWithTools, maxContextTokens, and maxOutputTokens. Driver capability declarations and tests are updated accordingly.
notes: Started refactoring DriverCapabilities to remove outputModes and expose explicit inference features/limits.
close_reason: Completed: removed outputModes from DriverCapabilities, updated driver declarations, and rewrote capability tests around explicit inference features and limits.

title: Promote ResponseFormat to the sole Polyglot response-format contract
description: Make ResponseFormat the only response-format value object in Polyglot.\n\nExpected outcome: ResponseFormat explicitly represents text, json object, and json schema requests without depending on OutputMode. The type stays small and boring: just the request shape needed by drivers.
acceptance_criteria: ResponseFormat no longer imports or depends on OutputMode. It has obvious explicit constructors/helpers for empty, text, json_object, and json_schema. Serialization/deserialization and driver-facing conversion tests are updated to reflect ResponseFormat as the source of truth.
notes: Started promoting ResponseFormat to the sole Polyglot response-format contract and removing its dependency on OutputMode.
close_reason: Completed: ResponseFormat is now an explicit immutable response-shape value object, body mappers render explicit shapes themselves, and tests/docs were updated around ResponseFormat as the source of truth.

title: Remove OutputMode from Inference request, builder, and facade APIs
description: Remove OutputMode from the public and internal Polyglot request-building surface.\n\nExpected outcome: raw inference APIs become explicit and unsurprising. Callers shape output only through responseFormat plus tools/toolChoice, not through an upper-layer strategy enum.
acceptance_criteria: InferenceRequest has no mode field or outputMode accessor. InferenceRequestBuilder has no withOutputMode() and no mode parameter in with(). Inference::with(...) has no mode parameter. Serialization, unit tests, and affected examples are updated to the new API.
notes: Started removing OutputMode from Inference request, builder, and facade APIs while keeping request shaping explicit through responseFormat, tools, and toolChoice.
close_reason: Completed: removed OutputMode from InferenceRequest, InferenceRequestBuilder, HandlesRequestBuilder, and Inference facade APIs; updated tests, core Polyglot docs, and raw inference examples to use responseFormat/tools instead.

title: Refactor provider body mappers to derive request shape from ResponseFormat and tools only
description: Update provider request body mappers so they no longer read OutputMode.\n\nExpected outcome: drivers build request bodies from the explicit request data only: responseFormat, tools, toolChoice, options, and messages. This removes the current mode-first fallback logic and makes provider mapping easier to reason about.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI, OpenResponses, Mistral, Gemini, and any other affected drivers do not inspect request->outputMode(). Mode-based helpers such as toResponseFormatMode() are removed. Response format handling branches only on responseFormat type and tool configuration. Existing golden/mock tests are updated and remain green.
notes: Started removing request->outputMode() from provider body mappers and deriving request bodies from responseFormat, tools, toolChoice, and options only.
close_reason: Completed: removed mode-based body-shaping helpers from OpenAI, OpenResponses, Mistral, Gemini, and OpenAI-compatible driver body mappers; they now derive request shape from responseFormat and tool configuration only, with tests remaining green.

title: Make raw Inference JSON access explicit instead of mode-driven
description: Clean up raw inference response helpers that still switch behavior based on OutputMode.\n\nExpected outcome: content JSON parsing and tool-call JSON access are explicit and separate. The generic JSON helpers stop silently changing source depending on an upper-layer mode value.
acceptance_criteria: PendingInference::asJson()/asJsonData() no longer depend on OutputMode. InferenceResponse::findJsonData() is either simplified to content-based parsing or replaced with explicit helpers for content JSON vs tool-call args JSON. Tests and examples document the explicit API.
notes: Started making raw Inference JSON access explicit so content JSON and tool-call JSON are separate, unsurprising paths.
close_reason: Completed: split raw inference JSON access into explicit content-json and tool-call-json helpers, updated PendingInference/Inference convenience methods, adjusted StructuredOutput integration, and updated tests/examples/docs.

title: Move structured-output strategy concepts fully above Polyglot
description: Ensure structured-output strategy selection lives in Instructor, not Polyglot.\n\nExpected outcome: Instructor may still choose between tools, native JSON, native JSON schema, and markdown-json fallback, but it materializes that choice into a plain inference request before crossing into Polyglot. MdJson and other strategy-only concepts stop leaking into the inference layer.
acceptance_criteria: Polyglot public APIs and capabilities no longer model MdJson or other structured-output-only strategies. Instructor strategy/config code translates into responseFormat, tools, toolChoice, and prompt/extraction behavior before invoking Inference. Cross-package tests confirm the boundary.
notes: Started moving remaining structured-output strategy concepts above Polyglot so markdown-json fallback and similar behavior stop leaking into raw inference APIs.
close_reason: Moved OutputMode into Instructor, removed the Polyglot enum, updated cross-package callers to explicit responseFormat/tools or Instructor OutputMode, and verified with package test suites.

title: Update 2.0 docs, examples, and migration notes for explicit response shaping
description: Rewrite the user-facing surface around the new boundary.\n\nExpected outcome: docs and examples teach raw Inference through responseFormat, tools, and toolChoice only. StructuredOutput docs explain that strategy selection happens in Instructor. The 2.0 migration guide calls out the removed mode-based APIs and the explicit replacements.
acceptance_criteria: B01/B04 inference examples no longer use mode. Polyglot and Instructor docs no longer describe OutputMode as a Polyglot concept. 2.0 migration notes explicitly document removed APIs and their replacements. Targeted doctests/examples validation passes.
notes: Completed docs/examples/migration cleanup for explicit response shaping; validated updated examples with php -l and rescans.
close_reason: Updated docs, examples, facade annotations, and migration notes for explicit responseFormat/tools/toolChoice in Polyglot and Instructor-owned OutputMode. Validated changed examples with php -l and rescanned B01/B04 + Polyglot docs for removed raw mode APIs.

title: Events: validate HandlesEvents wiring failure mode for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests the HandlesEvents trait may fatal with an uninitialized typed-property error if a caller forgets to wire an event bus before dispatching or registering listeners. That may be an acceptable explicit-wiring contract or an avoidable footgun.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. Do not assume this should be changed immediately. First validate the claim against the current codebase, migration docs, and actual trait consumers, then judge whether the work is valuable enough for 2.0.0.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the failure mode with focused reproductions for dispatch(), onEvent(), and wiretap() before wiring.
- Clarify the intended contract of HandlesEvents in the explicit 2.0 wiring model: hard precondition, defensive no-op/default, or domain-specific exception.
- Make a release judgement: fix now, document as intentional, or defer based on value vs added complexity for 2.0.0.
- If the work is worth doing, implement the smallest clear change that makes the failure mode unsurprising and easy to diagnose.

Related chores:
- Create and confirm a regression test for the validated failure mode.
- Fix or update related trait/consumer tests and examples that demonstrate event wiring.
- Update README/CHEATSHEET/MIGRATION-2.0 docs if the trait contract or error behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and runtime behavior and records the result.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, HandlesEvents has a clear and predictable failure mode when used before wiring.
- A regression test is added and confirmed for the chosen scenario.
- Related tests, examples, and docs are updated where needed, or the task records why no changes are necessary.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro. HandlesEvents previously failed with an uninitialized typed-property Error when dispatch(), wiretap(), or onEvent() were used before withEventHandler(). For 2.0.0 the clearer no-surprise contract is explicit wiring with a domain-level LogicException instead of a PHP engine error. Implemented that guard in packages/events/src/Traits/HandlesEvents.php, added regression coverage in packages/events/tests/Regression/HandlesEventsUnwiredRegressionTest.php, and clarified the explicit wiring requirement in the events cheatsheet and migration notes.

title: CanUseTools returns AgentState + delete CanReportObserverState side-channel
description:
## Scope

Change `CanUseTools::useTools()` to return `AgentState` instead of `AgentStep`. Move `Tools` and `CanExecuteToolCalls` from per-call parameters to driver constructors. Inject `HookStackObserver` into drivers so they can merge hook-mutated state. Delete `CanReportObserverState` interface and all implementations. Simplify `AgentLoop::useTools()` to a single driver call.

This is the foundational change — it makes state flow explicit and removes the hidden observer-state side-channel that makes the current code hard to reason about.

## Files to change

Contracts:
- `packages/agents/src/Core/Contracts/CanUseTools.php` — change return type to `AgentState`, remove `Tools` and `CanExecuteToolCalls` params
- `packages/agents/src/Core/Contracts/CanReportObserverState.php` — DELETE

Drivers:
- `packages/agents/src/Drivers/ToolCalling/ToolCallingDriver.php` — accept `Tools`, `CanExecuteToolCalls`, `?HookStackObserver` via constructor; return `AgentState`; remove `CanReportObserverState` impl; merge observer state before returning
- `packages/agents/src/Drivers/ReAct/ReActDriver.php` — same pattern
- `packages/agents/src/Drivers/Testing/DeterministicAgentDriver.php` — accept `Tools`, `CanExecuteToolCalls` via constructor; return `AgentState`

Builder:
- `packages/agents/src/AgentBuilder/AgentBuilder.php` — `buildDriver()` now injects `Tools`, `CanExecuteToolCalls`, and `HookStackObserver` into driver constructors. Construction order: eventEmitter → observer → toolExecutor → driver (reversed from current: driver → observer → toolExecutor)

Loop:
- `packages/agents/src/Core/AgentLoop.php` — simplify `useTools()`: just `$this->driver->useTools($state)`. Delete `applyObserverState()` method.

Executor:
- `packages/agents/src/Core/Tools/ToolExecutor.php` — remove `implements CanReportObserverState`, remove `observerState()` method

## Acceptance criteria

- `CanUseTools::useTools(AgentState $state): AgentState` — single param, returns state with step embedded
- Drivers receive `Tools`, `CanExecuteToolCalls`, and (optionally) `HookStackObserver` via constructor
- Each driver merges observer state internally before returning `AgentState`
- `AgentLoop::useTools()` is a single call to `$this->driver->useTools($state)` — no post-processing
- `CanReportObserverState` interface deleted, no references remain
- `AgentLoop::applyObserverState()` deleted
- `ToolExecutor` no longer implements `CanReportObserverState`

## Validations / checks / tests

- Run full agent test suite: `vendor/bin/pest packages/agents/tests/`
- Tests that directly assert on `useTools()` return type (AgentStep → AgentState):
  - `AgentDeterministicExecutionTest` — update to assert AgentState
  - Any test mocking `CanUseTools` — update mock return type
- Tests that construct drivers directly — update constructor args
- Tests using `AgentBuilder::build()` — should pass without changes (builder handles wiring)
- Verify: hook-mutated state propagates correctly through tool execution (existing guard hook tests)
- Verify: `ToolCallingDriver` observer state from inference hooks merges into returned state
- Verify: `ReActDriver` observer state from both inference and tool hooks merges correctly
- No references to `CanReportObserverState` remain (grep)
- No references to `applyObserverState` remain (grep)

title: [polyglot] Fix Usage::fromArray pricing semantics to avoid silent zero-cost reports
description:
Problem and context:
Usage::fromArray() currently always assigns a Pricing object (Pricing::fromArray([])) even when pricing is absent. This changes cost() semantics and can silently underreport spend.

Observed behavior:
- new Usage(...)->cost() without pricing throws RuntimeException (expected: no pricing info).
- Usage::fromArray(['input'=>..., 'output'=>...])->cost() returns 0 instead of throwing.

Impact:
- Persisted/reloaded usage objects can report zero cost even though pricing is unknown.
- Cost dashboards or logs may silently undercount spend.

Code references:
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/Usage.php:22-30
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/Usage.php:67-85

Expected outcomes:
- Missing pricing stays semantically missing after fromArray round-trip.
- cost() throws when pricing is not configured (unless explicit zero-pricing is intentionally provided).
- Behavior is consistent between directly constructed and rehydrated Usage instances.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Usage::fromArray payload without pricing keeps pricing unset (or equivalent no-pricing state). 2) cost() throws for missing pricing in both new and fromArray instances. 3) Regression tests cover inconsistent behavior repro and fixed behavior. 4) Existing usage cost tests remain green with updated expectations.
close_reason: Fixed Usage::fromArray pricing null semantics: missing pricing key now keeps pricing unset instead of creating implicit zero-pricing object. Added regression test proving fromArray without pricing still throws on cost(), matching direct-construction behavior.

comments.author: ddebowczyk
comments.text: # Investigation & testing protocol\n\nObligatory: start by investigating the code causing the issue. After an initial understanding of the offending module/class, TEST FIRST: recreate the error using a deterministic mechanism (mock http client or mock inference or deterministic agent driver). Ensure the deterministic mechanism does not hide the problem; if needed, use a lower-level deterministic mechanism (mock http -> mock inference -> mock agent).

title: Adopt ToolDefinitions and ToolChoice in InferenceRequest and CachedInferenceContext
description:
Replace array $tools and string|array $toolChoice with typed value objects in InferenceRequest and CachedInferenceContext.

## Files to modify
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceRequest.php
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/CachedInferenceContext.php

## Changes

### InferenceRequest
- $tools: array → ToolDefinitions
- $toolChoice: string|array → ToolChoice
- Constructor normalizes: accepts ToolDefinitions|array for tools, ToolChoice|string|array for toolChoice (same pattern as Messages normalization)
- tools(): ToolDefinitions (was array)
- toolChoice(): ToolChoice (was string|array)
- withTools(ToolDefinitions|array): self
- withToolChoice(ToolChoice|string|array): self
- hasTools() uses $this->tools->isEmpty() and cached context
- toArray()/fromArray() serialize through value object toArray()/fromArray()
- with() method updated for new types
- withCacheApplied() uses typed isEmpty checks

### CachedInferenceContext
- Same pattern: $tools → ToolDefinitions, $toolChoice → ToolChoice
- Constructor normalizes arrays to typed objects
- Accessors return typed objects
- toArray()/fromArray() serialize correctly

## Expected outcomes
- No raw array tool handling remains in InferenceRequest or CachedInferenceContext
- External callers passing arrays still work (constructor normalization)
- Serialization round-trips are preserved

## Acceptance criteria
1. tools() returns ToolDefinitions, toolChoice() returns ToolChoice
2. Constructor accepts both typed objects and raw arrays (backwards compat)
3. with()/withTools()/withToolChoice() accept both typed and raw
4. toArray()/fromArray() round-trip produces identical results to current behavior
5. hasTools()/hasToolChoice() work correctly with typed isEmpty()
6. withCacheApplied() merges typed objects correctly
7. Existing InferenceRequest tests pass (InferenceRequestBuilderTest, InferenceRequestSerializationTest)
close_reason: Completed: InferenceRequest and CachedInferenceContext now use ToolDefinitions and ToolChoice internally, preserve array input normalization, and pass request serialization/cache tests.

title: Separate internal inference options from provider request options
description: retryPolicy (and other execution-control options) are stored in InferenceRequest::options and merged into provider request bodies. This leaks internal options into HTTP payloads (e.g., Anthropic rejects retryPolicy). Refactor so internal execution options are stored separately from provider options (e.g., new internalOptions/retryPolicy field on InferenceRequest/EmbeddingsRequest), update InferenceRequestBuilder + PendingInference/Embeddings to read internal options, and ensure all body formatters only use provider options. Add migration notes for AgentBuilder/ToolCallingDriver options.
close_reason: Separated retry policy from provider options; added explicit retryPolicy APIs and guards; updated drivers/tests

title: Events: validate object payload normalization semantics for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests Event constructor object payload normalization may silently discard non-public object state by converting payload objects with get_object_vars(). This may be either a real bug or an intentional best-effort logging snapshot behavior.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. This task must start by validating the actual intended semantics of event payload normalization in the current codebase and documentation, then judge whether changing it is valuable enough for 2.0.0. Avoid adding magical deep serialization if the value is not clearly worth it.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the reported payload-loss behavior with minimal reproductions using objects with public and non-public state.
- Decide whether Event should preserve raw objects, keep best-effort public snapshots, or fail/document unsupported payload shapes more clearly.
- Make a release judgement: implement a targeted change now, document the current semantics, or defer based on value vs simplicity for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing, deliver the smallest clear change that keeps payload semantics explicit and unsurprising.

Related chores:
- Create and confirm a regression test for the validated payload scenario.
- Fix or update related tests around Event serialization/log formatting if behavior changes.
- Update examples/docs/CHEATSHEET only if public payload semantics change or need clarification.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and records whether the reported payload normalization behavior is actually occurring.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, object payload handling is clearly defined for the chosen supported scope and does not silently behave in a surprising way.
- A regression test is added and confirmed for the chosen scenario.
- Related tests, examples, and docs are updated where needed, or the task records why no changes are necessary.

title: TypeDetailsFactory::fromValue crashes on homogeneous arrays containing unsupported items (null/resources)
description:
Problem and context:
`TypeDetails::fromValue()` still crashes for homogeneous arrays where element type is unsupported (e.g. `[null, null]`, `[resource, resource]`). This regressed from the intended fix that handled scalar `null`/resource values only.

Observed behavior:
Runtime `get_class()` type error is thrown when array item is not an object.

Why this matters:
Payload inference paths can still hard-fail in production for edge-case arrays instead of degrading safely to `mixed`/`array` semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add regression tests for `TypeDetails::fromValue([null, null])` and homogeneous resource arrays; both must not throw.
- Add regression test for mixed unsupported arrays (e.g. `[null, resource]`) to ensure safe fallback behavior.
- Implementation must never call `get_class()` on non-object values in array inference path.
- Define and document expected return type for unsupported-element arrays (e.g. `mixed` or untyped `array`) and assert it in tests.
close_reason: Confirmed high-impact crash (TypeError from get_class on null/resource array items), added regression tests for unsupported homogeneous/mixed arrays, and fixed TypeDetailsFactory::fromValue array inference to safely fall back to untyped array for unsupported/null-only samples while preserving typed collection inference for supported values.

title: [http-client][2.0] Validate record/replay request identity and collision risk
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that record/replay request identity is too weak because it ignores headers and request options, then judge whether tightening that behavior is worth the effort for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current codebase, tests, and real usage patterns. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. If a stronger identity model adds too much complexity relative to actual value, say so explicitly.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: `packages/http-client/src/Extras/Support/RecordReplay/RequestRecords.php` keys recordings using only method + URL + body, while replay matching also ignores headers/options. This may cause collisions or incorrect replays for requests that differ by authorization headers, accept headers, tenant/model headers, or request options.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the collision risk with concrete code references and deterministic tests.
- Decide whether the request identity should be expanded for 2.0.0, documented as intentionally narrow, or left unchanged.
- If worth doing now, implement the simplest explicit matching model and make it obvious to callers.

## Acceptance criteria
- A deterministic test demonstrates whether two requests with same method/URL/body but different headers/options collide or replay incorrectly.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: request identity/matching rules are explicit in code and docs, with no surprising hidden collisions for supported use cases.
- If go: regression tests cover at least one header-based and one option-based differentiation case, or intentionally document why some fields are excluded.
- If go: docs/examples/CHEATSHEET reflect the chosen replay identity contract.
- If no-go: document the limitation clearly where record/replay is described.

## Related chores
- Add or update tests in `RequestRecordsTest` / replay coverage.
- Update docs/examples/CHEATSHEET for record/replay identity semantics.
- Remove stale wording that implies a stronger matching guarantee than the code actually provides.
close_reason: Validated and judged no-go for a broader 2.0.0 identity model without explicit approval. Current record/replay identity is intentionally narrow: method + full URL + body, with streaming mode separated by filename prefix; headers and other request options are ignored. Added focused coverage demonstrating header/option collisions and documented the contract in middleware docs/CHEATSHEET so the behavior is explicit instead of surprising.

title: Add new regression test suite for issue-raising edge cases in schema generation
description:
Context:
Recent reassessment found high-impact schema defects that were not caught early enough by existing tests. We need dedicated tests that fail on these edge cases so issues are raised before release.

Expected outcome:
A new focused regression test suite is added for edge-case failures that should trigger issue escalation in development/review.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add at least one new failing-then-passing regression test for each uncovered edge-case category: nested refs completeness, ref key collisions, self-referential schema safety, and type normalization/null-resource handling.
- Tests are deterministic and run in package CI (`packages/schema/tests`).
- Document in test names/comments which production defect each test is guarding against (issue ID linkage).
- Ensure these tests are part of release quality gate execution path.
close_reason: Assessed as mostly already implemented by existing schema regression tests; added explicit issue-linkage comments for guarded defects across nested refs,  collisions, self-reference safety, and type normalization/null-resource handling; verified schema regression and full package suites pass.

title: agents docs: simplify AgentBuilder guide to Laravel-style quick reference
description: Problem and context: packages/agents/docs/13-agent-builder.md is one of the most referenced docs, but it is long, concept-heavy, and mixes architecture deep dive with day-to-day usage. This increases onboarding time and makes copy-paste usage harder before 2.0.0.
acceptance_criteria: Expected outcomes: document optimized for quick adoption with short examples and plain language. Acceptance criteria: (1) top section is a 30-second quickstart that compiles against current 2.0 API; (2) capability examples are reduced to short recipes grouped by goal; (3) deep internal details moved to a separate internals section or document; (4) every public API snippet in the page is verified against current classes/methods; (5) no multi-import one-liners in code blocks.
close_reason: Completed in docs rewrite: concise Laravel-style updates with API verification

title: Preserve null tool inheritance semantics in file-based AgentDefinition parsing
description:
Problem and context:
- AgentDefinition::fromArray() currently maps missing/null tools and toolsDeny to NameList::fromArray([]), producing empty NameList instead of null.
- That makes inheritsAllTools() false for file-loaded definitions (Markdown/YAML/JSON), so subagents built from templates get no tools by default.
- Docs historically describe null tools as inherit-all, creating a cross-doc/runtime mismatch.

Expected outcomes:
- File-loaded definitions preserve null for missing tools/toolsDeny (and skills if intended), matching constructor semantics.
- inheritsAllTools() is true when tools is omitted in template files.
- Tool resolution in DefinitionLoopFactory and SpawnSubagentTool behaves consistently between programmatic and file-based definitions.
- Documentation can remove caveats and use one mental model.

Acceptance criteria:
- Parsing a minimal markdown/yaml/json definition without tools yields AgentDefinition->tools === null.
- DefinitionLoopFactory with a tool registry inherits all tools when tools is omitted.
- SpawnSubagentTool inherits parent tools when a file-loaded definition omits tools.
- Regression tests cover fromArray/parser behavior for omitted and explicit tools fields.
- Existing behavior for explicit allow-list and toolsDeny remains unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed AgentDefinition::fromArray null-semantics for tools/toolsDeny (and skills) by preserving omitted/null fields as null while keeping explicit lists unchanged; added regression tests for file-loaded definitions, DefinitionLoopFactory tool inheritance, and SpawnSubagentTool parent-tool inheritance; updated parser unit expectations; targeted pest/phpstan passed.

title: agents: WriteFileTool static withPolicy -> fromPolicy (2.0 clean cut)
description:
Goal: align static constructor naming in WriteFileTool for 2.0.0.

Changes:
- Rename WriteFileTool::withPolicy(ExecutionPolicy) to WriteFileTool::fromPolicy(ExecutionPolicy).
- Keep/retain WriteFileTool::inDirectory(...) as-is.
- Update all call sites/examples/docs/tests.
- No deprecations/aliases; clean break.

Acceptance:

close_reason: Renamed WriteFileTool::withPolicy to fromPolicy, verified no remaining usages, and ran packages/agents tests.

title: Detect stepResults/steps mismatch in StepByStep canContinue
description: canContinue() falls back to fresh evaluation when lastStepResult is null, even if stepCount > 0 (e.g., state built via recordStep/withAddedStep or legacy deserialization). This violates the StepResult design (canContinue should rely on recorded outcomes) and can hide missing outcomes. Add mismatch detection (stepCount > stepResults count) and fail fast or mark state invalid; update recordStep/failWith or provide explicit migration path.
close_reason: Detect mismatched steps vs stepResults in canContinue and fail fast; added mismatch unit test

title: Investigate ClaudeCodeSearch example - runs too fast (0.10s), likely silent failure
description: Example [5] ClaudeCodeSearch completes in 0.10 seconds which is abnormally fast for an LLM API call. Likely a silent failure or muted error messages. Check examples/B05_LLMExtras/ClaudeCodeSearch/run.php
close_reason: Examples working correctly. Recent commits added proper CLI detection (ProcUtils::findOnPath). Fast execution times occur when CLI not installed, now properly showing ERROR status.

title: Add tests for buffer/summary behavior per intended design
description: Create tests that assert: message overflow moves raw messages to buffer; buffer overflow prepends to summary then summarizes; summary used rarely; messages/buffer remain raw, summary contains summarized content; inference compilation order is system -> summary -> buffer -> messages.
close_reason: Added ChatBufferFlowTest to cover overflow to buffer and compilation order; tests pass with current implementation.

title: HttpRequest: make updatedAt and metadata readonly to enforce clone-on-write
description: updatedAt and metadata are public mutable fields while id and createdAt are readonly. External code can bypass the clone-on-write pattern by direct assignment, creating hidden shared state bugs. Both fields should be readonly with any mutation going through clone-returning methods.
close_reason: Added fail-first regression for direct mutation bypass, made HttpRequest updatedAt and metadata readonly, refactored mutators to constructor-based clone-on-write copies, validated roundtrip/immutability tests, and updated cheatsheet lifecycle note.

title: [polyglot-docs] Align internals/extending docs with current 2.0 API
description:
## Problem and context
Internal Polyglot documentation is materially out of sync with current 2.0 code, and currently documents removed namespaces/interfaces/methods.

This affects architecture and extension docs used as source of truth by contributors.

### Confirmed drift examples
- `internals/request-response.md` uses `namespace Cognesy\Polyglot\LLM` and `toText()` in `PendingInference` examples.
- `internals/providers.md` documents `CanHandleInference` / `CanVectorize` contracts instead of current inference/vectorization contracts.
- `internals/configuration.md` documents `providerType`/`httpClient` fields that are not present in current `LLMConfig`.
- `advanced/extending.md` still references obsolete extension contracts in multiple snippets.

### Code references for current API
- `packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Inference.php`
- `packages/polyglot/src/Inference/PendingInference.php`
- `packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Config/LLMConfig.php`
- `packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Contracts/CanProcessInferenceRequest.php`
- `packages/polyglot/src/Embeddings/Contracts/CanHandleVectorization.php`

## Expected outcomes
- Internal architecture docs match current package API and naming.
- Extension docs show valid, current extension points (driver registration, adapter contracts, runtime wiring).
- No snippets in internal docs rely on removed methods/types.

## Acceptance criteria
- Update at minimum:
  1. `packages/polyglot/docs/internals/request-response.md`
  2. `packages/polyglot/docs/internals/providers.md`
  3. `packages/polyglot/docs/internals/configuration.md`
  4. `packages/polyglot/docs/advanced/extending.md`
- Replace obsolete identifiers (`Cognesy\\Polyglot\\LLM`, `CanHandleInference`, `CanVectorize`, `providerType`, `toText`) with current API equivalents.
- Validate all PHP snippets in those files are syntactically valid and consistent with public API signatures.
close_reason: Updated internals/configuration.md, internals/request-response.md, internals/providers.md, and advanced/extending.md to current 2.0 APIs; removed obsolete namespaces/interfaces/methods and aligned examples with runtime/driver contracts.

title: [stream] Eliminate PHPStan level-max errors in core stream paths
description:
Problem
phpstan analyse packages/stream/src --level=max --memory-limit=1G currently reports 17 errors in reducers, filesystem streams, result transforms, and TransformationExecution. This reduces confidence in type contracts in release-critical code paths.

Context
Current errors include:
- offset access on mixed in reducers (GroupByReducer, ToArrayReducer, result reducers/transducers)
- mixed iterator element type in filesystem directory streams
- TransformationExecution::onReduced() mixed misuse warning

Expected outcomes
- Stream package passes PHPStan level max without suppressing real issues.
- Mixed-heavy reducer contracts are made explicit via safer local typing/assertions/generics where possible.

Acceptance criteria
- vendor/bin/phpstan analyse packages/stream/src --level=max --memory-limit=1G returns 0 errors.
- Changes keep existing tests green.
- No broad cross-package refactors; scope remains within packages/stream unless explicitly approved.
close_reason: Eliminated PHPStan level-max errors in packages/stream/src by tightening reducer accumulator typing, adding filesystem iterator type guards, and fixing Reduced value typing in TransformationExecution; stream package tests remain green.

title: Generate unique attempt IDs per retry
description:
Outcomes:
- Each retry attempt has a unique attemptId in emitted events.

Context:
- Problem: PendingInference::getCurrentAttemptId returns execution->id when no attempt exists yet. For exception-based retries, no attempt is added at attempt-start, so multiple attempts share the same id.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/PendingInference.php (dispatchAttemptStarted/getCurrentAttemptId).
- Suggested fix: generate and store a new UUID when dispatching attempt-start (e.g., $this->currentAttemptId) and use it for subsequent attempt events, or create an InferenceAttempt object at attempt-start.

Acceptance Criteria:
- InferenceAttemptStarted/Failed/Succeeded events use a unique attemptId for each retry.
- Successful attempts still correlate with execution attempts list correctly.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test with a stub driver that throws once then succeeds; assert attemptId changes between attempt 1 and attempt 2 in emitted events.
close_reason: Create attempts via InferenceExecution::startAttempt to generate IDs in constructor; reuse currentAttempt id on finalize and add retry attempt-id test

title: Agent Framework Improvements - PRM Team Feedback
description:
Epic addressing critical bugs and feature gaps identified by the PRM team in the InstructorPHP Agent framework.

## Reference Documents
All specifications are in `research/2026-01-16-agents/`:
- `epic-agent-framework-improvements.md` - Master epic document
- `continuation-api-proposal.md` - Complete API specification for continuation tracing + error policy

## Phases
- Phase 0: Critical Bug Fix [COMPLETED]
- Phase 1: Foundation [PARTIALLY COMPLETED]
- Phase 2: Continuation Tracing
- Phase 3: Error Policy
- Phase 4: Cumulative Time Tracking

## Timeline
Estimated: 4 weeks (~54 hours)
close_reason: Completed all tasks in Agent Framework Improvements epic (continuation tracing, error policy, time tracking, serialization, events, docs).

title: ExecutionTimeLimit Uses Wrong Timestamp [Phase 0]
description:
**Status**: ✅ COMPLETED (2026-01-16)

## Objective
Fix bug where multi-turn conversations timeout immediately when sessions span multiple days.

## Problem
`ExecutionTimeLimit` was using `stateInfo.startedAt` (session creation time) instead of per-execution time.

## What was Implemented
1. Added `executionStartedAt` field to `AgentState`
2. Added `markExecutionStarted()` method called at execution entry point
3. Updated `StepByStep.php` to reset execution clock in `finalStep()` and `iterator()`
4. Updated `AgentBuilder.php` to use `executionStartedAt() ?? startedAt()` fallback

## Files Modified
- `packages/addons/src/Agent/Core/Data/AgentState.php`
- `packages/addons/src/StepByStep/StepByStep.php`
- `packages/addons/src/Agent/AgentBuilder.php`
- `packages/addons/src/StepByStep/Continuation/Criteria/ExecutionTimeLimit.php`

## Reference
See `research/2026-01-16-agents/epic-agent-framework-improvements.md` Task 0.1
close_reason: Previously completed

title: Integrate ErrorPolicy into AgentBuilder [Phase 3.4]
description:
## Objective
Update AgentBuilder to use new ErrorPolicy, removing deprecated criteria.

## Implementation Requirements

**Modify** `packages/addons/src/Agent/AgentBuilder.php`:

1. Add property and builder method:
```php
private ?ErrorPolicy $errorPolicy = null;

public function withErrorPolicy(ErrorPolicy $policy): self {
    $this->errorPolicy = $policy;
    return $this;
}
```

2. Update buildContinuationCriteria():
```php
private function buildContinuationCriteria(): ContinuationCriteria {
    $baseCriteria = [
        new StepsLimit(...),
        new TokenUsageLimit(...),
        new ExecutionTimeLimit(...),
        new FinishReasonCheck(...),

        // REPLACE ErrorPresenceCheck + RetryLimit with:
        ErrorPolicyCriterion::withPolicy(
            $this->errorPolicy ?? ErrorPolicy::stopOnAnyError()
        ),

        new ToolCallPresenceCheck(...),
    ];
    // ...
}
```

3. **Remove** references to ErrorPresenceCheck and RetryLimit from base criteria.

## Breaking Changes
- ErrorPresenceCheck removed from base criteria
- RetryLimit removed from base criteria

**Migration path**:
```php
// Before
->addContinuationCriteria(new ErrorPresenceCheck(...))
->addContinuationCriteria(new RetryLimit(3, ...))

// After
->withErrorPolicy(ErrorPolicy::retryToolErrors(3))
```

## Dependencies
- Task 3.3 (ErrorPolicyCriterion)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default behavior unchanged - stops on any error (integration test)
- [ ] Custom error policy applied when provided (integration test)
- [ ] withErrorPolicy() returns self for fluent chaining (unit test)
- [ ] No ErrorPresenceCheck in base criteria (code review)
- [ ] No RetryLimit in base criteria (code review)

## Reference
See `research/2026-01-16-agents/epic-agent-framework-improvements.md` Task 3.4
close_reason: Integrated ErrorPolicyCriterion into AgentBuilder with withErrorPolicy support and tests for default vs custom policy behavior.

title: Enhance StateInfo with Cumulative Time [Phase 4.1]
close_reason: Added cumulativeExecutionSeconds to StateInfo with accessors, addExecutionTime, serialization support, and unit tests.

title: Create CumulativeExecutionTimeLimit Criterion [Phase 4.2]
close_reason: Added CumulativeExecutionTimeLimit criterion with explanation context and unit tests for under/over limit behavior.

title: Track Step Duration in Agent [Phase 4.3]
close_reason: Tracked step duration in Agent.performStep, accumulated cumulative execution seconds, and added a deterministic duration test.

title: Add withCumulativeTimeout to AgentBuilder [Phase 4.4]
close_reason: Added withCumulativeTimeout to AgentBuilder, switched time limit criterion selection, and added unit tests for wall-clock vs cumulative behavior.

title: Create SlimSerializationConfig [Phase 5.1]
close_reason: Added SlimSerializationConfig with minimal/standard/full presets and unit tests covering defaults.

title: Create SlimAgentStateSerializer [Phase 5.2]
close_reason: Added SlimAgentStateSerializer and CanSerializeAgentState with truncation/redaction logic, execution metadata, and unit tests for serialization/deserialization.

title: Create Event Adapter Interface [Phase 6.1]
description:
## Objective
Create interface for broadcasting agent events to external systems.

## Implementation Requirements

**Create** `packages/addons/src/Agent/Broadcasting/CanBroadcastAgentEvents.php`:

title: Fix JsonSchema enum bridge to support int-backed enum values
description:
Problem and context:
`Schema -> JsonSchema` conversion currently fails for enum values that are not strings. `JsonSchema` enforces string-only enum arrays, but domain `Schema` supports enum backing values that may be integers (and `Schema\TypeInfo::enumValues()` can return int|string). This creates runtime breakage for numeric-backed enums and blocks safe schema rendering in production.

Expected outcomes:
- Rendering enum schemas with int-backed enum values is supported end-to-end.
- JSON schema output preserves enum values exactly (including integers).
- Parsing/roundtrip behavior remains compatible for existing string enum cases.

Solution direction:
- Update `JsonSchema` enum value validation to align with domain schema semantics (`int|string`), not string-only.
- Keep validation strict for unsupported enum member types.
- Add regression coverage at bridge level (`Schema -> JsonSchema`) and constructor/parser level (`JsonSchema::fromArray`).

Process (mandatory):
1. Add regression test first that reproduces the failure.
2. Run test to confirm it fails on current code.
3. Implement fix in smallest safe change set.
4. Re-run regression test and affected package tests to confirm pass.

Notes:
- This is a behavior bug, not API redesign.
- Keep public API stable while fixing semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A regression test demonstrates pre-fix failure for enum values containing integers (e.g., [1,2,3]).
2. After fix, rendering `EnumSchema`/option schema with integer enum values no longer throws.
3. Rendered JSON keeps integer enum values unchanged (no string coercion).
4. Existing string-enum behavior remains unchanged and covered by tests.
5. Workflow evidence in PR/commit history follows: fail-first test -> fix -> pass.
close_reason: Added fail-first regressions and fixed enum bridge for int-backed enums: renderer now maps int enums to JSON type integer, JsonSchema enum validation accepts int|string, scalar transformers emit enum for integer/number/boolean/string, docs updated.

title: [dynamic] Include readonly DTO properties in StructureFactory::fromClass()
description:
Problem
`StructureFactory::fromClass()` drops readonly properties, producing incomplete or empty dynamic structures for modern DTOs. In this codebase, readonly data classes are a primary modeling style, so reflected schemas become unusable.

Context
- packages/dynamic/src/StructureFactory.php:152 skips properties when `$propertyInfo->isReadOnly()`
- Repro confirmed:
  readonly class Dto { public function __construct(public string $name, public int $age) {} }
  StructureFactory::fromClass(Dto::class) -> 0 fields (expected: name, age)

Expected outcomes
- Readonly properties are included when generating dynamic structure definitions from class metadata.
- Resulting structure reflects actual DTO shape used at runtime.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test for readonly DTO class reflected via `fromClass()`.
- Generated structure contains readonly properties with correct type details.
- Existing exclusions for static and ignored fields remain intact.
close_reason: Fixed StructureFactory::fromClass readonly handling: readonly properties are now included (only static/ignored remain excluded). Added regression test covering readonly DTO constructor-promoted fields and their type details.

title: Clarify ResponseModel mutability

- Suggested fix: either replace with withPropertyValues(): static that returns a new instance, or document that ResponseModel is mutable and adjust API accordingly.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Call sites updated if method signature changes.

How to Test:
- Add a unit test verifying set/withPropertyValues behavior if changed.
close_reason: Replace setPropertyValues with immutable withPropertyValues and add unit test

title: [polyglot-docs] Align event docs with current dispatcher API and event payloads
description:
## Problem and context
Event-related docs are out of sync with current events package and polyglot event names/payload usage.

Observed mismatches:
- `packages/polyglot/docs/internals/events.md` references wrong namespace and stale event names (`InferenceResponseReceived`, `PartialInferenceResponseReceived`).
- `packages/polyglot/docs/internals/lifecycle.md` references stale `InferenceResponseReceived`.
- `packages/polyglot/docs/troubleshooting/debugging.md` uses `$events->listen(...)`, while dispatcher exposes `addListener(...)`.
- Debugging examples read `$event->request` / `$event->inferenceResponse`, but event payloads are available through `$event->data`.

This creates high confusion risk for observability/instrumentation in 2.0.0.

## Expected outcomes
- Event documentation reflects actual dispatcher API and currently emitted event classes.
- Debugging examples show correct listener registration and payload access patterns.
- Lifecycle doc uses current event names.

## Acceptance criteria
- `internals/events.md`, `internals/lifecycle.md`, and `troubleshooting/debugging.md` are updated to current API.
- Examples use `addListener(...)` (or otherwise explicitly document valid listener API).
- Event class names and payload field access match current implementation.
- Updated snippets pass docs QA syntax checks.
close_reason: Updated polyglot event docs to current API: fixed dispatcher namespace/API usage, replaced stale event names (InferenceResponseCreated/PartialInferenceResponseCreated), updated lifecycle event names, and switched debugging examples to addListener(...) with payload access via event->data.

title: Files::removeDirectory follows symlink roots and deletes target contents
description:
Problem and context:
`Files::removeDirectory()` treats a symlink-to-directory root as a real directory and recursively deletes through it. This can remove files in the symlink target unexpectedly, then fail on final `rmdir()` with "Not a directory".

Code:
- `packages/utils/src/Files.php:26`
- `packages/utils/src/Files.php:33`
- `packages/utils/src/Files.php:49`

Repro:
1. Create directory `target/keep.txt`
2. Create symlink `link -> target`
3. Call `Files::removeDirectory(link)`
4. Observed: `target/keep.txt` is deleted, method returns false/warning.

Expected outcomes:
Directory removal must never traverse symlink roots/entries in a way that can delete data outside the intended directory tree.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression test for symlink root behavior (must not delete symlink target contents).
- Decide and implement policy for symlink root input:
  - either reject with explicit exception, or
  - unlink symlink directly without traversing.
- Ensure recursive removal skips symlink traversal safely for nested entries as well.
- Remove warning-based failure path; return/throw deterministic error semantics.
close_reason: Already resolved: Files::removeDirectory unlinks symlink roots and does not traverse nested symlinks; regression tests pass.

title: Events: validate dispatcher sorting overhead against 2.0.0 value bar
description:
Problem:
Review suggests the core EventDispatcher may re-sort listener candidates on every dispatch and every getListenersForEvent() call, creating avoidable per-event overhead. This may be a real hotspot or just acceptable simplicity for a small event layer.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. This task must start by validating whether the current dispatcher cost is materially meaningful in realistic runtime usage, then judge whether any optimization is valuable enough to include in 2.0.0. If the gain is marginal, prefer leaving the implementation simple.

Expected outcomes:
- Measure or otherwise validate whether repeated listener sorting is a meaningful overhead in representative code paths.
- Identify the smallest change, if any, that improves cost without making ordering semantics harder to reason about.
- Make a release judgement: optimize now, document and leave as-is, or defer.
- If worth doing, implement the narrowest clear optimization and keep ordering behavior unchanged.

Related chores:
- Create and confirm a focused performance regression/characterization test only if it is stable and worthwhile.
- Fix or update related ordering/dispatcher tests if implementation details change.
- Update docs only if there is user-visible guidance or rationale worth documenting.
- Avoid complexity that is not justified by evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and records whether the suspected overhead is materially real.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, the optimization is small, behavior-preserving, and justified by evidence rather than intuition.
- A regression or characterization test is added and confirmed if that is actually worth doing, or the task records why it is not.
- Related tests, examples, and docs are updated where needed, or the task records why no changes are necessary.

title: Inference::with() method resets model to empty string when model param not provided
description:
The with() method in Inference class calls withModel($model) where $model defaults to empty string ''. This overwrites any previously set model via withModel() method.

Example:
```php
$inference = (new Inference)
    ->using('anthropic')
    ->withModel('claude-sonnet-4-20250514')  // Sets model
    ->withCachedContext(...);

$response = $inference
    ->with(
        messages: [...],
        options: ['max_tokens' => 512],
    )  // This resets model to '' because with() calls withModel('')
    ->response();
```

The with() method should only call withModel() if a non-empty model is provided.

Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Inference.php:92-108
close_reason: Fixed Inference::with() to delegate to InferenceRequestBuilder::with() which already handles null values correctly. StructuredOutput::with() was already correct. Added 5 regression tests to InferenceRequestBuilderTest.php

title: [laravel] Add package-level integration tests (Testbench/Pest) for provider, facades, commands
description:
Problem
`packages/laravel` currently ships no test suite (`packages/laravel/tests` missing), despite containing framework integration wiring, console commands, and testing fakes. Several concrete regressions slipped in (fake bypass, command exit codes, stub path mismatch), indicating inadequate package-level safety net.

Context

- Integration-heavy package surface (service provider bindings, facades, commands) is not exercised in CI at package level.

Expected outcomes

- Prevent recurrence of integration regressions before 2.0.

Acceptance criteria
- Introduce package test suite with at least:
  1) service provider binding/wiring smoke tests
  2) facade fake behavior tests
  3) artisan command success/failure tests
- Ensure tests run from monorepo root and package context.

Adding separate package-level Testbench harness would be broad infra duplication without proportional value before 2.0.

title: Define OutputMode expectations for StructuredOutput
description: Review docs + code to capture expected behavior per OutputMode (Tools/Json/MdJson/JsonSchema/Text), including streaming vs non-streaming and tool-call vs content sources. Sources: docs-build/instructor/essentials/modes.mdx, packages/instructor/src/Extraction/ResponseExtractor.php, packages/instructor/src/ResponseIterators/Sync/ResponseNormalizer.php, packages/instructor/src/ResponseIterators/ModularPipeline/ExtractDeltaReducer.php. Produce a short checklist of expected behaviors to validate against.
notes:
Checklist (expected behaviors):
- Tools: extraction uses toolCalls when present (ResponseExtractor::getToolCallContent); content may be empty; fallback to content if toolCalls missing (provider limitation). Streaming uses toolArgs deltas (ExtractDeltaReducer: toolArgs else contentDelta) and ToolsBuffer with Json::fromPartial. Sync uses ResponseNormalizer to set content = first tool call argsAsJson else content.
- Json: response content is JSON object; extraction from content only; ResponseNormalizer parses Json::fromString; streaming accumulates contentDelta.
- JsonSchema: same extraction path as Json; strict schema enforcement occurs upstream; content must be valid JSON object; no tool calls required.
- MdJson: content may be markdown codeblock; extraction via MarkdownBlock/Bracket/Resilient from content; streaming accumulates contentDelta.
- Text: raw text output; not intended for StructuredOutput extraction; ResponseNormalizer passes through content.
- Unrestricted: inference-only freeform; no structured extraction.
close_reason: Captured OutputMode expectations and data-source checklist

title: SchemaFactory: prevent recursion blowup for self-referencing classes
description:
Context:
With `useObjectReferences=false` (default), self-referential models recurse through inline object expansion until fatal/timeout.

Expected outcome:
Self-referencing types are handled safely (cycle detection and/or placeholder registration and/or fallback to refs) with deterministic termination.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add regression test using a self-referencing model (e.g. parent->self) that previously timed out/fataled and now passes quickly.
- Schema generation terminates without fatal error.
- Resulting schema is structurally valid and documents cycle handling approach in code/tests.
close_reason: Fixed: added inline object expansion cycle guard in SchemaFactory to truncate repeated-class branches and prevent recursion blowup; added regression test and verified schema suite passes.

title: Utils package quality scripts/config are not runnable in monorepo workflow
description:
Problem and context:
`packages/utils/composer.json` scripts assume package-local `vendor/bin/*`, which does not exist in current monorepo usage. `packages/utils/psalm.xml` likewise assumes package-local vendor path. This breaks package-level quality gates.

Repro:
- `cd packages/utils && composer run tests` -> `Could not open input file: vendor/bin/pest`
- `vendor/bin/psalm --config=packages/utils/psalm.xml` -> cannot resolve `packages/utils/vendor`

Expected outcomes:
Package-level tests/static-analysis commands are executable and reliable from both package and monorepo contexts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update scripts/config so `tests`, `phpstan`, and `psalm` run without path/bootstrap errors in repo layout.
- Ensure command failures represent real code problems, not tooling path assumptions.
- Document canonical commands for maintainers in package docs.
close_reason: Deprioritized by maintainer; no current release value for 2.0.0 hardening.

title: Clarify retry behavior for streaming requests
description:
Outcomes:
- Streaming retry behavior is explicit (documented and/or signaled) for consumers of PendingInference::stream().

Context:
- Problem: PendingInference::response() has retry logic, but consumers who call stream() directly get an InferenceStream with no retry handling; mid-stream failures just throw without guidance or events.
- Location: packages/polyglot/src/Inference/PendingInference.php (stream) and packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Streaming/InferenceStream.php.
- Suggested fix: document that streaming does not retry when using stream(), and/or emit a stream-failed event so callers can react; optionally add a retry wrapper for stream() if desired.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Stream failure behavior for stream() consumers is documented or emits a dedicated failure event.
- No unintended retries are introduced for streaming without explicit configuration.

How to Test:
- If adding an event, add a Pest test with a failing streamed driver and assert the failure event is dispatched once.
- If documenting only, verify docs mention streaming retry limitations.
close_reason: Documented that PendingInference::stream does not apply retry logic

title: ResponseIterators: StreamedToolCallStarted can be emitted multiple times for the same active tool
description:
## Problem and context
`EventTap::transitionToolStart()` emits `StreamedToolCallStarted` whenever a tool signal key is present, even if the same tool remains active across chunks.

Location:
- `packages/instructor/src/ResponseIterators/ModularPipeline/Events/EventTap.php`

Impact:
- Duplicate tool-start events in telemetry/UI.
- Harder consumer logic (must de-duplicate externally).

## Expected outcomes
- Start event is emitted exactly once per active tool call key.
- Update and completed events remain intact.
- Tool transitions between different keys still emit completion/start correctly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test proving duplicate start emissions for repeated same-key signals before fix.
- Fix transition logic to skip redundant start emission when active key unchanged.
- Keep existing EventTap tests passing; add/adjust tests for start/update/complete ordering.
- Confirm regression test passes after fix.

## Process spec (bug)
1. Add regression test that demonstrates duplicated start event.
2. Run tests to confirm failure on current code.
3. Implement fix in EventTap transition logic.
4. Re-run regression and related EventTap pipeline tests.
acceptance_criteria: Regression test first: show duplicate StreamedToolCallStarted for repeated tool signal chunks; fix start-transition guard; confirm exactly-once start emission per active tool key.
notes: Added regression in EventTapTest for repeated same-key tool signals; fixed EventTap::transitionToolStart() to skip duplicate start emission when active key unchanged; verified with pest packages/instructor/tests/Unit/ModularPipeline/EventTapTest.php
close_reason: Closed

- packages/agents/src/Tool/Traits/HasReflectiveSchema.php — return ToolDefinition
- All ToolInterface implementations with custom toToolSchema() (~15 classes)

## Current state
- ToolInterface::toToolSchema(): array returns ['type'=>'function','function'=>[...]]
- Tools::toToolSchema(): array returns array of those arrays
- ~15 tool classes override toToolSchema() with custom implementations (BashTool, SpawnSubagentTool, StructuredOutputTool, etc.)

## Changes
- ToolInterface::toToolSchema(): ToolDefinition
- HasReflectiveSchema trait: construct ToolDefinition($this->name(), $this->description(), $this->paramsJsonSchema())
- Tools::toToolSchema(): ToolDefinitions — wraps the mapped ToolDefinition objects
- Each custom toToolSchema() override: return new ToolDefinition(name, description, parameters) instead of array
- MockTool in tests updated

## Expected outcomes
- Fully typed from tool definition through to InferenceRequest
- No array-to-ToolDefinition conversion needed at the boundary — tools produce typed objects directly
- Tools collection returns ToolDefinitions that can be passed directly to InferenceRequest

## Acceptance criteria
1. ToolInterface::toToolSchema() returns ToolDefinition (not array)
2. Tools::toToolSchema() returns ToolDefinitions (not array)
3. HasReflectiveSchema produces ToolDefinition using name()/description()/paramsJsonSchema()
4. All ~15 custom toToolSchema() implementations updated
5. Agent tests pass (packages/agents/tests)
6. Tool schema tests (BashToolTest, ReadFileToolTest, etc.) updated to assert on ToolDefinition
close_reason: Completed: ToolInterface and agents Tools collection now emit ToolDefinition/ToolDefinitions, custom tool schema implementations updated, and full agents test suite passes.

title: [instructor] High: class-string schema providers with constructors crash in ResponseModelFactory
description:
## Problem and context
`ResponseModelFactory` supports class-string schema providers (`CanProvideJsonSchema` / `CanProvideSchema`) but instantiates them via `ReflectionClass::newInstanceWithoutConstructor()`.

If provider class has required constructor arguments and/or typed properties initialized in constructor, provider method access can crash with uninitialized-property errors.

### Repro
Class implementing `CanProvideJsonSchema` with constructor-promoted property:
- `ResponseModelFactory::fromAny(Provider::class)` throws:
  - `Error: Typed property ... must not be accessed before initialization`

### Why this matters
This breaks an advertised advanced extension path (`withResponseModel(class-string)` for schema providers) in a non-obvious way and yields low-quality runtime errors.

### Code context
- `packages/instructor/src/Creation/ResponseModelFactory.php`
  - provider class handling in `buildFrom()` / `resolveClassAndInstance()` / `makeInstance()`

## Expected outcomes
- Class-string provider handling is safe and explicit:
  - either support provider classes with constructors, or
  - fail early with clear message requiring instance input for non-default-constructible providers.
- Avoid uninitialized-property runtime errors from provider internals.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test for provider class-string with non-default constructor.
- Add deterministic behavior:
  - either successful model creation path, or
  - explicit `InvalidArgumentException` with actionable guidance.
- Keep existing behavior for provider instances and simple no-arg provider classes.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for class-string schema providers with required constructors, now fail fast with actionable InvalidArgumentException for class-string mode, while provider-instance and no-arg provider flows remain supported.

title: Implement Middleware-Based Hooks for Agent
description: Extend the proven middleware pattern to provide hook functionality for tool-level and step-level interception. This implements the design from research/2026-01-22-hooks/hooks-middleware-plan.md. Key components: ToolMiddleware stack for tool calls, convenience wrappers (BeforeToolMiddleware, AfterToolMiddleware), matchers for conditional execution, and AgentBuilder integration with syntactic sugar methods like onBeforeToolUse().
close_reason: Hooks middleware system implemented: ToolMiddleware interface, ToolMiddlewareStack, BeforeToolMiddleware, AfterToolMiddleware, ToolNameMatcher, CallableProcessor. AgentBuilder updated with onBeforeToolUse, onAfterToolUse, onBeforeStep, onAfterStep methods. All 47 middleware tests and 178 Agent tests passing.

title: Implement ToolMiddlewareStack
description:
Create packages/addons/src/Agent/Core/Middleware/ToolMiddlewareStack.php

Manages stack of middleware with priority-based ordering. Features:
- push(ToolMiddleware, priority) method
- process(ToolCall, AgentState, executor) builds chain and executes
- Higher priority = runs earlier (wraps outer)
- Uses SplPriorityQueue or similar for ordering

Reference implementation in hooks-middleware-plan.md:95-100

title: Config package: package-level quality scripts are not runnable in monorepo
description:
Problem and context:
`packages/config/composer.json` scripts assume package-local binaries (`vendor/bin/*`) and psalm config assumptions that do not exist in the monorepo workflow. This breaks package-level quality gates and makes release checks inconsistent.

Repro:
- `cd packages/config && composer run tests` -> `Could not open input file: vendor/bin/pest`
- `vendor/bin/psalm --config=packages/config/psalm.xml` fails to resolve package-local vendor path

Expected outcomes:
Package-local commands for tests and static analysis execute reliably in current repository layout and CI.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update scripts/config so package quality commands are executable from package context and monorepo root.
- Ensure `tests`, `phpstan`, and `psalm` commands fail only on real code issues, not path/config bootstrapping.
- Document canonical commands for maintainers in package docs.
close_reason: Made config package quality scripts runnable in monorepo (tests/phpstan/psalm), fixed package tool/config path assumptions, and documented canonical commands in packages/config/README.md.

title: [stream] Fix empty input handling in TransformationStream::from([])
description:
Problem
TransformationStream incorrectly treats an empty array input as if no input source was provided. This makes valid empty streams fail with InvalidArgumentException instead of producing an empty result.

Context
- packages/stream/src/TransformationStream.php:112 conditionally applies input only when $this->input !== []
- For TransformationStream::from([]), source is skipped and Transformation::execution() throws
- Repro: TransformationStream::from([])->getCompleted() throws InvalidArgumentException('Source is not set...')

Expected outcomes
- Empty iterables are treated as valid inputs, not as missing configuration.
- TransformationStream execution path works for empty arrays and returns an empty stream/result without exceptions.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests covering:
  1) TransformationStream::from([])->getIterator() yields []
  2) TransformationStream::from([])->getCompleted() follows the documented empty-result contract
- No InvalidArgumentException is thrown for empty-array input.
close_reason: Fixed: added empty-input regression tests and changed TransformationStream execution setup to always bind provided input (including []), so empty streams return empty iterator/completed outputs instead of throwing.

title: Events: decide and harden Event::toArray behavior for non-JSON payloads

Expected outcomes:

acceptance_criteria:

- Align implementation and phpstan expectations with chosen policy (no ambiguous return contract).
- No dedicated regression test required for `toArray()` per product direction; update docs/comments instead.
close_reason: Applied explicit policy: keep Event::toArray as best-effort informational helper. Simplified implementation to return jsonSerialize() snapshot (no empty-array degradation on non-JSON payloads) and documented non-JSON-safe contract explicitly in method docs.

title: [dynamic] Fresh package rewrite to new Structure/Builder/CallableSchemaFactory model
description:
Goal
Rewrite `packages/dynamic` from scratch around the new model:
- `Structure` as record (`Schema + array data`)
- `StructureBuilder` for ergonomic schema construction
- `CallableSchemaFactory` for function/method parameter schema generation

Context
Current dynamic API mixes schema definition, runtime values, reflection, validation and serialization in one mutable graph. We want a clean package reset with migration of external callsites.

Expected outcomes
- Legacy dynamic implementation is replaced with a clean, test-backed package.
- External runtime callsites use the new dynamic API.
- Monorepo tests and dynamic-related examples pass against new implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
- All epic child tasks are closed.
- `packages/dynamic` contains only new implementation and tests.
- No `packages/dynamic-old` directory remains.
- Monorepo `composer test` passes.
- Dynamic-related examples execute successfully.
notes:
Alignment note (confirmed):
1) `Structure` is the runtime record with core API: `schema()`, `data()`, `withData()`, `validate()`, `toArray()`.
2) `StructureBuilder` (and/or transitional static facade methods on `Structure`) provides ergonomic schema construction.
3) `CallableSchemaFactory` maps function/method signatures directly to `Schema`.
4) Transitional compatibility methods on `Structure` may exist, but delegate to builder and are marked transitional/deprecated.
close_reason: Dynamic rewrite epic completed: new package in place, callsites updated, tests/examples validated, legacy package removed.
