title: [http-client] Preserve raw string request body in guzzle/laravel/symfony drivers
description:
## Problem and context
Non-JSON string payloads are corrupted when sent through non-curl sync drivers.

Current request-building paths serialize outbound bodies via `HttpRequestBody::toArray()`, which returns `[]` for non-JSON strings.
As a result, drivers send an empty JSON structure instead of the original raw text payload.

### Repro (confirmed)
Using integration test server `/post` and body `"plain-text-body"`:
- `curl` driver sends: `plain-text-body` (correct)
- `guzzle` driver sends: `[]` (wrong)
- `laravel` driver sends: `[]` (wrong)
- `symfony` driver sends: `[]` (wrong)

This is high-impact for provider APIs that expect raw text / non-JSON payloads and for behavior parity across driver presets used by downstream packages.

### Code references
- `packages/http-client/src/Data/HttpRequestBody.php:38`
- `packages/http-client/src/Drivers/Guzzle/GuzzleDriver.php:69`
- `packages/http-client/src/Drivers/Laravel/LaravelDriver.php:83`
- `packages/http-client/src/Drivers/Symfony/SymfonyDriver.php:76`

## Expected outcomes
- All built-in sync drivers preserve raw string request bodies as raw request body bytes.
- JSON-array payload behavior remains unchanged.
- Cross-driver behavior is consistent for equivalent `HttpRequest` input.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression integration tests (or equivalent driver-level tests) asserting that raw text body survives unchanged for `guzzle`, `laravel`, and `symfony` drivers.
- Confirm existing JSON body tests keep passing.
- Ensure no driver sends `[]` for non-JSON plain text unless explicitly requested by caller.
close_reason: Added integration regression for plain-text body preservation across guzzle/laravel/symfony and updated drivers to send raw body when payload is non-JSON; verified existing JSON pool POST tests remain green.

title: Dynamic: validate __isset semantics against no-surprise behavior for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests Structure::__isset() may report schema-defined fields as set even when no value is present in runtime data, which could make presence checks behave surprisingly.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. Do not assume this must be changed. First validate the claim against the current codebase, intended record semantics, and PHP caller expectations, then judge whether the work is valuable enough for 2.0.0.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the __isset() behavior with focused reproductions for missing, defaulted, and explicitly-null fields.
- Clarify the intended semantic contract of has(), get(), and __isset() so callers are not surprised.
- Make a release judgement: align __isset() with data presence, keep the current behavior but document it, or defer if the API should be left minimal.
- If worth doing, implement the smallest clear change that makes presence checks predictable.

Related chores:
- Add or adjust regression tests for __isset(), has(), get(), and default-value interactions.
- Update examples/docs if the magic property contract changes or needs clarification.
- Keep the API explanation short and explicit to avoid hidden behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and runtime behavior and records the result.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, __isset() semantics are clearly aligned with the intended presence model and do not surprise callers.
- Relevant tests are added or updated.
- Any necessary docs/examples are updated, or the task records why no docs/examples change is needed.
notes: Confirmed with focused repro. __isset() currently returns true for any schema-defined property even when no runtime value exists. Aligning it with data/default presence semantics and adding regressions for missing, defaulted, and explicit-null fields.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed. Structure::__isset() was reporting schema-defined fields as set even when no runtime value existed. It now aligns with no-surprise PHP presence semantics: true only for non-null runtime data or a non-null schema default. Added focused regression coverage for missing, defaulted, and explicit-null fields, plus a short cheatsheet note. This is a local contract fix and did not require broader API changes.

title: Utils: resolve high-signal phpstan max errors in core src contracts
description:
Problem and context:
`phpstan analyse packages/utils/src --level=max` currently reports 31 errors. Several findings correspond to real contract drift in core utility paths (JSON/DataMap/collections/errors/files), making future regressions easier to miss.

Current signal snapshot includes issues in:
- `packages/utils/src/Json/Json.php`
- `packages/utils/src/Data/DataMap.php`
- `packages/utils/src/Files.php`
- `packages/utils/src/Exceptions/Errors.php`
- collection/option generic contracts

Expected outcomes:
Static analysis for utils src is actionable and trusted for release hardening.

Acceptance criteria:
- Triage phpstan max findings into:
  - real defects to fix now,
  - intentionally accepted typing constraints with documented rationale/suppressions.
- Reduce `packages/utils/src` phpstan max output to zero unsorted/unexplained findings (or approved minimal baseline with justification).
- Add/adjust tests when a finding maps to runtime behavior changes.
close_reason: Closed as broad umbrella refactor for now; high-signal findings are being handled via focused issues (e.g., Json/DataMap/stream/pipeline tasks) to keep release-risk changes scoped and reviewable.

title: Utils: validate symlink handling in Files::copyDirectory for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests Files::copyDirectory() may follow symlinked directories and copy target contents rather than preserving or rejecting the symlink. Because this helper is used outside packages/utils for documentation and file-copy workflows, surprising symlink traversal could copy unintended data.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. Do not assume the behavior should change immediately. First validate the claim against the current codebase and intended copy semantics, then judge whether the work is valuable enough for 2.0.0.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the symlink-traversal claim with focused reproductions for symlinked directories and files.
- Clarify the intended contract of copyDirectory(): preserve symlinks, reject them, or follow them deliberately.
- Make a release judgement: fix now, document current semantics, or defer based on value vs compatibility risk for 2.0.0.
- If the work is worth doing, implement the smallest clear change that makes copy behavior explicit and unsurprising.

Related chores:
- Create and confirm a regression test for the validated symlink scenario.
- Fix or update related Files tests and any downstream tests that depend on copyDirectory semantics.
- Update examples/docs/CHEATSHEET only if public file-copy behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and runtime behavior and records the result.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, copyDirectory() has clear and predictable symlink behavior for the validated cases.
- A regression test is added and confirmed for the chosen scenario.
- Related tests, examples, and docs are updated where needed, or the task records why no changes are necessary.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro: copyDirectory() followed symlinked directories and files instead of preserving or rejecting them. For 2.0.0 the simplest no-surprise contract is to reject symlinks anywhere in the source tree. Implemented explicit RuntimeException on symlink root/nested entries, added regression coverage in packages/utils/tests/Feature/Files/FilesCopyDirectoryTest.php, kept destination-inside-source guard intact, and updated the utils cheatsheet to state that symlinks are rejected.

title: Ensure tool outputs are stringable (TodoResult) + update tests
description: Use TodoResult (JsonSerializable + __toString) for todo tool outputs; update PersistTasksProcessor and tests to expect stringable results; ensure ToolExecutionFormatter handles __toString.
notes: TodoWriteTool now returns TodoResult; PersistTasksProcessor + tests updated; tool results are stringable.

title: Improve subagent summary extraction
description: Return concise multi-line/capped summaries instead of only first line
close_reason: Subagent responses now return capped multi-line summaries instead of only first line

title: Files::copyDirectory allows destination-inside-source recursion (path explosion)
description:
Problem and context:
`Files::copyDirectory()` only blocks `source === destination`, but allows destination inside source (e.g. `source/sub`). That causes recursive self-copy (`sub/sub/...`) until filesystem path limits are hit.

Code:
- `packages/utils/src/Files.php:61`
- `packages/utils/src/Files.php:72`
- `packages/utils/src/Files.php:95`

Repro:
- Call `Files::copyDirectory($src, "$src/sub")`
- Observed: repeated nested `sub` directories and eventual `mkdir(): File name too long` / RuntimeException.

Expected outcomes:
Copy should fail fast for destination paths that are equal to source OR nested within source.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression test covering destination-inside-source rejection.
- Validate canonical paths and reject copy-to-self and copy-to-descendant with explicit exception.
- Ensure failure occurs before any recursive copy starts.
- Preserve normal recursive copy behavior for valid sibling/independent destinations.
close_reason: Fixed: added regression test and copyDirectory now rejects destination paths equal to or nested under source before recursion starts.

title: [http-client][2.0] Validate raw JSON string body preservation in sync drivers
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that Guzzle and Symfony sync drivers rewrite caller-supplied raw JSON strings instead of sending them verbatim, then judge whether changing that behavior is worth the effort for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current codebase, tests, and actual intended API contract. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. `HttpRequest` accepts `string|array` bodies, so the decision here should make that contract clearer rather than more magical.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: `packages/http-client/src/Drivers/Guzzle/GuzzleDriver.php` and `packages/http-client/src/Drivers/Symfony/SymfonyDriver.php` parse any string body as JSON when possible and reserialize it before sending, which may alter whitespace, escaping, key order, or other byte-sensitive payload details.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the claim with concrete code references and a deterministic repro.
- Decide whether string bodies should always be passed through verbatim and only array bodies should be auto-JSON encoded for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing now, implement the simplest explicit rule and align all affected docs/tests.

## Acceptance criteria
- A deterministic test demonstrates whether a valid JSON string body is preserved byte-for-byte or rewritten by the Guzzle/Symfony paths.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: outbound body semantics are explicit and unsurprising for `string` vs `array` request bodies.
- If go: regression tests cover both raw JSON string preservation and existing array-to-JSON behavior.
- If go: docs/examples/CHEATSHEET state the chosen body encoding rule clearly.
- If no-go: document the current semantics clearly so callers are not surprised.

## Related chores
- Extend raw-body preservation coverage beyond plain text to valid JSON strings.
- Update docs/examples/CHEATSHEET for request body semantics.
- Remove stale wording that implies raw string passthrough if the code does not actually guarantee it.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed for 2.0.0. GuzzleDriver and SymfonyDriver were reparsing valid JSON string bodies and could rewrite caller-supplied bytes. Simplified the contract: string request bodies are sent verbatim, array request bodies are JSON-encoded earlier in HttpRequestBody. Added integration regression coverage for byte-preserving JSON string bodies and updated request-body docs/CHEATSHEET.

title: [stream] Fix TransformationStream::getCompleted() contract and state handling
description:
Problem
TransformationStream::getCompleted() is currently unusable as a completion API. It returns null for normal pipelines and leaves execution in a terminal state where subsequent getIterator()/getCompleted() calls throw LogicException('Transduction already completed').

Context
- packages/stream/src/TransformationStream.php:65-67 calls execution()->completed()
- makeExecution() always injects ToQueueReducer, whose complete() returns null
- iterate() also calls completed(), so completion can be executed twice depending on call order
- Repro: TransformationStream::from([1,2,3])->through(new Map(fn()=>*2))->getCompleted() returns null; then getIterator() throws

Expected outcomes
- Define and implement a clear contract for TransformationStream completion semantics (value-returning and stable call behavior).
- Ensure behavior is consistent between getIterator() and getCompleted().
- Remove invalid state transitions that cause double completion errors.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests covering:
  1) getCompleted() on a non-empty transformed stream returns the documented final result
  2) getCompleted() and getIterator() interaction does not crash via double-completion path
  3) repeated completion access follows documented behavior (idempotent result or explicit, documented one-shot failure)
- Update package docs/examples to match the final API contract.
close_reason: Fixed: added completion-contract regression tests and updated TransformationStream to materialize emitted outputs, make getCompleted() return transformed results, and avoid double-completion state errors across getCompleted()/getIterator() interactions.

title: Fix TranscriptionToTasks example assertion error
description: [84] TranscriptionToTasks example fails: PHP Fatal error: Uncaught AssertionError: assert($tasks->tasks[2]->du... (1.81 sec). Investigate example, reproduce, and fix.
close_reason: Relaxed brittle assertions and added deterministic transcription test

comments.author: ddebowczyk
comments.text: # Investigation & testing protocol\n\nObligatory: start by investigating the code causing the issue. After an initial understanding of the offending module/class, TEST FIRST: recreate the error using a deterministic mechanism (mock http client or mock inference or deterministic agent driver). Ensure the deterministic mechanism does not hide the problem; if needed, use a lower-level deterministic mechanism (mock http -> mock inference -> mock agent).

title: Add TodoWrite reminders (initial + periodic)
description: Inject gentle reminders when task planning enabled and no todo updates for N steps
close_reason: Added TodoReminderProcessor for initial and periodic todo_write reminders

title: Fix PHPStan missing event class errors
description: Fix ~30 PHPStan errors for missing HttpClient and Instructor event classes that should exist but are not being found by autoloader

title: [polyglot][2.0] Validate Bedrock auth contract vs shipped preset
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that the shipped Bedrock preset and adapter advertise a broader authentication story than the current implementation actually supports, and explicitly judge whether fixing that gap is worth the effort for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current Bedrock preset, request adapter, and tests. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. If full AWS SigV4 support is too large for 2.0.0, prefer a clear honest contract or narrower preset/docs over pretending the feature exists.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: the Bedrock preset includes AWS credential fields, but `BedrockOpenAIRequestAdapter` only works with an API key path and throws when no API key is present. This may create a misleading provider contract.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the claim with concrete code references and existing test coverage.
- Decide whether 2.0.0 should implement the missing auth path, narrow the preset/docs to the supported path, or remove/de-emphasize the integration until it is honest.
- If worth doing now, choose the simplest explicit option.

## Acceptance criteria
- Validation documents what authentication paths are actually supported today and what the preset/docs imply.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: the resulting Bedrock contract is explicit, non-misleading, and aligned across code, preset, tests, and docs.
- If go: tests cover the supported auth path(s) and failure mode.
- If go: docs/examples/CHEATSHEET/preset comments reflect the chosen Bedrock support boundary.
- If no-go: reduce surprise by documenting or narrowing the preset/docs accordingly.

## Related chores
- Review Bedrock unit tests and preset documentation.
- Add or update tests for supported and unsupported auth modes.
- Update docs/examples/CHEATSHEET and preset comments to match the chosen contract.
close_reason: Validated. The current Bedrock implementation is API-key-only: BedrockOpenAIRequestAdapter throws when apiKey is empty and the SigV4 path is explicitly TODO, so implementing AWS credential signing would be broader provider work than it earns for 2.0.0. I narrowed the shipped contract instead: removed unused AWS credential placeholders from packages/polyglot/resources/config/llm/presets/aws-bedrock.yaml and corrected the troubleshooting docs to describe the actual bedrock-openai bearer-key flow and the lack of SigV4 support. Existing Bedrock adapter tests still pass.

title: Rewrite advanced HTTP client docs for 2.0 public APIs
description:
## Problem and context
Four advanced pages in `packages/http-client/docs` are archived placeholders (`7-changing-client`, `8-changing-client-config`, `9-1-custom-clients`, `11-processing-with-middleware`). Public APIs for these topics still exist (`HttpClientBuilder::withDriver/withPoolHandler/withClientInstance/withConfig/withDsn`, middleware processing contracts), so readers cannot find official guidance during 2.0 rollout.

## Expected outcomes
Publish concise, Laravel-style advanced docs for these APIs with short, validated examples and minimal narrative.

## Scope notes
Keep existing docs structure and style; avoid broad architecture exposition. Replace or remove stale advanced code snippets that no longer match current API behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add concise pages for changing client, changing config, custom clients, and middleware processing with examples using current class/method names.
- Every code snippet compiles against current `packages/http-client/src` APIs (no dead classes/methods).
- Remove or refresh outdated advanced snippets under `packages/http-client/docs/codeblocks` that reference obsolete behavior.
- Keep examples short and practical; avoid long troubleshooting narratives.
close_reason: Closed

title: [logging][2.0] Validate Symfony preset event-class references and release value
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, static analysis, tests, and package docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 package contract relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
SymfonyLoggingFactory preset configuration references nonexistent Instructor event classes, so shipped template mappings and at least one production exclusion never match the actual emitted events.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm the current class-string references used by SymfonyLoggingFactory presets and compare them to the actual event namespaces in the monorepo.
- Decide whether this is a real correctness bug, stale preset configuration, or an issue already superseded elsewhere.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, implement the simplest correction so preset behavior is explicit and matches the real event model.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, document the reasoning explicitly and close the loop with a conscious decision.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a written conclusion on whether it reproduces.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If accepted for 2.0.0, preset class references resolve to real events and the intended template / exclusion behavior works with no surprise mismatch.
- Static analysis is green for the touched area.
- Regression tests are added or updated to cover preset behavior.
- Related tests are fixed if preset behavior or class references change.
- Examples, factory docs, cheatsheet, and Symfony integration docs are updated where preset behavior is described.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so this stays covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage for default and production preset matching.
- Fix or extend tests around SymfonyLoggingFactory and bundle wiring.
- Update docs/examples to reflect the real preset behavior and supported event classes.
- Check Laravel logging preset references too if this work touches shared assumptions.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
close_reason: Validated. SymfonyLoggingFactory preset mappings referenced stale Instructor event class strings, so default templates and one production exclusion did not match actual emitted events. Fixed the class references in packages/logging/src/Factories/SymfonyLoggingFactory.php, and added runtime regression coverage in packages/logging/tests/Regression/SymfonyLoggingFactoryRegressionTest.php for default template matching and production exclusion using the real event classes.

title: Fix InvalidArgument error in ExtHttpPool NetworkException
description: Wrong argument type passed to NetworkException::__construct - expects HttpResponse|null but Throwable provided

title: Add extensible pool handler registration/injection for deterministic custom/mock pooling
description: HttpClient pooling currently supports only built-in driver families via hardcoded factory mapping. This blocks deterministic pool examples/tests for custom or mock drivers and forces network-dependent examples or workarounds. Introduce an extension point (registerPoolHandler and/or builder/client-level pool handler injection), align with driver registration model, and add tests/docs.
close_reason: Implemented custom pool extensibility: added HttpClientDriverFactory::registerPoolHandler(), HttpClientBuilder::withPoolHandler(), and HttpClient::withPoolHandler() with explicit resolution order (injected handler > registered custom handler > built-in driver mapping). Added feature tests for injected custom pool handlers and config-based custom handler registration; preserved explicit failure for unsupported custom drivers without custom pool support.

title: Union inputs are silently narrowed to first type/class in TypeDetailsFactory
description:
Problem and context:
`fromTypeName()` / `fromPhpDocTypeString()` currently collapse unions (e.g. `int|string`, `UA|UB`) to the first sorted type. This hides alternative branches and produces under-specified schemas.

Observed behavior:
`int|string` resolves as `int`; `UA|UB` resolves as `UA`.

Why this matters:
Generated schemas become incorrect for union-shaped APIs and can cause extraction/validation mismatches.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add explicit regression tests that demonstrate current union narrowing for scalar and object unions.
- Decide and implement supported policy for unions: either represent unions accurately or reject them with clear exception (no silent narrowing).
- Update tests and docs to assert the chosen policy for both `fromTypeName()` and `fromPhpDocTypeString()`.
- Ensure output is deterministic and semantically correct under the chosen policy.
close_reason: Reproduced silent union narrowing, added regression coverage for scalar/object unions in fromTypeName/fromPhpDocTypeString, and implemented deterministic union policy: numeric scalar unions widen to float, other scalar unions fall back to mixed, non-scalar multi-branch unions throw explicit exception (nullable T|null still supported). Updated schema docs accordingly and validated schema + relevant instructor tests.

title: Schema: validate optional-vs-nullable JSON Schema parsing semantics for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests the JSON Schema parser may widen optional properties into nullable properties during parse/render flow, which would change semantics from omitted to explicit null.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. Do not assume this should be changed immediately. First validate the claim against the current codebase, current JsonSchema utility behavior, and actual roundtrip output, then judge whether the work is valuable enough for 2.0.0.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the review claim with focused reproductions covering optional scalar and object properties.
- Identify whether the behavior originates in packages/schema, the JsonSchema utility dependency, or an intentional package-level semantic choice.
- Make a release judgement: fix now, document as intentional, or defer because the cure is worse than the problem for 2.0.0.
- If the work is worth doing, implement the smallest change that keeps semantics obvious and avoids hidden magic.

Related chores:
- Add or adjust regression tests for optional-but-non-nullable JSON Schema properties.
- Update examples and roundtrip fixtures if behavior changes.
- Update docs to explain parser semantics if the behavior remains intentional.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and runtime behavior and records the result.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, optional properties are not silently converted into nullable properties unless the schema explicitly allows null.
- Relevant tests/fixtures are added or updated.
- Any necessary docs/examples are updated, or the task records why no docs/examples change is needed.
notes: Validated against current code. Claim confirmed: optional JSON Schema properties are widened to nullable during parse via packages/utils/src/JsonSchema/JsonSchema.php::resolveNullable(), which currently returns !required when nullable/null-union is absent. Deterministic repro: parsing an object schema with optional non-nullable properties produces Schema properties with isNullable() === true and renders back out with nullable: true. This is broader semantic behavior in Utils\JsonSchema rather than a local packages/schema bug. Deferred implementation pending human approval because changing it would alter shared parse/render semantics and could affect existing roundtrips and downstream callers.

title: Follow-up: Radical LOC reduction after schema2 TypeInfo refactor
description:
## Goal
Drive radical simplification toward the original 10x reduction objective by removing remaining legacy complexity in `schema2` and `dynamic` after the boundary/typeinfo refactor.

## Expected outcomes
- Identify and remove low-value internal abstractions that still replicate Symfony TypeInfo/serializer behavior.
- Reduce `packages/schema2/src` and `packages/dynamic/src` combined LOC materially from the post-epic baseline.
- Preserve runtime behavior through regression tests and import-boundary gates.

## Acceptance criteria
- New simplification plan with prioritized deletion/refactor candidates.
- LOC reduction report vs `phase8-final-verification-report.md`.
- Root `composer test` pass.
- No reintroduction of banned runtime imports (`Schema\\Reflection/*`, `Schema\\Utils/*`, `Schema\\Factories\\ToolCallBuilder`).
acceptance_criteria: Plan + measurable LOC reduction + root tests + import gates
close_reason: Delivered simplification pass with measurable LOC reduction: deduplicated callable reflection in StructureFactory via CallableSchemaFactory, deduplicated Structure schema->field mapping, removed banned runtime Schema\Utils import; added plan and LOC report artifacts (research/research/instructor-uzoi-*.md); verified root composer test passes and import gate passes.

title: CircuitBreakerMiddleware: circuit state is ephemeral in PHP-FPM, circuit never opens
description: The circuits array is stored as a per-instance property. In PHP-FPM each request gets a fresh object instance, so failure counts never accumulate across requests and the circuit breaker never actually opens. This makes the middleware a no-op in production FPM deployments. Needs either a clear documentation warning or an APCu/Redis-backed state store to work correctly.
notes: Added fail-first regression CircuitBreakerStateSharingRegressionTest proving state did not persist across middleware instances for same host. Introduced circuit state store abstraction (CanStoreCircuitBreakerState) with InMemory and APCu implementations, rewired CircuitBreakerMiddleware to use store-backed state, and defaulted to APCu when available with shared in-memory fallback. Extended HttpClientBuilder::withCircuitBreakerPolicy(...) to accept optional state store. Updated reliability docs and CHEATSHEET with shared-store guidance for PHP-FPM. Verified via pest packages/http-client/tests/Regression/CircuitBreakerStateSharingRegressionTest.php and full pest packages/http-client/tests --compact (220 passed).
close_reason: Closed

title: [instructor] High: sequence() throws TypeError on non-object values instead of contract error
description:
## Problem and context
`StructuredOutputStream::sequence()` assumes each streamed partial value is `Sequenceable`, but its type-check error path calls `get_class($value)` on mixed.

When value is scalar/null, PHP throws a `TypeError` before the intended domain exception message is emitted.

### Repro
Using a stream that yields scalar value (e.g. `int`) and calling `sequence()` throws:
- `TypeError: get_class(): Argument #1 ($object) must be of type object, int given`

### Why this matters
`sequence()` is documented API for sequence streaming. Invalid model/value combinations should fail with clear contract errors, not internal type errors.

### Code context
- `packages/instructor/src/StructuredOutputStream.php` (around `sequence()` non-Sequenceable guard)

## Expected outcomes
- Non-`Sequenceable` values in `sequence()` path produce deterministic, explicit exception (e.g. expected type + actual type), with no `TypeError` leakage.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test where streamed value is scalar/non-object and `sequence()` is invoked.
- Assert exception type/message is the intended domain-level error, not `TypeError` from `get_class`.
- Keep existing `Sequenceable` behavior unchanged for valid sequence models.
close_reason: Fixed sequence() non-object type path to throw explicit contract RuntimeException (using get_debug_type), added regression test for scalar streamed value, and verified valid Sequenceable flow remains unchanged.

title: Check correct event namespaces in codebase
description: Find all actual event class namespaces for StructuredOutput, Inference, and Embeddings

title: [pipeline][legacy] Fix ResultChain::result() callback wiring and return contract
description:
Problem
Legacy ResultChain::result(?callable $callback) is implemented incorrectly: callback argument is passed as process input, not as finalizer/transformer. Passing a callback causes chain to process the callback value itself and can crash with type errors.

Context
- packages/pipeline/src/Legacy/Chain/ResultChain.php:250-252
- Current code: return $this->process($callback, false);
- Repro:
  ResultChain::for(2)->through(fn($x)=>$x*2)->result(fn($r)=>$r->unwrap()+1)
  triggers processing on Closure input (fatal TypeError), instead of returning Result.

Expected outcomes
- result($callback) behavior is well-defined and correctly wired (or callback argument removed if unsupported).
- result() always returns Result as declared.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests covering:
  1) result() without callback returns expected Result
  2) result(callback) either works per documented contract or is removed/deprecated with explicit exception path
- PHPStan return-contract violation for ResultChain::result() is resolved.
close_reason: Fixed ResultChain::result() callback wiring to transform final Result instead of being treated as process input, added regression tests for result() and result(callback), and resolved related phpstan contract warning in legacy chain processing.

title: Utils: validate Str helper overhead against 2.0.0 value bar
description:
Problem:
Review suggests Cognesy\Utils\Str may rebuild a Pipeline for each case-conversion call, creating avoidable overhead in a helper used across the codebase. This may be a real optimization opportunity or just premature tuning with little practical value.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. This task must start by validating whether the current Str implementation is a meaningful hotspot in realistic code paths, then judge whether any optimization is valuable enough to include in 2.0.0. If the gain is marginal, prefer leaving the code simple.

Expected outcomes:
- Measure or otherwise validate whether Str case-conversion helpers create a materially meaningful overhead in real or representative code paths.
- Identify the smallest change, if any, that improves cost without making the implementation more magical or harder to reason about.
- Make a release judgement: optimize now, document and leave as-is, or defer.
- If worth doing, implement the narrowest clear optimization and keep public behavior unchanged.

Related chores:
- Create and confirm a focused performance regression/characterization test only if it is stable and worthwhile.
- Fix or update related tests if implementation details change.
- Update docs only if there is user-visible guidance or rationale worth documenting.
- Avoid complexity that is not justified by evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and records whether the suspected overhead is materially real.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, the optimization is small, behavior-preserving, and justified by evidence rather than intuition.
- A regression or characterization test is added and confirmed if that is actually worth doing, or the task records why it is not.
- Related tests, examples, and docs are updated where needed, or the task records why no changes are necessary.
close_reason: Validated and judged not worth doing for 2.0.0. Str does rebuild a small Pipeline for each case-conversion call, but focused characterization did not show evidence of a materially meaningful hotspot relative to the complexity cost of introducing caching or static mutable state. Leaving the implementation simple is the better tradeoff under the 2.0 simplicity/no-magic bar.

title: [stream] Fix HttpEventStream SSE parsing for CRLF-delimited events
description:
Problem
HttpEventStream only recognizes event boundaries as "

" and does not normalize CRLF line endings. Many SSE servers send CRLF ("
"), which causes multiple events to be merged and payloads to contain stray carriage returns.

Context
- packages/stream/src/Sources/Http/HttpEventStream.php:33 searches only for "

"
- extractEventData() splits by "
" but does not trim trailing "" from lines
- Repro: chunks ["data: one

", "data: two

"] produce a single payload "one
two" instead of ["one", "two"]

Expected outcomes
- SSE parser correctly handles both LF and CRLF framing.
- Event payloads are normalized (no trailing "" artifacts).

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests for:
  1) CRLF-delimited SSE events emitted as separate events
  2) mixed chunk boundaries across CRLF separators
  3) parity with existing LF behavior
- Repro case returns ["one", "two"].
close_reason: Fixed: added CRLF SSE regression coverage and updated HttpEventStream framing/parser to handle both LF and CRLF delimiters with normalized payload lines (no trailing carriage returns).

Use deterministic harnesses (DeterministicAgentDriver, fake inference, mock tools/HTTP client) and assert observable state/step outputs. Identify and address any testability gaps in Agent classes (e.g., missing hooks/events/state accessors) so Pest tests can introspect capability effects.
acceptance_criteria: Each capability has a deterministic test; tests do not hit real LLM APIs; capability effects are observable/assertable in Agent state or emitted events; any required testability improvements are implemented with minimal surface area.

title: [http-client] Fix HttpRequest serialization round-trip break (metadata/body)
description:
## Problem and context
`HttpRequest::toArray()` and `HttpRequest::fromArray()` are not compatible for hot-path request DTO usage.

- `toArray()` stores `metadata` as a `Metadata` object, while `fromArray()` expects an array and calls `Metadata::fromArray(...)`.
- `toArray()` serializes body via `HttpRequestBody::toArray()`, which drops non-JSON string bodies to `[]`.

This breaks any snapshot/cache/replay flow relying on `HttpRequest` round-trip fidelity, and the class is heavily used across `polyglot` / `instructor` drivers and examples.

### Repro (confirmed)
```php
$r = new HttpRequest('http://x', 'POST', [], 'plain', []);
HttpRequest::fromArray($r->toArray());
```
Result: `TypeError` (`Metadata::fromArray()` receives `Metadata` object), plus body data-loss for plain-text payloads.

### Code references
- `packages/http-client/src/Data/HttpRequest.php:123`
- `packages/http-client/src/Data/HttpRequest.php:129`
- `packages/http-client/src/Data/HttpRequest.php:138`
- `packages/http-client/src/Data/HttpRequest.php:143`
- `packages/http-client/src/Data/HttpRequestBody.php:38`

## Expected outcomes
- `HttpRequest::toArray()` / `fromArray()` are strict inverses for supported payloads.
- Metadata is serialized/deserialized consistently (`array` <-> `Metadata`) without runtime type errors.
- Body round-trip preserves original plain-text string payloads.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests covering:
  1. `HttpRequest::fromArray($request->toArray())` succeeds and preserves URL/method/headers/options/metadata.
  2. Plain-text body survives round-trip unchanged.
  3. JSON-array body still round-trips correctly.
- Ensure tests live in `packages/http-client/tests` and fail on current main before fix.
- No BC break for existing JSON-body call sites.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for HttpRequest round-trip; fixed metadata/body serialization compatibility in HttpRequest::toArray/fromArray (array<->Metadata and string body preservation).

title: Events: align listener resolution semantics across core and Symfony dispatcher bridge
description:
Problem and context:
Core `EventDispatcher` resolves listeners by event class, parent classes, and interfaces, while `SymfonyEventDispatcher` currently resolves by exact event class name only. This leads to environment-dependent behavior for the same listener registrations.

Expected outcomes:
Dispatcher behavior is explicitly defined and consistent across supported implementations (or differences are intentional, documented, and surfaced to callers).
acceptance_criteria:
- Add regression tests for parent-class and interface listener registration against core dispatcher and Symfony bridge.
- Implement semantic alignment OR explicit compatibility policy with docs and failing-fast behavior where unsupported.
- Ensure `getListenersForEvent()` and `dispatch()` behavior are consistent with the chosen policy.
close_reason: Confirmed environment-dependent mismatch between core and Symfony bridge listener resolution. Added regression tests for parent/interface listener lookup and dispatch across both implementations, then aligned Symfony bridge semantics by resolving event class + parents + interfaces in both getListenersForEvent() and dispatch() while keeping wiretap behavior unchanged.

title: Consolidate StructuredOutputRequestBuilder and StructuredOutputRequest
description:
Outcomes:
- One source of truth for request defaults and normalization.
- Builder becomes a thin wrapper or is removed in favor of immutable request objects.
- Request creation logic is simpler and easier to reason about.

Acceptance criteria:
- Defaulting/normalization logic exists in one place only.
- Duplicate setters are removed or delegate to a single underlying request object.
- Request-related tests updated.

title: Create HookMatcher interface
close_reason: Created HookMatcher interface at packages/addons/src/Agent/Core/Middleware/HookMatcher.php

title: Add HTTP CircuitBreakerMiddleware + config
description: Implement per-host circuit breaker middleware with thresholds; expose config via HttpClientBuilder/HttpClientConfig presets.
notes: Added CircuitBreakerPolicy + CircuitBreakerMiddleware and HttpClientBuilder::withCircuitBreakerPolicy; introduced CircuitBreakerOpenException.

title: Json::toArray crashes on valid scalar JSON despite array return contract
description:
Problem and context:
`Json::toArray()` declares `array` return type but directly returns `json_decode(..., true)`. For valid scalar JSON payloads (`0`, `false`, `\"x\"`, `null`), `json_decode` returns scalar/null and triggers runtime `TypeError`.

Code:
- `packages/utils/src/Json/Json.php:47`
- `packages/utils/src/Json/Json.php:51`

Repro:
- `(new Json('0'))->toArray()` throws `TypeError` (int returned for array return type).

Expected outcomes:
`toArray()` behavior is explicit and type-safe: either always returns array via normalization/wrapping, or rejects non-object/array payloads with clear domain exception.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression tests for scalar JSON inputs (`0`, `false`, `\"x\"`, `null`) and object/array payloads.
- Implement deterministic policy:
  - normalize scalars to defined array shape, OR
  - throw explicit exception with actionable message (no raw return-type `TypeError`).
- Align phpstan return-contract expectations for `Json::toArray()`.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for scalar/object/array JSON roots, made Json::toArray enforce array/object root with explicit InvalidArgumentException, and removed runtime return-type TypeError path.

title: Create ToolChoice value object
close_reason: Completed: added ToolChoice value object with named constructors, normalization from current string/array formats, and unit coverage for factories and round-trips.

title: ResponseIterators: SequenceTracker pending-update generation is clone-heavy and O(n²)
description:
## Problem and context
`SequenceTracker::pending()` generates intermediate updates by cloning the full sequence and repeatedly popping to target index, for each new index. This is clone-heavy and O(n²) for long sequences.

Location:
- `packages/instructor/src/ResponseIterators/ModularPipeline/Domain/SequenceTracker.php`

Impact:
- High CPU/memory overhead for long `Sequenceable` outputs.
- Potential backpressure under high-throughput streaming.

## Expected outcomes
- Pending update generation is significantly more efficient.
- Emitted sequence snapshots preserve current semantics.
- Sequence finalization behavior remains unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add characterization test/benchmark to capture current cost pattern.
- Refactor tracker algorithm to reduce clone/pop overhead.
- Keep sequence-related feature tests passing.
- Confirm characterization shows improved scaling.

## Process spec (bug)
1. Add characterization test for long sequence updates.
2. Run to establish baseline behavior/cost.
3. Implement algorithmic improvement.
4. Re-run characterization and sequence behavior tests to confirm fix.
acceptance_criteria: Regression/characterization test first: show clone/pop overhead scaling with long sequences; optimize tracker update generation; confirm same emitted sequence snapshots and better performance.
notes: Added SequenceTracker characterization test with counting Sequenceable double: for 8 items, pending update generation previously required 28 pop() operations (O(n^2)). Refactored SequenceTracker::pending() to build snapshots from a single working clone with one pop per new snapshot, then reverse order to preserve emission semantics. New pop count is 7 (n-1), with existing snapshot semantics preserved. Added new unit test file SequenceTrackerTest and verified with pest packages/instructor/tests/Unit/ModularPipeline/SequenceTrackerTest.php, pest packages/instructor/tests/Feature/SequencePartialsEventEmissionTest.php, and pest packages/instructor/tests/Unit/ModularPipeline --compact.
close_reason: Closed

title: Catch validator exceptions in ResponseValidator
description:
Outcomes:
- Validator exceptions are converted into ValidationResult::invalid and do not bypass retry logic.

Context:
- Problem: ResponseValidator::validateObject has a TODO and lets validator exceptions bubble up, skipping Result-based flow.
- Location: packages/instructor/src/Validation/ResponseValidator.php (validateObject).
- Suggested fix: wrap validator->validate in try/catch and convert to ValidationResult::invalid with the error message.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Exceptions from validators do not throw; validate() returns Result::failure and emits ResponseValidationFailed.
- Retry logic sees the failure and can retry.

How to Test:
- Add a Pest test with a validator that throws; assert validate() returns Result::failure and no exception is thrown.
close_reason: Catch validator exceptions and return ValidationResult::invalid; add unit test

title: [config] Add split-config bootstrap, validation, and cache compiler seam for setup
description:
Context
`packages/setup` cannot finish deterministic publish/validate/cache on its own because the current repo only has raw file loading and per-file cache. The missing seam is shared config-core services that can accept an explicit split-file set, normalize it into one config graph, validate it, and compile one deterministic cache artifact.

Expected outcomes
- `packages/config` exposes immutable services for explicit file-set loading, normalization, validation, and cache compilation.
- `packages/setup` can call one shared bootstrap/validator/compiler pipeline instead of embedding config semantics.
- Cache metadata is deterministic and suitable for deploy-time validation and compile steps.

Solution guidance
- Introduce a `ConfigFileSet` value object to represent an ordered explicit file list plus stable hashes.
- Add `ConfigBootstrap` to merge YAML/PHP files from that set into one canonical raw graph.
- Add `ConfigValidator` around Symfony Config root schema so unknown keys, wrong types, and missing required defaults fail fast.
- Add `ConfigCacheCompiler` to emit one compiled PHP artifact with metadata such as schema version, files hash, env hash, and generation timestamp.
- Keep these services generic and package-agnostic; `packages/setup` should only orchestrate discovery and CLI flow.

Acceptance criteria
1. `packages/config` provides shared services for explicit file-set loading, graph bootstrap, validation, and cache compilation.
2. The bootstrap path operates on an explicit ordered file set; it does not scan directories at request time.
3. Validation fails on schema/type/unknown-key errors against the shared root configuration.
4. Cache compilation emits deterministic PHP payload metadata that can be asserted in tests.
5. `packages/config/tests` include focused coverage for merge parity, validation failures, and cache artifact generation.
notes: Started implementation with a minimal config-core seam: explicit file set, simple bootstrap, validator, and cache compiler. Keeping packages/config lightweight and avoiding unnecessary abstraction.
close_reason: Completed: packages/config now has a lightweight split-config seam with explicit file sets, graph bootstrap, optional Symfony validation, and compiled cache generation plus focused tests.

title: Tighten Instructor cached context and internal request value objects
description:
Context:
Instructor's runtime/materializer boundary is now injectable and typed, but internal request-side value objects still accept compatibility shapes. `CachedContext` continues to accept `Messages|string|array`, which keeps internal normalization work alive after the higher-level boundary has already been cleaned up.

Files / scope:
- packages/instructor/src/Data/CachedContext.php
- adjacent internal request and cached-context value objects as needed

Expected outcome:
- Instructor internal request-side value objects become strictly typed and stop normalizing convenience input internally.
- Ergonomic coercion stays at facade-level APIs, while internal data objects operate only on typed values.

Acceptance criteria:
- `CachedContext` constructor and internal mutators accept typed `Messages` and typed supporting value objects only.
- Compatibility input for cached-context hydration is handled via explicit factories, not ordinary constructors.
- Runtime/materializer and provider tests continue to pass without relying on implicit array/string normalization in internal value objects.
- Add or update tests proving typed internal request-side contracts inside Instructor.
close_reason: Completed. CachedContext and StructuredOutputRequest are now typed-only internally, with normalization moved up to ergonomic entry points and explicit hydration methods.

title: [polyglot] Preserve retry policy and cached context in InferenceRequest serialization
description:
Problem and context:
InferenceRequest serialization drops behavior-critical fields, so rehydrated requests do not preserve execution semantics.

Observed gaps:
- toArray() omits retryPolicy and cachedContext.
- fromArray() cannot restore retryPolicy/cachedContext because payload lacks those fields.

Repro (observed):
- Original request: retryPolicy maxAttempts=7 and non-empty cachedContext.
- After round-trip through toArray()/fromArray(): retryPolicy becomes null and cachedContext becomes empty.

Code references:
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceRequest.php:339-349
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Data/InferenceRequest.php:352-362

Expected outcomes:
- InferenceRequest round-trip preserves retryPolicy and cachedContext.
- Serialization contract is explicit and stable for persisted executions.
- No silent behavior drift after deserialization (retry logic and context application stay intact).
acceptance_criteria: 1) toArray includes retry policy and cached context payloads. 2) fromArray restores retry policy and cached context exactly. 3) Regression test proves round-trip equality for request fields that influence runtime behavior. 4) Existing InferenceRequest serialization tests remain green.
close_reason: Fixed InferenceRequest serialization contract: toArray now includes cached_context and retry_policy; fromArray restores both (supports snake/camel keys). Added InferenceRetryPolicy toArray/fromArray and regression test proving round-trip preserves retry semantics and cached context.

title: Extract shared request-shaping helpers from provider body formatters
description:
Context:
The refactor cleaned up request typing and message mapping, but provider body formatters still duplicate substantial request-shaping logic. Similar rules for cached vs non-cached messages, system/developer extraction, effective tools, tool choice handling, and response format shaping are spread across multiple drivers.

This duplication increases drift risk: providers evolve independently even when they are implementing the same cross-provider request rules. Now that the typed request layer is stable, this is a good point to reduce duplication without muddying the public API refactor.

Expected outcomes:
- Shared request-shaping rules are extracted into a small number of reusable helpers or request-view objects.
- Provider body formatters keep only provider-specific payload mapping logic.
- Cross-provider behavior becomes easier to test and change consistently.

Scope:
- packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Drivers/*BodyFormat.php
- any new shared helper under packages/polyglot/src/Inference or an appropriate sub-namespace

Examples of duplicated behavior to evaluate:
- splitting system/developer messages from conversation messages
- combining cached and non-cached context
- resolving effective tools and tool choice
- response-format preprocessing before final provider payload mapping

What good looks like:
- Body formatters read as thin provider adapters, not partial request orchestration layers.
- Shared behavior changes can be made in one place and verified once.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Identify the repeated request-shaping rules currently duplicated across multiple Polyglot body formatters and extract them into shared helpers or a dedicated request-view abstraction.
2. Keep provider-specific payload details inside the individual body formatters; the extracted layer should own only shared shaping/orchestration logic.
3. Refactored body formatters become smaller and easier to compare, with no behavioral regressions in existing drivers.
4. Add focused tests for the extracted shared logic where the behavior is cross-provider and high-risk.
5. Existing provider body-format and integration tests continue to pass after the extraction.
notes: Started extraction pass: reviewing duplicated request-shaping logic across provider body formatters and identifying the smallest shared abstraction that keeps provider-specific payload mapping local.
close_reason: Completed: extracted shared request-shaping helpers for message partitioning, response-format type resolution, schema key stripping, and empty payload filtering; refactored main body formatters to use them with focused regression coverage.

title: [instructor-docs] Add docs snippet/API verification to prevent 2.x drift
description:
## Problem and context
Instructor docs had multiple stale API references (e.g., removed static calls, renamed methods, invalid signatures). This kind of drift is likely to recur without an explicit verification step in docs workflow.

## Expected outcomes
- Breaking API drift in docs is detected early.
- Maintainers get fast feedback when examples reference non-existing methods/classes.
- 2.x docs remain aligned with current package API.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Add a lightweight docs QA step for packages/instructor/docs that checks for known API anti-patterns and PHP snippet syntax errors where feasible.
2. Gate CI (or pre-release checks) with this docs QA step.
3. Document how to run the check locally for contributors.
4. Validate that current instructor docs pass the new check.
close_reason: Implemented lightweight Instructor docs QA and wired it into CI/release flows. Added packages/instructor/tools/docs-qa.php with two checks: (1) known stale API anti-pattern scan, (2) fenced PHP snippet syntax linting (best-effort with skip heuristics for non-standalone fragments). Added composer scripts: docs:qa, test alias, and tests now include docs QA. Added contributor docs for local run instructions (README + docs/misc/contributing.md). Gated CI by adding php workflow step: composer --working-dir=packages/instructor docs:qa. Validated current docs pass: docs-qa: passed (115 checked, 41 skipped). Also fixed snippet syntax defects found by the new check in several instructor docs files.

title: Sandbox drivers: remove dead binary-not-found checks and validate binaries upfront
description:
Problem: execute() checks for empty binary path are effectively unreachable because constructors always fallback to literal binary names. Failures happen later as generic process startup errors.
Context: current checks do not provide meaningful early diagnostics.

Expected outcomes:
- Binary discovery/validation strategy is explicit and deterministic.
- Error messages are actionable (which binary, which lookup paths).
- No dead branches remain in execute() for empty-path checks.
acceptance_criteria:
- Refactor constructors/factories to either validate executable availability upfront or intentionally defer with clear error wrapping.
- Remove unreachable `=== ''` guards from execute() paths.
- Add tests for missing binary behavior asserting clear diagnostic error.
- Sandbox package tests pass.
close_reason: Resolved via deferred startup strategy: removed dead empty-binary guards in sandbox drivers, added missing-binary diagnostics regression tests, and wrapped proc_open startup errors for consistent 'Failed to start <driver>' messages.

title: Add Gemini CLI coding agent bridge to AgentCtrl
description: Implement a Gemini CLI bridge in agent-ctrl, following the established pattern (ClaudeCode, Codex, OpenCode, Pi). Gemini CLI (google @google/gemini-cli) supports headless mode (-p), JSON output (-o json), streaming JSONL (-o stream-json), model selection (-m), approval modes (default/auto_edit/yolo/plan), session resume (-r), sandbox (-s), extensions (-e), include-directories, and MCP servers. The stream-json format emits init, message, tool_use, tool_result, error, result events — very similar to Claude Code's format.

title: Gemini bridge domain layer (enums, value objects, stream events)
description:
## Context
Create the domain types for the Gemini CLI bridge, following the pattern established by Pi, ClaudeCode, and Codex bridges.

## Scope

### Enums (src/Gemini/Domain/Enum/)
- `OutputFormat`: json, stream-json, text
- `ApprovalMode`: default, auto_edit, yolo, plan

### Value Objects (src/Gemini/Domain/ValueObject/)
- `GeminiSessionId`: extends OpaqueExternalId, wraps session UUID

### Stream Events (src/Gemini/Domain/Dto/StreamEvent/)
Base class `StreamEvent` with `fromArray()` factory dispatching on `type` field.

Concrete events from Gemini stream-json format:
- `InitEvent`: session_id, model, timestamp
- `MessageEvent`: role (user|assistant), content, delta flag — text streaming chunks have delta=true
- `ToolUseEvent`: tool_name, tool_id, parameters
- `ToolResultEvent`: tool_id, status (success|error), output, error
- `ErrorEvent`: severity (warning|error), message
- `ResultEvent`: status (success|error), stats (total_tokens, input_tokens, output_tokens, cached, duration_ms, tool_calls, per-model breakdown), error
- `UnknownEvent`: fallback for unrecognized types

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All enums are string-backed with correct Gemini CLI values
- [ ] GeminiSessionId extends OpaqueExternalId
- [ ] StreamEvent::fromArray() dispatches all 7 event types correctly
- [ ] MessageEvent exposes `isAssistant()`, `isDelta()`, `textDelta()` helpers
- [ ] ResultEvent exposes `inputTokens()`, `outputTokens()`, `cachedTokens()`, `totalTokens()`, `durationMs()`, `toolCallCount()`
- [ ] ToolResultEvent exposes `isSuccess()`, `isError()`, `resultAsString()`
- [ ] UnknownEvent handles any unrecognized type gracefully
close_reason: Closed

title: Gemini bridge request DTO and command builder
description:
## Context
Create the request DTO and command builder that translates fluent builder config into a `gemini` CLI argv array.

## Scope

### Request DTO (src/Gemini/Application/Dto/GeminiRequest.php)
Readonly DTO with fields:
- prompt (string)
- outputFormat (OutputFormat) — always stream-json for bridge use
- model (?string) — pro, flash, flash-lite, auto, or full model name
- approvalMode (?ApprovalMode) — default, auto_edit, yolo, plan
- sandbox (?bool)
- includeDirectories (?array)
- extensions (?array)
- allowedTools (?array)
- allowedMcpServerNames (?array)
- policy (?array)
- resumeSession (?string) — 'latest' or session UUID
- screenReader (?bool)
- debug (?bool)

### Command Builder (src/Gemini/Application/Builder/GeminiCommandBuilder.php)
Builds `CommandSpec` from `GeminiRequest`:
- Always adds `--output-format stream-json`
- Always adds `--prompt` with the prompt text
- Maps each optional field to CLI flags when non-null
- `--include-directories` accepts array, passed as repeated flags
- `--extensions` accepts array, passed as repeated flags
- `--allowed-mcp-server-names` accepts array
- `--policy` accepts array
- Validates: empty prompt, conflicting flags, model string characters

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GeminiRequest is readonly with all fields
- [ ] CommandBuilder always emits --output-format stream-json and --prompt
- [ ] Model aliases (pro, flash, flash-lite, auto) pass through correctly
- [ ] ApprovalMode maps to --approval-mode flag
- [ ] --sandbox flag added as boolean (no value) when sandbox=true
- [ ] Array flags (include-directories, extensions, policy) emit repeated flags
- [ ] Validation rejects empty prompt
- [ ] Validation rejects invalid model characters
close_reason: Closed

title: Gemini bridge response parser
description:
## Context
Create the response parser that processes Gemini stream-json JSONL output into a structured response DTO.

## Scope

### Response DTO (src/Gemini/Application/Dto/GeminiResponse.php)
Wraps:
- ExecResult (exit code, stdout, stderr)
- DecodedObjectCollection (parsed JSONL events)
- Extracted data: sessionId, messageText, inputTokens, outputTokens, cachedTokens, toolCalls, durationMs, parseFailures

### Response Parser (src/Gemini/Application/Parser/ResponseParser.php)
Parses JSONL stream output:
- Extracts session ID from `init` event (session_id field)
- Accumulates text from `message` events where role=assistant and delta=true
- Extracts tool calls from `tool_use` + `tool_result` event pairs (match by tool_id)
- Extracts token usage from `result` event stats (input_tokens, output_tokens, cached tokens)
- Extracts duration from `result` event stats (duration_ms)
- Tracks parse failures (malformed JSON lines) with count and samples
- Falls back to raw stdout text if no JSONL events found

## Key differences from Pi parser
- Session ID comes from `init` event (not `session` event)
- Text comes from `message` events with delta=true (not `message_update` with text_delta)
- Usage comes from `result` event (not `message_end`)
- Tool calls have explicit `tool_use` / `tool_result` pair matching by tool_id
- No cost data (Gemini CLI doesn't expose cost)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extracts session ID from init event
- [ ] Accumulates text from assistant message deltas
- [ ] Pairs tool_use and tool_result events by tool_id
- [ ] Extracts token usage (input, output, cached) from result event
- [ ] Handles malformed JSON lines gracefully with failure counts
- [ ] Falls back to raw text when no JSONL events present
- [ ] Handles empty/missing fields without errors
close_reason: Closed

title: Gemini bridge and builder classes
description:
## Context
Create the bridge (execution) and builder (fluent config) classes, following the established pattern from ClaudeCode/Pi bridges.

## Scope

### Bridge (src/Bridge/GeminiBridge.php)
Implements `AgentBridge`. Full execution pipeline:
1. Switch to working directory
2. CliBinaryGuard preflight check for `gemini` binary
3. Build GeminiRequest from internal state
4. Build CommandSpec via GeminiCommandBuilder
5. Execute via SandboxCommandExecutor
6. Stream JSONL via JsonLinesBuffer
7. Parse response via ResponseParser
8. Construct AgentResponse

Streaming event mapping:
- `message` (role=assistant, delta=true) → `onText()` callback
- `tool_result` → `onToolUse()` callback with tool name, input, output, error status
- `error` → `onError()` callback
- Final `result` event → completion

### Builder (src/Builder/GeminiBridgeBuilder.php)
Extends `AbstractBridgeBuilder`. Gemini-specific methods:
- `withApprovalMode(ApprovalMode $mode): static`
- `yolo(): static` — shorthand for ApprovalMode::Yolo
- `planMode(): static` — shorthand for ApprovalMode::Plan

title: Add hook registration methods to AgentBuilder

Step hooks:

Update build() to:

title: Serialize InferenceRequest::toArray output

title: [http-client][2.0] Validate ext-http streaming contract and buffering cost
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that the ext-http driver does not provide real streaming semantics and instead buffers the full response body before yielding chunks, then judge whether correcting that behavior is worth the effort for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current codebase, tests, and any actual package usage. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. Prefer deletion, limitation, or a simpler contract over a clever but heavy implementation.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: `packages/http-client/src/Drivers/ExtHttp/ExtHttpResponseAdapter.php` calls `toString()` on the entire body and only then slices it into chunks, so streamed ext-http responses are effectively fully buffered and only delivered after completion.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the claim with code references and a deterministic repro or measurement.
- Decide whether ext-http streaming should be fixed, constrained, documented as non-streaming, or removed from the 2.0.0 streaming story.
- If worth doing now, choose the simplest explicit contract that avoids surprise.

## Acceptance criteria
- A deterministic test or focused repro demonstrates whether ext-http streamed responses are progressive or fully buffered.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: the resulting behavior is explicit, simple, and aligned with the public streaming contract.
- If go: tests cover the chosen contract and guard against accidental full-body buffering on the streaming path.
- If go: docs/examples/cheatsheet clearly describe ext-http capabilities and limitations.
- If no-go: docs/cheatsheet explicitly call out the limitation or ext-http is removed from streaming guidance.

## Related chores
- Add or fix driver-level coverage for ext-http streamed responses.
- Review docs and examples that imply driver parity for streaming.
- Update CHEATSHEET and package docs to match the chosen contract.
- Remove stale wording that overstates ext-http streaming support if needed.
notes: Validated by code inspection. ExtHttpResponseAdapter::stream() calls getBody()->toString() and only then slices the full string into chunks, so the ext-http streaming path is buffered rather than progressive. The local environment does not have the pecl_http extension installed, so I did not close this on runtime repro alone. No code change applied; this remains open pending either explicit approval for a contract/doc narrowing or an environment where runtime behavior can be exercised directly.

title: Dynamic: validate title handling in fromJsonSchema for 2.0.0
description:
Problem:
Review suggests StructureFactory::fromJsonSchema() may ignore standard JSON Schema title and prefer x-title or a hard-coded fallback, which would unexpectedly rename reconstructed structures.

North star for 2.0.0:
We are preparing 2.0.0 with simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach as the north star. Do not assume the behavior should change immediately. First validate the claim against the current codebase, JsonSchema parser behavior, and actual reconstructed Structure metadata, then judge whether the work is valuable enough for 2.0.0.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm or reject the title-handling claim with focused reproductions covering title, x-title, and no-title inputs.
- Identify whether the current precedence is intentional compatibility behavior or accidental metadata loss.
- Make a release judgement: fix now, document as intentional, or defer based on value vs complexity for 2.0.0.
- If the work is worth doing, implement the smallest clear change that makes naming rules obvious and unsurprising.

Related chores:
- Add or adjust regression tests for title/x-title precedence and fallback naming.
- Update examples/fixtures if fromJsonSchema naming behavior changes.
- Update README/CHEATSHEET/docs if public metadata precedence changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- The task validates the claim against current code and runtime behavior and records the result.
- The task explicitly states whether this work is worth the effort for 2.0.0, with reasoning tied to simplicity, clarity, and no-surprise behavior.
- If implemented, fromJsonSchema() applies documented and unsurprising naming precedence for title/x-title/fallback cases.
- Relevant tests/fixtures are added or updated.
- Any necessary docs/examples are updated, or the task records why no docs/examples change is needed.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro: StructureFactory::fromJsonSchema() ignored standard title and only used x-title or the hard-coded fallback schema. For 2.0.0 the simplest no-surprise rule is standard title first, then legacy x-title, then schema. Implemented that precedence in packages/dynamic/src/StructureFactory.php and added regression coverage in packages/dynamic/tests/Regression/JsonSchemaMetadataRoundtripRegressionTest.php. No docs change needed because the resulting naming rule is the standard JSON Schema expectation with a legacy fallback.

title: Map continuation flow + event payloads (Core)
description: Inventory current continuation/stop flow in Core: where evaluations are created, aggregated, stored, and emitted. Capture current AgentEventEmitter payloads and required monitoring fields.
