title: Epic: Simplify pipeline package to one minimal implementation
description:
Goal
Replace the current dual pipeline stack (legacy chains + operator-heavy builder) with one smaller implementation that preserves capabilities actually used by repo clients.

Context
- packages/pipeline/src currently contains both Legacy/Chain classes and the newer operator-stack pipeline.
- Real production builder usage is narrow: sequential through()/throughAll(), tap(), onFailure(), finally(), executeWith(), ErrorStrategy, PendingExecution reuse, ProcessingState, and TransformState compatibility.
- No production callsites use the Legacy/Chain classes.
- No production callsites outside packages/pipeline use CanProcessState, throughRaw(), beforeEach()/afterEach()/aroundEach(), middleware operators, or observation operators.

Outcome
- One pipeline implementation remains.
- Dead legacy code and dead current-surface code are deleted, not deprecated.
- Package tests/docs/CHEATSHEET describe only the surviving API.

Acceptance
- packages/pipeline contains a single pipeline implementation oriented around the minimal supported contract.
- All production callsites in monorepo use the new implementation successfully.
- Legacy/Chain code, unused operator code, and their obsolete tests are removed.
- Relevant downstream tests pass for packages that use pipeline behavior directly or indirectly.
close_reason: Pipeline package now has a single minimal implementation with dead legacy/current residue removed and docs/tests aligned.

title: [pipeline] Specify the minimal supported pipeline contract
description:
Expected outcomes
- Write down the exact public capability set the new package will preserve based on real client usage, not existing internal abstractions.
- Decide which types survive: Pipeline, PipelineBuilder, PendingExecution, ProcessingState, TransformState, ErrorStrategy, and any state contracts required by actual clients.
- Decide which surfaces are deleted entirely: Legacy/Chain, operator stack internals, unused builder methods, unused contracts, and unused tests/docs.

Acceptance criteria
- There is a concrete written contract for the replacement API and semantics.
- The contract explicitly covers step normalization rules for raw values, Result values, and CanCarryState outputs.
- The contract explicitly covers failure behavior for FailFast vs state failure capture, onFailure handlers, finalizers, and PendingExecution caching/reuse.
- The contract lists every production callsite that must still work after migration.

Cleanup / chore
- Update the bd notes with the selected keep/delete list so later phases have a stable target.
- Call out any package docs/examples that will need updates because they mention removed builder features.
close_reason: Defined and implemented the minimal pipeline contract around value steps, failure callbacks, finalizers, ProcessingState, PendingExecution, TransformState, and ErrorStrategy. Removed the keep/delete ambiguity for the old operator and legacy surfaces.

title: [pipeline] Reimplement the package around one closure-based pipeline core
description:
Expected outcomes
- Replace the operator-stack execution model with one simpler core built from plain sequential steps and small lists of failure/final callbacks.
- Preserve supported semantics for sequential transforms, tap, conditional step execution if kept, onFailure, finally, ErrorStrategy, ProcessingState normalization, and PendingExecution reuse.
- Keep TransformState and state interoperability working for the surviving CanCarryState use cases.

Acceptance criteria
- packages/pipeline/src has one execution path and no duplicate legacy/current implementations.
- The new implementation passes focused unit coverage for success flow, Result flattening, state flattening + tag merge, failure capture, fail-fast propagation, finalizer behavior, and PendingExecution caching/each().
- Real production callsites can be expressed against the new API without compatibility shims that preserve deleted abstractions internally.

Cleanup / chore
- Remove dead internal plumbing as part of the implementation, not as follow-up: OperatorStack, unused Operators, and unused hook/middleware interfaces if they are no longer part of the supported contract.
- Replace the old broad pipeline tests with a smaller suite centered on supported semantics.
close_reason: Replaced the operator-stack/legacy implementation with one closure-based pipeline core and rewrote package tests around the smaller supported semantics.

title: [pipeline] Migrate repo callsites and rewrite package tests to the minimal API
description:
Expected outcomes
- Update all monorepo callsites to the surviving pipeline API.
- Remove tests that exist only to cover deleted builder/operator features and add coverage for the reduced public contract.
- Verify TransformState / ProcessingState interoperability at the remaining real usage boundaries.

Acceptance criteria
- Production callsites in packages/instructor, packages/utils, packages/auxiliary, and packages/experimental run against the new implementation without fallback to deleted APIs.
- packages/pipeline tests only describe supported public behavior.
- Downstream tests that exercise the migrated callsites pass.

Cleanup / chore
- Sweep package docs, OVERVIEW/INTERNALS/README/CHEATSHEET for removed APIs and update them to the new single-version story.
- Fix examples or docs snippets if they rely on removed pipeline surface.
- Remove benchmark/integration tests that only compare old vs new pipeline implementations.
close_reason: Verified the real monorepo callsites still work against the new implementation, updated package docs/CHEATSHEET/INTERNALS, and replaced obsolete package tests.

title: [pipeline] Delete legacy pipeline residue and verify downstream stability
description:
Expected outcomes
- Delete all remaining legacy pipeline classes, obsolete tests, and stale documentation references after migration.
- Verify the simplified package and affected downstream packages remain green.

Acceptance criteria
- packages/pipeline/src/Legacy is deleted.
- No tests, docs, examples, or production code outside historical research/release-note material reference removed pipeline classes or removed builder methods.
- Focused package and downstream verification suites pass for pipeline and its real clients.

Cleanup / chore
- Run repo-wide grep for removed pipeline symbols and clean up leftovers in docs/CHEATSHEET/examples.
- Remove stale benchmark files that compare deleted implementations.
- Close or update the bd tasks with a short note on what was removed and what final API remains.
close_reason: Deleted legacy chain code, operator plumbing, pipeline-only tags/exceptions, and stale tests; verified the package and downstream response-generator usage.

title: [polyglot][2.0] Validate partial-stream finalization corruption and release value
description:
## Goal
Validate the claim that partially consuming an `InferenceStream` and then calling `final()` corrupts the assembled final response, and explicitly judge whether fixing that behavior is worth the effort for 2.0.0.

## Mandatory decision gate
Before changing code, validate the claim against the current codebase, tests, and a deterministic repro. Then explicitly judge whether this work is valuable enough to include in the 2.0.0 release.

North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no-surprise / no-magic approach. If the current stream contract is ambiguous, choose the simplest explicit behavior rather than adding clever state machinery.

## Problem to validate
Current review claim: `packages/polyglot/src/Inference/Streaming/InferenceStream.php` can duplicate or corrupt final content if the caller stops iterating `deltas()` early and later calls `final()`.

## Expected outcomes
- Confirm or reject the claim with concrete code references and a deterministic failing scenario.
- Decide whether `InferenceStream` should support early-stop + `final()` safely for 2.0.0, or whether the API should explicitly reject that usage.
- If worth doing now, implement the simplest clear contract that avoids surprise.

## Acceptance criteria
- A deterministic regression test covers the exact early-break then `final()` path and proves whether content/tool state/usage are preserved or corrupted.
- The task notes or close reason explicitly state a go/no-go judgement for 2.0.0 and why.
- If go: stream finalization behavior is explicit and unsurprising after partial consumption.
- If go: no duplicate content, duplicated tool args, or duplicated usage can occur from calling `final()` after partial iteration.
- If go: docs/examples/CHEATSHEET reflect the chosen stream-finalization contract.
- If no-go: docs/CHEATSHEET clearly describe the limitation or disallowed usage.

## Related chores
- Add or update focused regression tests around early stream interruption and `final()`.
- Review stream lifecycle docs/examples for implied guarantees.
- Update docs/CHEATSHEET and remove stale wording if the contract changes or becomes stricter.
notes: Confirmed bug with deterministic runtime repro: breaking after the first delta and then calling final() replayed already-consumed chunks, producing duplicated final content. Applying a local iterator-position fix plus focused regression coverage.

title: [polyglot-docs] Replace non-existent toJson() call in MdJson docs
description:
## Problem and context
`packages/polyglot/docs/modes/md-json.md` uses `)->toJson();`, but `Inference`/`PendingInference` do not expose `toJson()`.

Affected location:
- `packages/polyglot/docs/modes/md-json.md:68`

Real API methods are `asJson()` and `asJsonData()`. This is an API correctness bug in the docs.

## Expected outcomes

- Example remains minimal and clear for users migrating to 2.0.0 docs.

## Acceptance criteria

- Example uses either `asJson()` or `asJsonData()` consistently.

close_reason: Replaced non-existent toJson() call in MdJson docs with supported API usage (asJson()).

title: [polyglot-docs] Correct immutable withCachedContext() usage in examples
description:
## Problem and context
Context caching examples in docs ignore immutability and do not reassign the return value of `withCachedContext(...)`.

Affected locations:
- `packages/polyglot/docs/advanced/context-caching.md:21`
- `packages/polyglot/docs/advanced/context-caching.md:69`

`Inference` fluent methods are immutable and return a cloned instance. Current examples imply in-place mutation and can lead users to ineffective cache setup.

## Expected outcomes
- Context caching examples follow immutable fluent usage patterns.
- Examples clearly show reassignment/chaining so cached context is actually applied.

## Acceptance criteria
- Both affected examples assign the returned `Inference` instance (or chain directly into subsequent calls).
- Narrative text reflects immutable behavior (no implication of in-place mutation).
- Examples remain short and consistent with existing docs style.
close_reason: Corrected context-caching examples to immutable fluent usage by reassigning the return value of withCachedContext(...) in both documented scenarios.

title: Migrate polyglot tests from withAccumulatedContent to InferenceStreamState
description:
Rewrite 5 polyglot test files to build accumulated partials via InferenceStreamState::applyDelta()+partialResponse() instead of PartialInferenceResponse::withAccumulatedContent().

Files to migrate:
- InferenceResponseAssemblyTest.php (13 call sites)
- InferenceHydrationInProgressRegressionTest.php (2 call sites)
- InferenceExecutionUsageTest.php (1 call site)
- NoIdToolCallAccumulationRegressionTest.php (1 call site)
- PartialInferenceResponseImmutabilityTest.php (2 call sites — may be deleted entirely if it only tests withAccumulatedContent)

Expected outcome: All polyglot tests exercise the canonical InferenceStreamState accumulation path. Tests verify the same behaviors (content accumulation, tool tracking, usage merging, finish reason propagation, multi-tool sequences).

Acceptance criteria:
- All 5 test files migrated or removed
- All polyglot tests pass (400+ tests, 0 failures)
- No remaining calls to withAccumulatedContent in packages/polyglot/tests/
- Test coverage of tool accumulation scenarios preserved (single tool, multi-tool, no-id, id-based, name-based)

title: Logging Symfony integration: trait-based autoconfiguration never wires pipeline
description:
Problem and context:
Symfony integration tries to auto-wire `wiretap()` by registering autoconfiguration for `HandlesEvents` trait. Symfony autoconfiguration matches interfaces/classes (`instanceof`) and does not match traits, so the method call is never applied.

Code:
- `packages/logging/src/Integrations/Symfony/DependencyInjection/InstructorLoggingExtension.php:61`
- `packages/logging/src/Integrations/Symfony/DependencyInjection/InstructorLoggingExtension.php:62`

Repro:
- Container with `registerForAutoconfiguration(HandlesEvents::class)->addMethodCall('wiretap', ...)`
- Service class `use HandlesEvents`
- After compile, service definition has no injected `wiretap` method call.

Expected outcomes:
Instructor services in Symfony get pipeline wiring deterministically when logging is enabled.

Acceptance criteria:
- Replace trait-based autoconfiguration approach with a Symfony-compatible strategy (e.g. explicit tag/interface/registry-based compiler pass or framework hook).
- Add integration/regression test proving a service that handles events receives exactly one `wiretap` pipeline injection.
- Ensure behavior is preserved for `default`, `production`, and `custom` presets.
- Document the supported wiring mechanism and extension points.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced ineffective trait autoconfiguration with Symfony compiler-pass wiring for HandlesEvents trait services, added integration regression tests across presets, and documented wiring mechanism.

title: Cohere V2 real API validation across OutputModes
description:
Validate Cohere V2 StructuredOutput behavior (tools partially supported). Suggested runs:
- php examples/B04_LLMApiSupport/Cohere2/run.php
- php examples/A05_Extras/ComplexExtractionCohere/run.php
Checks: tool args remain in tool calls (not content), JSON/MdJson/JsonSchema parse results, and fallback behavior is acceptable if tools are unsupported in model.
close_reason: Ran Cohere examples (B04_LLMApiSupport/Cohere, ComplexExtractionCohere) successfully

title: [pipeline] Honor error strategy for TypeError/Error (catch Throwable)
description:
Problem
Pipeline error strategy `ContinueWithFailure` does not handle PHP Errors (e.g. TypeError). Pipeline::tryProcess catches Exception only, so type mismatches bypass strategy and crash instead of being converted to failed state.

Context
- packages/pipeline/src/Pipeline.php:136 catches Exception only
- Default strategy is ErrorStrategy::ContinueWithFailure, so callers reasonably expect runtime processor errors to produce failure states.
- Repro:
  Pipeline::builder()->through(fn(int $x) => $x*2)->create()->executeWith(ProcessingState::with('a'))
  throws TypeError instead of yielding failed state.

Expected outcomes
- ContinueWithFailure handles Throwable (including Error/TypeError), not only Exception.
- FailFast still rethrows immediately.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests covering:
  1) TypeError in processor under ContinueWithFailure -> failed state with captured exception
  2) same scenario under FailFast -> exception is rethrown
- Ensure existing exception-based behavior remains unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed: Pipeline::tryProcess now catches Throwable so ContinueWithFailure converts TypeError/Error into failed state; added regression tests for ContinueWithFailure and FailFast behavior.

title: Add HTTP client 2.0 upgrade guide
description:
## Problem and context
`packages/http-client/docs` has no dedicated 2.0 migration/upgrade page. Deprecated APIs and replacements are scattered (or missing) across pages, making upgrades from 1.x risky and slow.

## Expected outcomes
Publish a concise upgrade page focused on breaking/deprecated surface and direct replacements, in Laravel-style format.

## Scope notes
Keep it short, task-oriented, and example-driven.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a new docs page (for example `14-upgrade-guide.md`) and include it in `_meta.yaml` extras section.
- Include a migration table for known deprecations in v2 scope (`HttpClient::withSSEStream()`, `HttpClientBuilder::using()`, `HttpClientBuilder::withDebugPreset()`, `Middleware\ServerSideEvents\*`).
- Provide minimal before/after snippets for each migration path.
- Ensure all mentioned classes/methods exist in current codebase or are explicitly marked deprecated compatibility APIs.
close_reason: Verified 2.0 upgrade guide exists, includes required migration table and before/after snippets, is linked in docs _meta order, and docs QA passes for packages/http-client/docs.

title: Update ResponseContentCheck criterion

title: [logging][2.0] Validate default pipeline timestamp semantics and release value
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, tests, and package docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 package contract relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
When no formatter is configured, LoggingPipeline creates LogEntry timestamps at write time instead of using the event timestamp, so timestamp semantics differ depending on whether a formatter is present.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm the current timestamp behavior in formatter and no-formatter paths.
- Decide whether this is a real consistency bug, an intentional default-path simplification, or not important enough for 2.0.0.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, choose the simplest no-surprise contract and implement it consistently.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, document the reasoning explicitly and clarify the contract.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a clear written conclusion.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If accepted for 2.0.0, timestamp semantics are consistent and clearly defined across pipeline paths, or the documented contract explicitly preserves the chosen difference.
- Regression tests are added or updated to lock the chosen behavior.
- Related tests are fixed if timestamp semantics change.
- Examples, docs, and cheatsheet references are updated anywhere timestamp behavior is described or implied.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so the selected behavior remains covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage for delayed logging with and without formatters.
- Fix or extend LoggingPipeline and LogEntry tests.
- Update docs/examples so default pipeline semantics are explicit.
- Check whether writer/formatter examples assume event-time timestamps already.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro. When no formatter was configured, LoggingPipeline created LogEntry timestamps at write time, while formatter paths used the event timestamp. Fixed the default no-formatter path to use LogContext eventTime, aligning timestamp semantics across pipeline paths. Added regression coverage in packages/logging/tests/Regression/LoggingPipelineContractRegressionTest.php.

title: [dynamic] Fix falsy value handling (0/false/'0') in serialization and deserialization
description:
Problem
Dynamic structure processing uses `empty()` for field presence/value checks, which incorrectly treats valid values (`0`, `false`, `'0'`) as missing. This causes rejected required inputs and silent data loss for optional fields.

Context
- packages/dynamic/src/Traits/Structure/HandlesDeserialization.php:24-27 rejects required fields when `empty($fieldData)`
- packages/dynamic/src/Traits/Field/HandlesFieldValue.php:30-32 defines `isEmpty()` using `empty()`
- packages/dynamic/src/Traits/Structure/HandlesSerialization.php:20 uses `isEmpty()` to decide whether to emit field
- Repro confirmed:
  1) required int/bool with value `0`/`false` throws "Required field ... is empty"
  2) optional bool `false` deserializes to `null` and is omitted from output
  3) optional bool set to `false` is dropped by `toArray()`

Expected outcomes
- Presence semantics distinguish “missing/null” from valid falsy scalar values.
- Valid falsy values are preserved through deserialization and serialization.

Acceptance criteria
- Add regression tests covering required and optional fields for values: `0`, `false`, `'0'`.
- `fromArray()` must accept these values without throwing required-field errors.
- `toArray()` must retain explicitly set falsy values instead of dropping them.
- Behavior for truly missing/null values remains clearly defined and tested.
close_reason: Fixed falsy scalar handling in dynamic structures: replaced empty() checks with null semantics in deserialization and field emptiness; added regression coverage for required/optional 0/false/'0' roundtrip.

title: agents: LoadSkillTool static withLibrary -> fromLibrary (2.0 clean cut)
description:
Goal: make static constructor intent explicit in LoadSkillTool for 2.0.0.

Changes:
- Rename LoadSkillTool::withLibrary(SkillLibrary) to LoadSkillTool::fromLibrary(SkillLibrary).
- Update all call sites/examples/docs/tests.
- No deprecations/aliases; clean break.

Acceptance:
- No usages of LoadSkillTool::withLibrary remain.
- QA/static checks for touched scope pass.
close_reason: Renamed LoadSkillTool::withLibrary to fromLibrary across src/tests/docs/examples; packages/agents tests pass.

close_reason: Added explicit state hook interfaces and tests; replaced method_exists with instanceof.

title: [instructor] Critical: response()/get() before stream() leads to terminal stream failure
description:
## Problem and context
`PendingStructuredOutput` supports multiple consumption modes (`response()`, `get()`, `stream()`), but currently mode switching is not safe.

When non-stream mode is consumed first, creating/consuming stream afterward can fail with terminal runtime errors instead of returning a consistent result or explicit unsupported-mode error.

### Reproduced failures
1. `response()` -> `stream()->finalResponse()` fails with:
   - `RuntimeException: Expected final InferenceResponse, got null`
2. `stream()->getIterator()` consumption can also leave `finalResponse()` unable to resolve final response when internal tracking (`lastResponse`) was not populated through `streamResponses()` path.

### Why this is high impact
This is in the main public API path (`StructuredOutput`/`PendingStructuredOutput`) that is heavily used by docs and downstream packages. It can trigger confusing runtime failures and duplicate/undefined execution semantics.

### Code context
- `packages/instructor/src/PendingStructuredOutput.php`
- `packages/instructor/src/StructuredOutputStream.php`

## Expected outcomes
- Consumption-mode behavior is deterministic and explicitly documented.
- Calling `stream()` after non-stream consumption either:
  - reuses existing final response safely, or
  - fails fast with a clear, intentional exception that explains mode incompatibility.
- `finalResponse()` can derive final inference from execution state when available (not only `lastResponse` side effects).

## Acceptance criteria
- Add regression test covering `response()` then `stream()->finalResponse()` path (currently fails).
- Add regression test covering `getIterator()` consumption followed by `finalResponse()` behavior for both cache policies (`None`/`Memory`) according to chosen contract.
- Ensure no duplicate provider call is made in mode-switch path unless explicitly documented/intentional.
- Error messages (if incompatibility is intentional) are explicit and actionable, not internal-state artifacts.
close_reason: Fixed terminal stream failure when switching consumption modes by resolving final response from finalized execution state, added regression tests for response()->stream()->finalResponse and getIterator()->finalResponse (None/Memory), and verified no duplicate provider calls.

title: Fix ClaudeCode example missing command error

comments.author: ddebowczyk
comments.text: # Investigation & testing protocol\n\nObligatory: start by investigating the code causing the issue. After an initial understanding of the offending module/class, TEST FIRST: recreate the error using a deterministic mechanism (mock http client or mock inference or deterministic agent driver). Ensure the deterministic mechanism does not hide the problem; if needed, use a lower-level deterministic mechanism (mock http -> mock inference -> mock agent).

title: Preserve nullable/default metadata in Schema<->JsonSchema conversion
description:
Problem and context:
`Schema <-> JsonSchema` bridge currently drops core metadata in conversion paths. `JsonSchemaRenderer` does not emit nullable/default metadata from domain `Schema`, and parser mapping does not propagate default metadata into resulting `Schema` nodes. This can silently lose important structure semantics (nullability/defaults) used by runtime normalization and validation.

Expected outcomes:
- `nullable` and default metadata are preserved when converting `Schema -> JsonSchema`.
- Parsing `JsonSchema -> Schema` reconstructs `nullable`, `hasDefaultValue`, and `defaultValue` consistently.
- Roundtrip conversion keeps these semantics for scalar and object-property schemas.

Solution direction:
- Extend renderer to include nullable/default metadata from schema nodes.
- Extend parser property creation to pass nullable/default metadata into `Schema` node constructors.
- Add focused roundtrip regression coverage (scalar + object property + null default case).

Process (mandatory):
1. Add regression test first that proves metadata loss.
2. Run test to confirm failure on current code.
3. Implement minimal fix in renderer/parser bridge.
4. Re-run regression and affected suites to confirm pass.

Notes:
- Keep bridge behavior explicit and deterministic.
- Avoid broad API redesign in this fix.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A fail-first regression test captures current metadata loss (nullable/default) in conversion path.
2. After fix, `Schema -> JsonSchema` output includes nullable/default metadata where applicable.
3. After fix, `JsonSchema -> Schema` parsing sets `isNullable()`, `hasDefaultValue()`, and `defaultValue()` correctly.
4. Roundtrip tests pass for: nullable scalar, scalar with default, and nullable property with null default.
5. Existing unrelated schema rendering/parsing tests stay green.
close_reason: Covered nullable/default bridge preservation in regression suite and confirmed roundtrip behavior: renderer emits nullable/default metadata, parser maps nullable+hasDefaultValue+defaultValue back to schema nodes, tests pass for null default, scalar defaults, nested property defaults, and collection defaults.

title: ConfigResolver: explicit null config values are treated as missing
description:
Problem and context:
`ConfigResolver::resolveGet()` uses `null` as the sentinel for "not resolved". If a provider legitimately stores `null` for an existing key, resolver treats it as missing, may fall through to lower-priority providers, or returns the default/throws unexpectedly.

Repro:
- Provider data: `feature.flag => null`
- `has('feature.flag')` is `true`
- `get('feature.flag')` throws `ConfigurationException`
- `get('feature.flag', 'fallback')` returns `'fallback'`

Expected outcomes:
Resolver must distinguish between "path exists with null value" and "path not found".

Acceptance criteria:
- Add regression tests for:
  - existing null value is returned as null by `get(path)`;
  - existing null value does not fall through to lower-priority providers;
  - default is not applied when key exists and value is null.
- Update resolution logic to use provider existence checks (or explicit value wrapper/sentinel) rather than conflating null with missing.
- Preserve first-provider-wins precedence semantics.
close_reason: Assessed as high relevance but already resolved by ConfigResolver get/lookup semantics fix introduced for instructor-3bd7. Added dedicated regression tests for explicit-null semantics (strict get returns null, no provider fallthrough, default not applied) and verified full config suite passes.

title: [polyglot-docs] Fix invalid PHP snippets blocking docs QA
description:
## Problem and context
`packages/polyglot/docs` currently fails docs QA because fenced PHP snippets are syntactically invalid in multiple pages.

Affected files:
- `packages/polyglot/docs/essentials/creating-requests.md:19`
- `packages/polyglot/docs/troubleshooting/issues-connection.md:19`
- `packages/polyglot/docs/troubleshooting/issues-provider-specific.md:13`

This blocks reliable docs quality checks for 2.0.0 and can mislead users by showing non-runnable examples.

## Expected outcomes
- All affected snippets are valid PHP syntax.
- Snippets still convey the same guidance after fixes.
- Package-level docs QA for polyglot passes with no syntax errors.

## Acceptance criteria
- `php bin/instructor-docs qa --source-dir=packages/polyglot/docs --profile=none --extensions=md,mdx` exits successfully.
- The three listed files no longer produce snippet lint parse errors.
- No new docs QA failures are introduced in `packages/polyglot/docs`.
close_reason: Fixed invalid PHP snippets in creating-requests, issues-connection, and issues-provider-specific docs by replacing non-parseable fragment snippets with valid PHP examples.

title: [polyglot-docs] Fix PHP version requirement mismatch in setup docs
description:
## Problem and context
Polyglot setup docs still state PHP 8.2+, but package composer requirement is `^8.3`.

Affected locations:
- `packages/polyglot/docs/setup.md:29`
- `packages/polyglot/docs/setup.md:170`

This is a release-readiness mismatch that can cause failed installations for users following docs literally.

## Expected outcomes
- Setup docs align with actual package requirements.
- Requirement appears consistently across the page.

## Acceptance criteria
- `setup.md` states PHP 8.3+ (or exact equivalent) wherever runtime requirement is mentioned.
- No remaining 8.2 requirement claims remain in polyglot docs.
close_reason: Aligned polyglot setup docs with actual package requirement by updating PHP version references to 8.3+.

title: [stream][2.0] Validate TransformationStream sink contract and docs drift
description:
North star for 2.0.0: simplicity of the code, clarity, and a no surprise / no magic approach.

Before proposing or implementing any fix, validate this claim against the current codebase and judge whether the work is valuable enough to be included in the 2.0.0 release. Do not assume the report is correct just because it was filed. Reproduce or disprove it from the implementation, tests, and package docs. Then decide whether the change meaningfully improves the 2.0.0 API and runtime behavior relative to its complexity.

Claim to validate:
When a Transformation with a custom sink is used through TransformationStream, the sink is replaced by ToQueueReducer and getCompleted() returns emitted items rather than the configured sink result. Related docs/examples may currently imply unsupported APIs or unsupported sink behavior.

Expected outcomes:
- Confirm the current implementation and user-facing behavior around custom sinks with TransformationStream.
- Audit docs and examples for unsupported calls or misleading expectations.
- Decide whether this is a real bug, a deliberate design boundary, or mostly documentation debt.
- If worth doing for 2.0.0, choose the simplest no-surprise contract and implement it consistently.
- If not worth doing for 2.0.0, document the boundary clearly and close the loop with a conscious decision.

Acceptance criteria:
- The claim has been validated against the current codebase, with a clear written conclusion.
- The task includes an explicit judgement on whether this work is valuable enough for the 2.0.0 release.
- If accepted for 2.0.0, TransformationStream sink behavior is either implemented consistently or explicitly unsupported, and the docs/examples reflect that with no magic behavior.
- Regression tests are added or updated to lock the chosen contract.
- Related tests are fixed if behavior or documented API changes.
- Examples, README, CHEATSHEET, and INTERNALS references are corrected where they currently imply sink support or unsupported methods.
- Any related config or regression-test wiring is updated if needed so the chosen contract remains covered in CI.

Related chores:
- Add regression coverage for using(Transformation::define(...)->withSink(...)) with getCompleted().
- Fix or extend related unit tests.
- Update package docs and examples to remove unsupported APIs and align with the actual contract.
- Check nearby docs for other TransformationStream examples that accidentally mix eager and streaming semantics.

Again: make a judgement call. Only pull this into 2.0.0 if it clearly earns its keep under the simplicity / clarity / no-magic north star.
close_reason: Validated with runtime repro and code inspection. TransformationStream::makeExecution() always replaces the provided transformation sink with ToQueueReducer, so getCompleted() returns emitted stream items rather than the original sink result. For 2.0.0 this is the right no-magic contract: TransformationStream is emission-oriented, not a sink-preserving wrapper. Added regression coverage in packages/stream/tests/Regression/TransformationStreamContractRegressionTest.php and clarified the contract in packages/stream/CHEATSHEET.md and packages/stream/INTERNALS.md.

title: Logging package quality scripts are not runnable in monorepo and psalm config is missing
description:
Problem and context:
Package-level quality scripts in `packages/logging/composer.json` assume local `vendor/bin/*`; in monorepo this fails from package directory. Additionally, package has psalm script but no package psalm config file, making static-analysis workflow ambiguous and brittle.

Repro:
- `cd packages/logging && composer run tests` -> `Could not open input file: vendor/bin/pest`
- `vendor/bin/psalm --config=packages/logging/psalm.xml` -> missing config path

Expected outcomes:
Package quality commands are executable and deterministic for maintainers/CI.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update scripts/config so package `tests`, `phpstan`, and `psalm` commands run correctly in current repo layout.
- Add package psalm config (or explicit documented monorepo psalm command) to remove ambiguity.
- Ensure failures reflect real code issues, not tooling path/config assumptions.
- Document canonical quality-gate commands in package README/contributor docs.
close_reason: Made logging package quality scripts runnable in monorepo (tests/phpstan/psalm), added missing package psalm.xml, fixed path assumptions in scripts/config, and documented canonical quality commands in README.
